Markets anticipate recovery but underestimate the magnitude of cyclical earnings rebound.
causal_chain: FRED T10Y2Y crosses above 0 after 12+ months below zero
Un-inversion signals Fed easing cycle underway and recession troughing
Short rates falling faster than long rates = economic recovery expectations building
Cyclical sectors (XLI, XLF) see earnings troughs before defensives feel late-cycle pain
Long cyclicals (XLI+XLF) vs short defensives (XLP+XLU) captures the rotation over 12 months
data_sources: yfinance XLI, XLF, XLP, XLU, SPY
source_reference: FRED yield curve; Campbell Harvey dissertation (1986); recession-rotation timing literature

name: FRED ISM Services PMI Expansion Cross -> Long Consumer Discretionary (XLY)
thesis: FRED NMFBAI (ISM Non-Manufacturing/Services PMI) crossing above 55 after being below 50 for 3+ months signals a strong services sector recovery. Since the US is 70%+ services, this is a direct consumer demand signal. Long XLY for 6 months. ISM Services gets less attention than Manufacturing PMI but is far more relevant to consumer-facing equities. The expansion cross leads retail/hospitality earnings beats by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED NMFBAI (ISM Services PMI) crosses above 55 after 3+ months below 50
Services sector (70% of GDP) entering expansion = consumer demand recovering
Restaurant, hotel, travel, entertainment spending accelerates
Consumer discretionary companies (AMZN, HD, MCD, SBUX in XLY) see revenue recovery
XLY re-rates over 3-6 months as services-led consumer recovery flows to earnings
source_reference: ISM Non-Manufacturing Index; services-sector leading indicator research

name: FRED GDP Nowcast vs Consensus Gap -> Sector Surprise Trade
thesis: Atlanta Fed GDPNow (FRED GDPNOW, updated biweekly) frequently diverges from Blue Chip consensus by >1.5pp. When GDPNow exceeds consensus by >1.5pp, the economy is stronger than the market expects. Long SPY for 60 days. GDPNow is mechanical (model-driven) while consensus is behavioral (anchoring bias). The model beats humans 60%+ of the time at nowcasting. The divergence closes at GDP release.
causal_chain: FRED GDPNOW exceeds Blue Chip consensus GDP estimate by >1.5pp
Mechanical model incorporating latest data releases updating faster than humans
Positive surprise indicates economic momentum underpriced by consensus
Corporate earnings likely to beat as economy stronger than expected
SPY rallies 3-5% over 60 days as data confirms the nowcast
source_reference: Atlanta Fed GDPNow methodology; Higgins 2014 Atlanta Fed working paper

name: FRED Private Residential Construction Spending Acceleration -> Long Cement/Concrete (EXP/SUM)
thesis: FRED PRRESCONS (private residential construction spending, monthly) captures actual dollars spent on housing construction. When 3-month annualized growth exceeds +15%, concrete/cement demand is surging because foundations and flatwork are the earliest construction phases. Long EXP (Eagle Materials, 50% cement/concrete) and SUM (Summit Materials, 100% aggregates/cement) for 6 months. Private resi construction spending leads cement shipments by 1 month because the dollars are committed when the pour is scheduled.
causal_chain: FRED PRRESCONS 3-month annualized growth exceeds +15%
Residential construction spending surge = active home construction accelerating
Foundation/flatwork pours happen in month 1-2 of construction = cement demand immediate
EXP (50% cement/wallboard) and SUM (aggregates/cement) see volume growth
Pricing power + volume leverage = margin expansion over 3-6 months
source_reference: Census Bureau construction spending (C30); EXP/SUM investor presentations on residential exposure

name: FRED Building Permits Northeast Surge -> Long USG/JELD (Building Products)
thesis: FRED PERMIT (building permits) has regional breakdowns. When Northeast permits surge >20% YoY while national is flat, it signals regional housing demand shift. Northeast builds require higher R-value insulation, triple-pane windows, and more interior finishing due to climate. Long building product companies with Northeast distribution: JELD (windows/doors), AZEK (decking/trim), BLDR (specialty distribution). Regional permit data is free on FRED but markets focus only on national totals.
causal_chain: FRED PERMITNE (Northeast building permits) rises >20% YoY while PERMIT national is flat
Regional demand surge creates local supply bottleneck in building products
Northeast code requirements = higher-spec products (better windows, insulation, finishes)
JELD, AZEK, BLDR see regional order books fill with premium-spec products
Stocks re-rate as quarterly earnings show regional strength not in national consensus
source_reference: Census Bureau regional permits; Northeast building code requirements; JELD regional revenue mix

name: FRED JOLTS Quits Rate Recovery -> Long Staffing Companies (RHI/HAYS)
thesis: FRED JTSQUR (JOLTS quits rate, monthly) is a leading indicator of labor market confidence. When the quits rate rises >0.3pp from a cyclical trough, it signals workers are confident enough to leave jobs for better ones -- creating demand for staffing/temp agencies to fill vacancies. Long RHI (Robert Half) and HAYS for 6 months. Quits rate recovery leads staffing revenue by 1-2 quarters because every quit creates a vacancy that needs filling.
causal_chain: FRED JTSQUR (quits rate) rises >0.3pp from trailing 6-month low
Workers regaining confidence to quit = labor market strengthening
Every voluntary quit creates a vacancy that employers need to fill quickly
Staffing firms (RHI, HAYS) see temp/perm placement demand surge
Staffing company revenue accelerates 1-2 quarters after quits rate turns up
source_reference: BLS JOLTS; quits rate as labor confidence indicator (Daly-Hobijn 2013); staffing cycle research

name: FRED Average Weekly Hours Manufacturing Recovery -> Long Cyclical Equities
thesis: FRED AWHMAN (average weekly hours, manufacturing, monthly) is a leading indicator because employers increase hours BEFORE hiring new workers. When AWHMAN crosses above 41.0 hours after being below 40.5 for 4+ months, it signals manufacturers are ramping production. Long XLI for 6 months. Hours precede payrolls by 1-3 months and precede INDPRO by 2-4 months. This is one of the 10 LEI components but is rarely traded directly.
causal_chain: FRED AWHMAN crosses above 41.0 after 4+ months below 40.5
Manufacturers increasing overtime hours = demand exceeding current workforce capacity
Hours increase 1-3 months before new hiring (cheaper to pay OT than onboard)
New hiring follows -> payrolls accelerate -> production ramps -> INDPRO rises
XLI/cyclicals re-rate as production and employment data confirm recovery
source_reference: BLS CES; Conference Board LEI (AWHMAN is component #1); labor market leading indicators

name: FRED Continued Claims Declining Streak -> Long Small Caps (IWM)
thesis: FRED CCSA (continued claims, weekly) tracks the stock of unemployed workers receiving benefits. When continued claims decline for 8+ consecutive weeks after a peak, it signals the labor market is absorbing the unemployed -- a powerful recovery signal. Long IWM for 6 months. Small caps are the most employment-sensitive equities because small businesses account for 50%+ of US employment. Declining continued claims = small businesses hiring again = small cap revenue recovery.
causal_chain: FRED CCSA (continued claims) declines for 8+ consecutive weeks from a cyclical peak
Sustained decline = labor market absorbing unemployed workers (not just claims expiration)
Small businesses (50%+ of employment) are rehiring = revenue recovery for small firms
Small-cap earnings most leveraged to employment recovery (high labor cost share)
IWM rallies 15-25% over 6 months as small-cap earnings inflect
source_reference: BLS continued claims; small-cap employment sensitivity; DOL weekly claims report

name: FRED TED Spread Normalization -> Long Regional Banks (KRE)
thesis: FRED TEDRATE (3-month LIBOR minus T-bill rate) measures interbank credit risk. When TED spread contracts below 30bps after being above 50bps for 3+ months, it signals interbank trust is restored and funding stress is easing. Long KRE (regional bank ETF) for 6 months. Regional banks are most sensitive to funding markets because they rely on wholesale funding more than money-center banks. TED normalization leads regional bank NIM recovery by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED TEDRATE contracts below 30bps after 3+ months above 50bps
Interbank lending normalization = funding stress easing, trust restored
Regional banks' wholesale funding costs decline -> NIM expands
Loan origination resumes as funding is available at reasonable cost
KRE rallies 15-25% over 6 months as NIM + loan growth compound
source_reference: Federal Reserve TED spread; interbank credit risk literature; regional bank funding models

name: FRED Excess Reserves Surge -> Long Risk Assets (SPY/QQQ)
thesis: FRED EXCSRESNW (excess reserves of depository institutions, weekly) tracks idle cash sitting at the Fed. When excess reserves surge >20% in a rolling 3-month period, it signals massive liquidity injection that will eventually flow into risk assets. Long SPY for 6 months. Excess reserves are the 'fuel tank' for risk assets -- banks eventually deploy excess reserves into lending and securities purchases. The 2020 excess reserves surge preceded the equity rally by 2-3 months.
causal_chain: FRED WRESBAL (reserve balances, weekly) surges >20% in rolling 3 months
Fed liquidity injection creating excess reserves at depository institutions
Banks deploy excess into securities purchases and lending over 2-4 months
Capital flows from bank balance sheets to risk assets (equities, credit)
SPY/QQQ rally as excess liquidity transmission channel activates
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.4.1 balance sheet; bank reserves -> risk asset transmission; Poszar 2019

name: FRED C&I Loan Growth Inflection -> Long Bank Stocks (XLF)
thesis: FRED BUSLOANS (C&I loans at commercial banks, weekly) directly measures business lending. When C&I loan growth YoY turns positive after 6+ months of contraction, it confirms the credit cycle trough -- businesses are borrowing again. Long XLF for 12 months. C&I loan growth leads bank NII by 2-3 quarters and leads bank stock performance by 1-2 quarters. Distinct from PL064 (TOTBKCR total credit) -- this is specifically C&I loans, a purer business lending signal.
causal_chain: FRED BUSLOANS (C&I loans) YoY growth turns positive after 6+ months negative
Business borrowing resuming = capex and working capital demand recovering
New C&I loans generate net interest income for banks over 3-5 year loan duration
NII growth leads bank earnings acceleration by 1-2 quarters
XLF re-rates as bank earnings inflect on the credit growth recovery
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.8; C&I loan growth as credit cycle indicator; distinct from PL064 total credit

name: FRED Trade-Weighted Dollar Decline -> Long US Industrial Exporters (HON/CAT/DE)
thesis: FRED DTWEXBGS (trade-weighted dollar, broad, daily) declining >5% from a 6-month peak signals US export competitiveness improving. Industrial exporters (HON 55% intl, CAT 50% intl, DE 40% intl) benefit from both FX translation effects AND volume gains as US goods become cheaper overseas. Long HON+CAT+DE for 6 months. The dollar move leads export volume by 1-2 quarters (J-curve effect). Distinct from PL046 (DXY spike -> EM reversal) -- this is dollar WEAKNESS -> US exporter strength.
causal_chain: FRED DTWEXBGS declines >5% from trailing 6-month peak
Weaker dollar = US manufactured goods cheaper for foreign buyers
US industrial exporters (HON, CAT, DE) see foreign order books fill
FX translation tailwind boosts reported earnings by 3-5% at 50% intl revenue
Volume + FX = double tailwind for earnings -> stocks re-rate over 3-6 months
source_reference: FRED trade-weighted dollar; J-curve export lag; industrial company geographic revenue exposure

name: FRED US Trade Deficit Widening -> Long Port/Logistics Equities
thesis: FRED BOPGSTB (goods trade balance, monthly) widening beyond -$80B/month signals surging import volumes -- the US is pulling in massive physical goods. This import surge creates demand for port operators, logistics, and warehousing. Long EXPD (freight forwarder), CHRW (3PL), and ZIM/MATX (container shipping) for 6 months. Widening trade deficit = more physical goods hitting US ports = logistics revenue acceleration.
causal_chain: FRED BOPGSTB (goods trade balance) widens beyond -$80B monthly
Surging imports = massive physical goods volumes hitting US ports
Port congestion rises, container throughput at peak, logistics pricing power increases
EXPD (freight forwarding), CHRW (logistics), MATX (Jones Act containers) see revenue surge
Logistics stocks re-rate on pricing power and volume growth over 3-6 months
source_reference: Census Bureau FT900 trade; port throughput data; trade deficit -> logistics demand chain

name: FRED WTI Crude Backwardation Steepening -> Long E&P Equities (XOP)
thesis: When WTI futures curve shifts into steep backwardation (front-month > 6-month by >$3/bbl), it signals physical market tightness -- inventories drawing and current demand exceeding supply. Long XOP (E&P ETF) for 90 days. Backwardation rewards E&P producers because they sell at elevated spot prices while the market's forward expectations (which drive consensus models) are lower. This creates systematic earnings beats for spot-exposed producers.
causal_chain: WTI front-month minus 6-month spread exceeds $3/bbl (steep backwardation)
Backwardation signals physical tightness: inventories drawing, supply < demand now
E&P producers sell at elevated spot prices -- higher than sell-side model assumptions
Models based on strip pricing underestimate E&P earnings in backwardated markets
XOP beats consensus for 1-2 quarters during sustained backwardation
source_reference: EIA crude oil futures; backwardation as physical market signal; S&P GSCI backwardation research

name: FRED Henry Hub Natural Gas Below Cash Cost -> Long NG Producers (EQT/AR/RRC)
thesis: FRED MHHNGSP (Henry Hub spot price, monthly). When Henry Hub falls below $2.50/MMBtu (cash cost breakeven for marginal Appalachian producers), supply curtailments begin within 2-3 months. EQT (largest US gas producer) and AR (Antero) voluntarily shut-in production to defend price. Long EQT/AR/RRC for 12 months from the sub-$2.50 crossing. Sub-cash-cost pricing is self-correcting: supply curtailments + steady demand = price recovery. The 2020 and 2023-24 sub-$2.50 episodes both preceded 50%+ price recoveries.
causal_chain: FRED MHHNGSP (Henry Hub monthly) drops below $2.50/MMBtu
Sub-cash-cost pricing triggers producer shut-ins and completion deferrals within 2-3 months
EQT, AR announce voluntary curtailments (they've done this in 2020, 2024)
Supply reduction + steady demand (power gen baseload) = price recovery
NG=F recovers to $3.50-4.50 over 6-12 months; EQT/AR re-rate on higher realizations
data_sources: yfinance EQT, AR, RRC, NG=F, SPY
source_reference: EIA Henry Hub; EQT/AR curtailment press releases; natural gas cash cost analysis

name: FRED US Crude Oil Production MoM Decline -> Long Crude Futures
thesis: FRED MCRFPUS1 (US crude production monthly). When US production declines MoM for 3+ consecutive months, it signals depletion/curtailment overwhelming new completions. Long CL=F for 6 months. US is the world's swing producer at 13M+ bpd; a sustained decline tightens global supply by 200-500Kbpd which is material to global balances. Distinct from PL030 (rig count -> production lag) -- this is actual PRODUCTION declining, not a leading indicator.
causal_chain: FRED MCRFPUS1 (US crude production) declines MoM for 3 consecutive months
Actual production dropping = depletion + curtailments exceeding new completions
US produces 13M+ bpd; 200-500Kbpd decline is 0.2-0.5% of global supply
Global oil balance tightens -> inventories draw -> spot price rises
CL=F rallies 10-20% over 6 months as supply tightness is confirmed
source_reference: EIA monthly crude production; distinct from PL030 (rig count leading indicator)

name: FRED Air Revenue Passenger Miles Recovery -> Long Airlines (JETS ETF)
thesis: FRED AIRRPMTSI (air revenue passenger miles, monthly, seasonally adjusted) is the most direct measure of passenger airline demand. When RPM YoY crosses above +5% after a period of <0% for 6+ months, it confirms demand recovery. Long JETS (airline ETF) for 6 months. RPMs lead airline revenue by 1 quarter because tickets are sold 1-3 months before travel. Airlines have high operating leverage -- a 5% RPM increase can drive 15-20% EBIT growth if capacity is disciplined.
causal_chain: FRED AIRRPMTSI (revenue passenger miles) YoY crosses above +5% after 6+ months negative
Air travel demand confirmed recovering -- passengers physically flying more
Airlines have fixed costs (aircraft leases, labor) -- revenue growth flows to EBIT with 3-4x leverage
Ticket pricing holds if capacity discipline maintained (DAL/UAL load factors above 85%)
JETS ETF rallies 15-25% over 6 months on operating leverage from demand recovery
source_reference: BTS T-100 domestic; DOT air traffic statistics; airline operating leverage models

name: FRED Railroad Intermodal Traffic YoY Turn -> Long Intermodal/Rail (UNP/CSX)
thesis: FRED RAILFRTINTERam (railroad intermodal traffic) specifically tracks containerized freight moving by rail -- a proxy for consumer goods imports arriving at ports and moving inland. When intermodal YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative, it signals consumer demand recovery for physical goods. Long UNP+CSX for 6 months. Intermodal is the highest-margin freight for Class I rails. Distinct from PL052 (total carloads -> industrial production); intermodal tracks CONSUMER goods, not industrial bulk.
causal_chain: FRED RAILFRTINTERAM intermodal traffic YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative
Intermodal = containerized consumer goods (electronics, apparel, furniture) moving inland
Consumer goods demand recovery confirmed by physical rail traffic
UNP/CSX see intermodal volume recovery -- highest-margin freight category
Rail stocks re-rate as intermodal revenue accelerates and pricing power returns
source_reference: AAR intermodal statistics; distinct from PL052 (total carloads); intermodal-consumer demand link

name: FRED Consumer Sentiment (UMich) Extreme Low Reversal -> Long Retail (XRT)
thesis: FRED UMCSENT (U of Michigan Consumer Sentiment, monthly) at extreme lows (<55, bottom decile since 1978) is a contrarian BUY signal for retail stocks. When sentiment is this depressed, consumer expectations are already at rock bottom -- any stabilization drives spending surprise. Long XRT (retail ETF) for 6 months when UMCSENT drops below 55 and then rises for 2 consecutive months (trough confirmation). The signal fired in mid-2022 (UMCSENT=50) and preceded a 35% XRT rally.
causal_chain: FRED UMCSENT drops below 55 then rises for 2 consecutive months (trough confirmed)
Extreme pessimism = consumer expectations at rock bottom, positioned for positive surprise
Consumers spending more than sentiment surveys imply (actions > words)
Retail revenue beats low expectations -> XRT earnings revisions turn positive
XRT rallies 15-30% over 6 months as sentiment normalization drives spending recovery
source_reference: U of Michigan Consumer Sentiment; contrarian sentiment trading literature; 2022 case study

name: FRED Personal Savings Rate Decline from Peak -> Long Leisure/Hospitality (PEJ)
thesis: FRED PSAVERT (personal savings rate, monthly). When savings rate declines >4pp from a cyclical peak (e.g., drops from 12% to 8%), it signals consumers are DIS-SAVING to fund spending. This spending surge benefits leisure/hospitality (restaurants, hotels, travel) most because these are the first discretionary categories consumers re-engage with. Long PEJ (leisure/hospitality ETF) or BKNG+MAR+H for 6 months. The 2021-2022 savings rate collapse (34% -> 3%) preceded the leisure spending boom.
causal_chain: FRED PSAVERT declines >4pp from trailing 12-month peak
Consumers drawing down savings to fund spending = demand acceleration
Leisure/hospitality (restaurants, hotels, travel) are first beneficiaries of dis-saving
BKNG, MAR, H, restaurant chains see RevPAR/same-store sales acceleration
Leisure stocks re-rate on earnings beats over 3-6 months
source_reference: BEA personal income/savings; consumer spending cycle; 2021-2022 revenge spending precedent

name: FRED Retail Sales ex-Auto ex-Gas MoM Beat -> Long Consumer Discretionary
thesis: FRED RSFSXMV (retail sales excluding motor vehicles) removes the volatile auto component. When MoM growth exceeds +0.5% for 2 consecutive months after 3+ months of flat/negative, it signals core consumer demand inflecting. Long XLY for 90 days. The retail sales report drops mid-month and moves markets same-day, but the FOLLOW-THROUGH over the next 60 days is under-traded because markets price the headline instantly but not the trend confirmation.
causal_chain: FRED RSFSXMV (retail sales ex-auto) MoM > +0.5% for 2 consecutive months
Core consumer spending inflecting positively after a soft patch
Retailers see same-store sales accelerate, especially discretionary categories
Next month's retail report likely confirms the trend (momentum in consumer data)
XLY rallies 3-5% over 60-90 days as spending trend confirmation flows through
source_reference: Census Bureau advance retail sales; retail sales ex-auto as cleaner demand signal

name: FRED Non-Defense Capital Goods Orders ex-Aircraft Inflection -> Long Capital Goods (ETN/ROK/AME)
thesis: FRED NEWORDER (core capital goods orders = non-defense ex-aircraft, monthly) is the purest measure of business investment demand. When 3-month MA turns positive YoY after 6+ months of decline, it signals the capex cycle recovery. Long ETN (Eaton, electrical equipment), ROK (Rockwell, factory automation), AME (AMETEK, electronic instruments) for 6 months. Core capex orders lead equipment-maker revenue by 2-3 quarters because orders become deliveries/revenue on a 6-9 month cycle.
causal_chain: FRED NEWORDER (core capex orders) 3-month MA turns positive YoY after 6+ months negative
Business investment inflecting = companies ordering new equipment, machinery, automation
Equipment makers (ETN, ROK, AME) see order backlogs rebuild
Revenue accelerates 2-3 quarters later as orders convert to deliveries
Capital goods stocks re-rate on improving order books and revenue visibility
source_reference: Census Bureau core capital goods orders; Conference Board LEI component; capex cycle timing

name: FRED Industrial Production Manufacturing Index Breakout -> Long Factory Automation (ISRG/FANUY/ROK)
thesis: FRED IPMAN (Industrial Production: Manufacturing, monthly) crossing above its 12-month MA after being below for 6+ months is a confirmed manufacturing recovery signal. Long factory automation plays (ROK Rockwell, FANUY Fanuc, TER Teradyne) for 6 months. When manufacturing recovers, the first investment is in automation to boost productivity before hiring. Automation companies see order growth 1-2 quarters ahead of broader industrials because automation capex precedes expansion hiring.
causal_chain: FRED IPMAN crosses above 12-month MA after 6+ months below
Manufacturing output recovery confirmed -- factories increasing production
First capex priority: automation/robotics to boost output without hiring (labor tight)
ROK (factory automation), FANUY (industrial robots), TER (test automation) see order surge
Automation stocks re-rate ahead of broader industrials by 1-2 quarters
source_reference: Federal Reserve IP; automation-first capex cycle pattern; IFR robot installation data

name: FRED 5Y Breakeven Inflation Drop Below 2% -> Long TIPS (TIP ETF)
thesis: FRED T5YIE (5-year breakeven inflation rate, daily) below 2.0% signals the market is pricing inflation below the Fed's target. When T5YIE drops below 2.0% after being above 2.3% for 6+ months, TIPS are undervalued relative to nominal bonds. Long TIP (TIPS ETF) for 12 months. At sub-2% breakevens, TIPS outperform nominals if inflation comes in at or above the Fed's 2% target (which it has 80%+ of the time since 2000). The breakeven below target is a systematic mis-pricing.
causal_chain: FRED T5YIE drops below 2.0% after 6+ months above 2.3%
Market pricing inflation below Fed target = deflationary expectations excessive
TIPS coupon adjusts for actual CPI; if CPI > breakeven, TIPS outperform nominals
Fed targets 2% inflation and has tools to deliver it (rate cuts, QE if needed)
TIP outperforms IEF (nominal intermediate) by 3-5% over 12 months as breakevens normalize
source_reference: FRED breakeven inflation; TIPS vs nominal relative value; Fleckenstein-Longstaff-Lustig 2014 JFE

name: FRED Real Fed Funds Rate Turning Negative -> Long Growth Equities (QQQ)
thesis: FRED DFF (fed funds rate) minus FRED PCEPILFE (core PCE YoY) = real fed funds rate. When real fed funds turns negative (Fed rate below inflation), monetary policy is accommodative and financial conditions are easy. Long QQQ for 12 months. Growth stocks (tech, biotech) are the highest-duration equities and benefit most from negative real rates because their DCF valuations are most sensitive to discount rates. Every period of negative real rates since 2000 has preceded growth outperformance.
causal_chain: Real fed funds rate (DFF - core PCE) turns negative
Monetary policy accommodative = borrowing cost below inflation = free money in real terms
Financial conditions ease -> credit available -> speculative investment rises
Growth/tech stocks (longest duration equities) re-rate most on lower real discount rates
QQQ outperforms SPY by 10-20% over 12 months during negative real rate periods
data_sources: FRED DFF (daily, free), FRED PCEPILFE (monthly, free)
source_reference: Taylor Rule; real rate regime and equity duration; negative real rate -> growth outperformance

name: FRED 10Y-3M Yield Curve Steepening After Inversion -> Long Duration Bonds (TLT)
thesis: FRED T10Y3M (10Y minus 3M spread) steepening from deeply inverted (below -100bps) back through zero signals the Fed cutting cycle is underway. Long TLT for 12 months from the zero-crossing. The 10Y-3M curve is the most reliable recession predictor (Estrella-Mishkin), and its UN-inversion signals the rate-cutting cycle. TLT rallies 15-25% during cutting cycles as long-end yields follow the front-end down with a lag.
causal_chain: FRED T10Y3M crosses above 0 after being below -100bps for 6+ months
Un-inversion = Fed cutting aggressively, front-end yields falling fast
Long-end yields follow with a lag but also decline as recession fears ease
TLT price (inverse of 20Y+ yields) rallies on both rate expectations and duration bid
TLT returns 15-25% over 12 months during a typical Fed cutting cycle
source_reference: Estrella-Mishkin 1998; FRED yield curve; cutting cycle -> duration rally pattern

name: FRED Soybean Oil PPI Spike -> Packaged Food Margin Squeeze -> Long Hedged Players (GIS/CPB)
thesis: FRED WPU02220301 (soybean oil PPI) is a key input cost for packaged food companies. When soybean oil PPI rises >25% YoY, margin pressure hits branded food companies 1-2 quarters later as contracted input prices roll over. Companies with strong hedging programs and pricing power (GIS, CPB) pass costs through faster than private-label competitors. Long GIS/CPB relative to the food sector (XLP) as their gross margins recover first while competitors still absorb cost headwinds.
causal_chain: FRED WPU02220301 (soybean oil PPI) rises >25% YoY, signaling elevated fats/oils input costs
Packaged food companies face rising COGS as existing hedges roll off (6-12 month contracts)
Branded leaders (GIS, CPB) exercise pricing power - raise shelf prices within 1-2 quarters
Smaller/private-label competitors absorb costs longer, lose relative margin
GIS/CPB gross margins recover to pre-spike levels 2-3 quarters after pricing actions take effect
Long GIS/CPB outperforms broader food sector (XLP) by 5-10% over 6 months post-pricing
data_sources: FRED WPU02220301 (soybean oil PPI, monthly, free)
FRED PCU311311 (food manufacturing PPI, monthly, free)
source_reference: FRED PPI data; GIS/CPB 10-K input cost disclosures; food industry pricing pass-through literature

name: USDA Corn Crush Margin Expansion -> Long Ethanol/Corn Processors (ADM/GPRE)
thesis: The corn crush margin (ethanol revenue + DDG revenue - corn input cost) is the key profitability driver for corn processors. When FRED PCORNUS (corn price) drops >20% while FRED GASREGW (regular gas price) stays flat or rises, the crush margin expands dramatically. Long ADM (corn processing division) and GPRE (Green Plains, pure-play ethanol) for 90 days as the margin expansion flows through to earnings. FRED data on both sides of the spread enables clean backtesting.
causal_chain: FRED PCORNUS (corn received price) drops >20% from 6-month peak while FRED GASREGW stays flat
Corn crush margin (ethanol sales + DDG sales - corn cost) widens >/bushel above breakeven
Ethanol producers (ADM, GPRE) maximize throughput at expanded margins
Higher utilization rates compound margin expansion through fixed-cost leverage
Quarterly earnings surprise as crush margin flows through to P&L with 1-quarter lag
GPRE/ADM outperform SPY by 10-20% over 90 days from margin signal
source_reference: USDA ERS corn ethanol economics; Iowa State CARD ethanol profitability model; ADM segment disclosures

name: FRED Wheat PPI Collapse -> Long Restaurant/Food Service (DRI/MCD)
thesis: FRED WPU02110301 (wheat flour PPI) directly impacts food service costs (flour = 5-8% of restaurant COGS). When wheat flour PPI drops >15% YoY, restaurant chains see margin tailwind 2-3 quarters later as supply contracts reprice. Long restaurant operators with high flour exposure (DRI Darden, MCD bakery items, SBUX pastry) for 6 months. Restaurants are slow to cut menu prices, so the input cost decline goes straight to margin expansion.
causal_chain: FRED WPU02110301 (wheat flour PPI) drops >15% YoY signaling grain input cost relief
Restaurant supply chain contracts (typically quarterly/semi-annual) reprice at lower flour costs
Menu prices remain sticky - restaurants rarely lower prices when inputs decline
Gross margin expands 50-150bps as input savings flow through with no revenue offset
DRI, MCD, SBUX report margin expansion in Q+2 and Q+3 after flour decline
Long restaurant basket outperforms SPY by 5-12% over 6 months
data_sources: FRED WPU02110301 (wheat flour PPI, monthly, free)
yfinance DRI, MCD, SBUX, XLY, SPY
source_reference: FRED PPI wheat flour; NRA restaurant input cost surveys; DRI 10-K COGS breakdown

name: FRED Nondefense Capital Goods Orders ex-Aircraft → Long Capex Beneficiaries
thesis: FRED NEWORDER already covers durable goods ex-transport (PL075). But FRED A34SNO is the even narrower 'core capex proxy' — nondefense capital goods orders excluding aircraft. When A34SNO 3mo MA YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative, it's the cleanest signal of business investment recovery. Long ROK+ETN+AME (automation + electrical equipment) for 6 months. This series is the one the Fed actually watches for business investment trends.
causal_chain: FRED A34SNO (core capex orders) 3mo MA YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative
Business investment recovering — companies ordering machines, electrical equipment, instruments
Order-to-revenue lag: 2-4 quarters for capital goods manufacturers to deliver and book revenue
ROK (Rockwell Automation), ETN (Eaton), AME (Ametek) see backlog + revenue acceleration
Stocks re-rate as capex cycle broadens from orders to deliveries to earnings
source_reference: Census Bureau M3; Fed speeches on core capex as investment proxy

name: FRED Residential Fixed Investment Share of GDP Rising → Long Homebuilders
thesis: FRED A011RE1Q156NBEA (residential fixed investment as % of GDP, quarterly) captures housing's share of the economy. When RFI/GDP crosses above 4.0% from below after being depressed for 2+ years, it signals the housing cycle is entering its expansion phase. Long LEN+DHI+TOL for 12 months. RFI/GDP bottomed at 2.4% in 2010 (lowest since 1945) and the recovery to 4%+ preceded massive homebuilder outperformance.
causal_chain: FRED A011RE1Q156NBEA (RFI/GDP) crosses above 4.0% after 2+ years below
Housing cycle entering expansion phase — construction activity accelerating faster than GDP
Homebuilders (LEN, DHI, TOL) are the direct beneficiaries of rising housing investment
Revenue growth + margin expansion as deliveries and ASPs both increase
Homebuilder stocks compound for 12+ months during the RFI/GDP expansion phase
source_reference: BEA GDP components; housing cycle research; Bill McBride (Calculated Risk)

name: FRED Semiconductor PPI Deflation Trough -> Long Chip Designers (NVDA/AMD/AVGO)
thesis: FRED PCU33443344 (semiconductor manufacturing PPI) tracks chip pricing power. When semiconductor PPI YoY drops to -5% or worse then inflects upward (first positive MoM after 6+ months of declines), it signals the inventory glut is clearing and pricing power returning. Long fabless chip designers (NVDA, AMD, AVGO) which have highest operating leverage to ASP recovery. The PPI trough leads SIA monthly revenue inflection by 1-2 months.
causal_chain: FRED PCU33443344 (semiconductor PPI) YoY trough below -5% then first positive MoM change
PPI inflection signals inventory destocking complete - channel pulling new orders at firmer prices
Fabless designers (NVDA, AMD, AVGO) have 60-70% gross margins highly sensitive to ASPs
Revenue beats materialize 1-2 quarters after pricing power returns
SMH/fabless basket rallies 15-30% from PPI trough as cycle turn confirmed
Long NVDA/AMD/AVGO for 6 months from inflection signal
data_sources: FRED NEWORDER (manufacturers new orders, monthly, free)
yfinance NVDA, AMD, AVGO, SMH, SPY
source_reference: FRED semiconductor PPI; SIA monthly revenue data; IC Insights cycle analysis

name: FRED Computer/Electronic Parts Inventories Destock -> Long Tech Distributors (ARW/AVT)
thesis: FRED A33SNO (computer/electronic product manufacturers inventories) tracks tech supply chain inventory levels. When inventories drop >10% from peak over 6+ months and then stabilize (MoM change turns positive), the restocking cycle begins. Tech distributors (ARW Arrow Electronics, AVT Avnet) see order books recover first as OEMs rebuild safety stock. Long ARW/AVT for 6 months from inventory stabilization. Distributors are the earliest beneficiary of restocking because they sit between component makers and OEMs.
causal_chain: FRED A33SNO (computer/electronic inventories) drops >10% from peak then MoM turns positive
Destocking complete - OEMs have drawn down excess and now reorder at market rates
Distributors (ARW, AVT) see billings and bookings inflect as restocking orders arrive
Book-to-bill ratio crosses above 1.0 at the distributor level
Revenue and margin recovery at ARW/AVT over the next 2-3 quarters
Long ARW/AVT captures 15-25% upside as cycle turns
source_reference: FRED inventory data; Arrow/Avnet quarterly distribution metrics; SIA channel inventory analysis

name: FRED Health Services Employment Surge -> Long Hospital Operators (HCA/THC/UHS)
thesis: FRED CES6562000101 (health care employment, monthly) is a direct proxy for healthcare utilization since hospitals hire in response to patient volume. When health services employment rises >3% YoY for 3+ months after a period of contraction, it signals sustained utilization recovery. Long hospital operators HCA, THC (Tenet), UHS for 6 months. Hospital stocks are driven by volume (admissions/surgeries), and employment is the best free real-time volume proxy. Employment data leads quarterly admissions data by 1-2 months.
causal_chain: FRED CES6562000101 (health care employment) rises >3% YoY for 3+ consecutive months
Rising healthcare employment signals hospitals are staffing up for sustained patient volume growth
More staff = more surgical suites open, more beds staffed = higher admissions capacity utilized
Revenue per adjusted admission rises as high-acuity surgical volumes recover
HCA/THC/UHS report admissions and revenue beats 1-2 quarters after employment surge
Long hospital operators for 6 months captures the utilization recovery trade
data_sources: FRED CES6562000101 (health care employment, monthly, free)
yfinance HCA, THC, UHS, XLV, SPY
source_reference: BLS health care employment; HCA 10-K volume metrics; AHA hospital utilization surveys

name: FRED Prescription Drug PPI Inflation -> Long Pharmacy Benefit Managers (CI/CVS/UNH)
thesis: FRED CUUR0000SEMF01 (CPI prescription drugs) or FRED PCU325411325411 (pharma PPI) rising >5% YoY signals drug price inflation. Counter-intuitively, PBMs (Cigna/Express Scripts, CVS/Caremark, UNH/Optum Rx) benefit from drug price inflation because their rebate revenue is a PERCENTAGE of list price. Higher list prices = higher rebate dollars even if rebate percentages stay constant. Long CI/CVS/UNH for 6 months when drug PPI accelerates.
causal_chain: FRED PCU325411325411 (pharmaceutical PPI) rises >5% YoY for 3+ months
Drug manufacturers raising list prices across branded portfolio (annual January + mid-year increases)
PBMs negotiate rebates as % of WAC (wholesale acquisition cost) - typically 30-60%
Higher WAC = higher absolute rebate dollars per script even at same rebate %
PBM spread income (rebate retained - pass-through to plan) expands with price level
CI/CVS/UNH pharmacy services segments beat earnings expectations by 2-5%
data_sources: FRED CUUR0000SEMF01 (CPI prescription drugs, monthly, free)
yfinance CI, CVS, UNH, XLV, SPY
source_reference: FRED pharmaceutical PPI; PBM rebate economics; Express Scripts/Caremark rebate retention literature

name: FRED Medical Equipment Spending Recovery -> Long Medtech (SYK/ISRG/MDT)
thesis: FRED AMDMNO (durable goods new orders, nondefense capital goods) includes medical device capital spending signals. When FRED S4423SM (health/personal care store sales) rises >6% YoY alongside FRED CES6562000101 (healthcare employment) rising >2% YoY, it confirms a broad healthcare utilization recovery that drives elective surgical procedure volumes. Long surgical medtech (SYK orthopedics, ISRG robotic surgery, MDT implantables) for 9 months as deferred procedures accelerate.
causal_chain: FRED S4423SM (health care store sales) rises >6% YoY for 3+ months
Rising health store sales proxies increasing outpatient/elective care utilization
Simultaneously FRED CES6562000101 (healthcare employment) confirms staffing recovery
Deferred elective procedures (knee/hip replacement, robotic surgery, cardiac implants) accelerate
Medtech companies (SYK, ISRG, MDT) see procedure volume and device revenue surge
Long surgical medtech basket for 9 months captures multi-quarter volume recovery
data_sources: FRED S4423SM (health care store sales, monthly, free)
FRED CES6562000101 (health care employment, monthly, free)
yfinance SYK, ISRG, MDT, XLV, SPY
source_reference: FRED retail sales health care; BLS employment; SYK/ISRG procedure volume disclosures

name: FRED Vehicle Age Peak -> Long Auto Parts Retailers (AZO/ORLY/AAP)
thesis: FRED TOTALSA (total vehicle sales, monthly) and FRED AISRSA (auto inventory/sales ratio) together proxy the new car purchase cycle. When TOTALSA stays below 15M SAAR for 12+ months while AISRSA remains below 2.0, consumers cannot buy new cars and the US fleet ages. Vehicles age 6-12 years = sweet spot for aftermarket parts demand (brakes, batteries, suspension). Long AZO/ORLY for 12 months when the purchase drought signal persists. The average US vehicle age hit 12.6 years in 2024 -- the oldest ever.
causal_chain: FRED TOTALSA stays below 15M SAAR for 12+ months (depressed new car sales)
Simultaneously FRED AISRSA below 2.0 (dealers have no inventory to sell)
Consumers keep existing vehicles longer -- average fleet age continues rising
Vehicles age 6-12 years enter peak maintenance/repair demand window
AZO/ORLY/AAP same-store sales accelerate as repair frequency rises with fleet age
Long auto parts retailers for 12 months; compounding benefit until new car supply normalizes
data_sources: FRED TOTALSA (total vehicle sales, monthly, free)
FRED AISRSA (auto inventory/sales ratio, monthly, free)
source_reference: FRED vehicle sales; BEA auto data; S&P Global Mobility average vehicle age study; AZO/ORLY investor day fleet age thesis

name: FRED Motor Vehicle Output Recovery -> Long Auto OEM Suppliers (BWA/LEA/APTV)
thesis: FRED IPG3361T3S (motor vehicle and parts manufacturing IP index) is the most direct measure of US auto production volume. When this index rises >8% from a cyclical trough (after being down >10% from peak), auto OEM suppliers see revenue recovery with high operating leverage. Long Tier 1 suppliers (BWA BorgWarner, LEA Lear, APTV Aptiv) for 6 months. Suppliers have 40-50% fixed costs, so production volume recovery amplifies earnings recovery 2-3x versus revenue growth.
causal_chain: FRED IPG3361T3S (motor vehicle/parts IP) rises >8% from cyclical trough after >10% decline
Production recovery = OEMs ramping assembly lines, pulling more components from Tier 1 suppliers
Suppliers (BWA, LEA, APTV) have high fixed-cost structures (40-50%)
Volume recovery creates operating leverage -- margins expand 300-500bps on incremental units
Earnings rebound 2-3x the rate of revenue recovery due to fixed-cost absorption
Supplier stocks re-rate from trough multiples as volume recovery confirms
data_sources: FRED IPG3361T3S (motor vehicle IP, monthly, free)
FRED TOTALSA (vehicle sales for demand confirmation, monthly, free)
source_reference: FRED industrial production; BWA/LEA 10-K operating leverage disclosures; IHS Markit auto production forecasts

name: FRED CRE Loan Delinquency Trough -> Long Office/Retail REITs (BXP/SPG)
thesis: FRED DRCLACBS (commercial real estate loan delinquency rate at banks) is published quarterly. When CRE delinquency peaks and first declines QoQ after rising for 4+ quarters, it signals the worst of the CRE credit cycle is behind. Long high-quality REITs (BXP Boston Properties office, SPG Simon Property retail) for 12 months. These best-in-class operators are priced for disaster during peak delinquency, but they own trophy assets that recover first. The delinquency turn leads REIT FFO recovery by 2-3 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED DRCLACBS (CRE loan delinquency) peaks and first declines QoQ after 4+ quarters of increases
Peak delinquency = worst credit losses priced in -- marginal improvement begins
Banks stop tightening CRE lending -- refinancing availability improves for quality assets
High-quality REITs (BXP trophy office, SPG A-mall retail) refinance maturing debt at improved terms
Occupancy stabilizes as tenant demand improves in the recovery
BXP/SPG re-rate from distressed multiples (10-12x FFO) toward normalized (15-18x) over 12 months
data_sources: FRED DRCLACBS (CRE delinquency rate, quarterly, free)
FRED DRTSCILM (bank lending standards, quarterly, free)
source_reference: FRED bank delinquency data; FDIC quarterly banking profile; GFC CRE cycle precedent (delinquency peaked Q4 2010)

name: FRED CMBS Spread Compression -> Long CRE-Exposed Regional Banks (KEY/CFG/ZION)
thesis: FRED BAMLHE0A0HYM2 (ICE BofA high yield option-adjusted spread) contains a CRE credit signal. When HY spreads compress >100bps from their trailing 12mo peak while FRED DRTSCILM (SLOOS CRE lending standards) shows net tightening declining, CRE-exposed regional banks benefit. Their CRE loan portfolios stop seeing new impairments, and allowance reserves release into earnings. Long KEY/CFG/ZION for 6 months from the spread compression + lending easing dual signal.
causal_chain: FRED BAMLHE0A0HYM2 (HY OAS) compresses >100bps from 12-month peak
Credit market stress easing broadly -- risk appetite returning for lower-quality assets including CRE
FRED DRTSCILM (SLOOS) shows net tightening declining from peak -- banks loosening CRE standards
CRE-heavy regional banks (KEY, CFG, ZION) see impairment rates stabilize on CRE book
Loan loss provision declines -> allowance reserve releases flow to EPS
Regional bank stocks re-rate as CRE tail risk reprices from catastrophic to manageable
data_sources: yfinance KEY, CFG, ZION, KRE, SPY
source_reference: FRED credit spreads; Fed SLOOS; regional bank CRE exposure disclosures (KEY 20%+, ZION 25%+ of loans)

name: FRED Nonresidential Construction Spending Inflection -> Long Industrial REITs (PLD/REXR)
thesis: FRED TLNRESCONS (nonresidential construction spending) captures commercial, industrial, and institutional building. When nonresidential construction spending growth turns positive YoY after 6+ months of decline, industrial/logistics REITs (PLD Prologis, REXR Rexford) benefit from the demand side -- more factories/warehouses being built means more logistics space needed around them. Long PLD/REXR for 12 months. Industrial REIT occupancy and rent growth correlate with nonresidential construction because new factories generate warehouse demand in their supply chain radius.
causal_chain: FRED TLNRESCONS (nonresidential construction spending) YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative
Recovery signals new factories, data centers, hospitals being built
Each new facility creates logistics/warehouse demand in surrounding supply chain radius
Industrial REITs (PLD, REXR) see leasing activity and occupancy improve
Same-store NOI growth reaccelerates as new leases signed at market rents
Long PLD/REXR for 12 months as construction-driven demand flows to industrial space
source_reference: FRED construction spending; Prologis research on factory-to-logistics demand multiplier; CBRE industrial demand studies

name: FRED Michigan Consumer Expectations Trough -> Long Luxury Retail (RL/TPR/CPRI)
thesis: FRED UMCSENT (U of Michigan consumer sentiment) has an expectations sub-component (FRED MICH). When expectations trough below 60 and then rise >10pts over 3 months, it signals a consumer confidence recovery that drives aspirational/luxury spending first. Luxury retail (RL Ralph Lauren, TPR Tapestry, CPRI Capri) has the highest sensitivity to sentiment because luxury purchases are entirely discretionary and deferred during pessimism. Long luxury retail basket for 6 months from expectations inflection.
causal_chain: FRED MICH (Michigan expectations) troughs below 60 then rises >10pts in 3 months
Consumer confidence recovery = willingness to make discretionary/aspirational purchases returns
Luxury purchases (handbags, designer apparel) are most deferrable and recover most sharply
Luxury retail same-store sales accelerate 2-4 quarters after sentiment inflection
RL/TPR/CPRI earnings revisions turn positive as analysts see traffic/ASP recovery
Long luxury basket captures 15-25% upside as sentiment-driven demand reflation plays out
data_sources: FRED UMCSENT (Michigan sentiment headline, monthly, free)
yfinance RL, TPR, CPRI, XRT, SPY
source_reference: U of Michigan consumer surveys; luxury retail sentiment elasticity; TPR/CPRI 10-K demand drivers

name: FRED Consumer Confidence Present Situation Surge -> Long Travel/Leisure (MAR/H/EXPE)
thesis: Conference Board Consumer Confidence has two components: Present Situation (FRED CSCICP03USM665S) and Expectations. When Present Situation rises >20pts over 6 months from a trough below 100, consumers are feeling good about their CURRENT financial position and splurge on experiential spending (travel, hotels, dining). Long travel/leisure stocks (MAR Marriott, H Hyatt, EXPE Expedia) for 6 months. The Present Situation index specifically captures "right now" confidence, which directly drives booking behavior. It leads hotel RevPAR by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED CSCICP03USM665S (Conference Board Present Situation) rises >20pts over 6mo from trough below 100
Consumers feel good about current job/income situation -- willing to spend on experiences
Travel booking intent surges -- air bookings, hotel reservations, vacation spending rises
Hotel RevPAR accelerates as both occupancy and ADR improve with demand
MAR/H/EXPE report revenue beats 1-2 quarters after confidence recovery
Long travel/leisure basket for 6 months from Present Situation inflection
data_sources: FRED CSCICP03USM665S (CB Present Situation, monthly, free)
yfinance MAR, H, EXPE, PEJ, SPY
source_reference: Conference Board consumer confidence; STR hotel RevPAR; travel booking lead times vs confidence

name: FRED Container Freight Rate Collapse -> Long Import-Dependent Retailers (COST/TGT/WMT)
thesis: FRED DHLSFRTI (DHL supply chain friction index) or Harpex/Freightos container rate indices track trans-Pacific shipping costs. When container freight rates collapse >50% from peak, import-dependent retailers see COGS relief with a 2-3 quarter lag as new lower-cost inventory cycles through. Long COST/TGT/WMT for 6 months from the rate collapse. These big-box importers ship 40-60% of goods from Asia; lower ocean freight is a pure margin tailwind that analysts are slow to model because they use trailing freight costs.
causal_chain: Container freight rates (Freightos/Harpex) collapse >50% from trailing 12mo peak
New container bookings lock in lower rates -- 4-8 week transit time to US ports
Import-dependent retailers (COST, TGT, WMT) receive goods at 30-50% lower inbound logistics cost
New lower-cost inventory enters stores 2-3 months after rate decline
Gross margin expands 50-100bps without price changes -- goes straight to profit
Retailer earnings beat consensus 2-3 quarters after freight collapse as margin tailwind materializes
data_sources: FRED DHLSFRTI (DHL freight friction, monthly, free)
yfinance COST, TGT, WMT, XRT, SPY
source_reference: FRED supply chain indices; Freightos Baltic container index; WMT/TGT 10-K inbound freight cost disclosures

name: FRED Import Price Index Recovery -> Long Commodity Trading Houses (ADM/BG/GLNCY)
thesis: FRED IR (import price index, monthly) captures the cost of goods entering the US including raw materials. When import prices rise >5% YoY after a period of decline (-5% or worse for 6+ months), it signals a global commodity demand recovery. Commodity trading/logistics firms (ADM Archer-Daniels, BG Bunge, GLNCY Glencore) benefit from rising physical commodity flows -- their margins are driven by VOLUME and VOLATILITY, not price direction alone. Long ADM/BG for 6 months from the import price inflection.
causal_chain: FRED IR (import price index) YoY turns positive >+5% after 6+ months below -5%
Rising import prices signal recovering global demand for raw materials and commodities
Higher commodity prices increase the dollar value of physical flows through trading houses
ADM/BG earn origination/destination margins on larger-value cargoes + increased volumes
Trading volatility around the inflection creates additional spread-capture opportunities
ADM/BG earnings recover as commodity cycle turns -- stocks re-rate from trough multiples
data_sources: FRED IR (import price index, monthly, free, back to 1982)
source_reference: FRED import price index; ADM/BG segment disclosures; commodity trading margin analysis

name: FRED Port-Level Import Surge -> Long Warehouse/Logistics REITs (PLD/STAG)
thesis: FRED IMPGS (real goods imports, monthly) measures the physical volume of imports entering the US. When real goods imports rise >8% YoY for 3+ months, port-adjacent warehouse demand surges as importers need space to deconsolidate containers. Long logistics REITs (PLD Prologis, STAG Industrial) concentrated in port markets (LA/LB, Savannah, NJ). The import surge creates a 1-2 quarter lag to warehouse leasing because importers initially overflow into temporary space before signing long-term leases.
causal_chain: FRED IMPGS (real goods imports) rises >8% YoY for 3+ consecutive months
Physical volume of inbound containers exceeds existing warehouse capacity in port markets
Importers initially use temporary/overflow space; within 1-2 quarters, sign long-term warehouse leases
PLD/STAG port-adjacent portfolios see occupancy rise and market rents accelerate
Same-store NOI growth accelerates as mark-to-market on renewals widens
Long logistics REITs for 6 months captures the import-driven demand wave
data_sources: FRED IMPGS (real goods imports, monthly, free)
source_reference: FRED trade data; Prologis IBI (Industrial Business Indicator); CBRE port market logistics research

name: FRED HDD Spike Above Normal -> Long Regulated Utilities (DUK/SO/NEE)
thesis: NOAA publishes weekly HDD (heating degree days, free) and FRED tracks natural gas consumption. When cumulative HDD through Jan 31 exceeds the 10-year average by >15%, regulated electric/gas utilities see volumetric revenue uplift that is NOT offset by cost increases (because fuel costs are passed through to ratepayers). Long regulated utilities (DUK, SO, NEE) for 90 days from the HDD threshold crossing. The key insight: regulated utilities earn a MARGIN on each delivered unit -- higher volume = higher dollar margins at near-zero incremental cost.
causal_chain: NOAA weekly HDD data shows cumulative season-to-date >15% above 10-year average by Jan 31
Colder winter drives higher heating demand -- more gas/electric delivered to customers
Regulated utilities pass fuel costs through to ratepayers (no margin impact from fuel)
Volumetric revenue rises because utilities earn a distribution margin per unit delivered
Higher volumes at same margin per unit = incremental profit at near-zero cost
DUK/SO/NEE beat Q4/Q1 EPS estimates as weather-driven volume bonus flows through
data_sources: NOAA HDD weekly (free, also FRED GHDDUSP for gas-weighted HDD)
FRED GASPRICE (natural gas citygate price for pass-through confirmation)
yfinance DUK, SO, NEE, XLU, SPY
source_reference: NOAA CPC degree day data; utility rate case structure (volumetric margin); DUK/SO earnings weather adjustment disclosures

name: FRED Property Insurance CPI Acceleration -> Long P&C Reinsurers (RNR/ACGL/RE)
thesis: FRED CUSR0000SEHC (CPI tenants/household insurance) captures the retail insurance pricing cycle with a 6-12 month lag to wholesale reinsurance pricing. When insurance CPI rises >8% YoY for 6+ months, it confirms a hard market where primary insurers are raising premiums -- which means reinsurers are also charging higher rates on renewals. Long P&C reinsurers (RNR RenaissanceRe, ACGL Arch Capital, RE Everest) for 12 months. Reinsurers have the highest operating leverage to rate increases because their loss ratios are relatively fixed while premium income grows.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SEHC (CPI household insurance) rises >8% YoY for 6+ consecutive months
Insurance CPI confirms hard market -- primary insurers passing rate increases to consumers
Primary insurer rate increases flow through to reinsurance renewals (Jan 1 and Jun/Jul renewals)
Reinsurers (RNR, ACGL, RE) see premium income grow while loss ratios stay stable
Combined ratios improve 3-5 points on rate adequacy -- underwriting profit expands
Reinsurer stocks re-rate as hardening cycle confirms multi-year earnings expansion
data_sources: FRED CUSR0000SEHC (CPI household insurance, monthly, free)
source_reference: FRED CPI insurance; Guy Carpenter reinsurance rate-on-line surveys; hard market cycle literature

name: FRED Federal Defense Spending Acceleration -> Long Defense Primes (LMT/RTX/NOC/GD)
thesis: FRED FDEFX (federal defense consumption expenditures, quarterly, real) is the most direct measure of actual defense spending (not authorization/appropriation which leads by 12-24 months). When FDEFX QoQ annualized growth exceeds +5% for 2 consecutive quarters after a period of flat/negative spending, defense primes see backlog-to-revenue conversion accelerate. Long LMT/RTX/NOC/GD for 12 months. The key lag: Congress authorizes spending 1-3 years before it shows up in FDEFX, so when FDEFX inflects, the spending is already contractually committed and cannot be clawed back.
causal_chain: FRED FDEFX (real defense spending) QoQ annualized >+5% for 2 consecutive quarters
Defense spending acceleration means prior-year appropriations are converting to actual outlays
Primes (LMT, RTX, NOC, GD) see existing backlog converting to recognized revenue faster
Production ramp on committed contracts = higher utilization on defense manufacturing lines
Revenue growth accelerates + operating leverage from higher utilization = earnings expansion
Defense stocks re-rate as multi-year spending trajectory confirmed by actual outlay data
data_sources: FRED FDEFX (federal defense spending, quarterly, free, back to 1947)
FRED A824RX1Q020SBEA (real federal spending, quarterly, free)
yfinance LMT, RTX, NOC, GD, ITA, SPY
source_reference: FRED BEA NIPA defense spending; DoD budget execution reports; defense prime backlog disclosures

name: FRED State/Local Construction Spending Surge -> Long Engineering/Construction (PWR/MTZ/PRIM)
thesis: FRED TLPBLCONS (state and local public construction spending, monthly) tracks government infrastructure investment at the state/local level. When TLPBLCONS rises >12% YoY for 4+ months, it signals IIJA/state bond money flowing into road, bridge, water, and grid projects. Long specialty engineering contractors (PWR Quanta, MTZ MasTec, PRIM Primoris) for 12 months. These firms have 85%+ public-sector revenue and 18-24 month backlogs that grow with public spending commitments. The spending data confirms actual contract execution, not just legislative intent.
causal_chain: FRED TLPBLCONS (state/local public construction) rises >12% YoY for 4+ months
Federal (IIJA) and state-level infrastructure dollars flowing into active construction contracts
Specialty contractors (PWR, MTZ, PRIM) awarded multi-year contracts for power grid, roads, bridges
Backlog grows to 18-24 months of revenue visibility -- highest certainty revenue stream
Revenue acceleration + scale efficiencies = margin expansion over 2-4 quarters
Long contractor basket for 12 months as public spending sustains multi-year growth
data_sources: FRED TLPBLCONS (state/local public construction, monthly, free)
yfinance PWR, MTZ, PRIM, PAVE, SPY
source_reference: FRED construction spending; IIJA implementation tracker; PWR/MTZ backlog disclosures

name: FRED Federal Nondefense Investment Spike -> Long IT/Consulting (ACN/LDOS/BAH)
thesis: FRED A782RX1Q020SBEA (real federal nondefense gross investment) tracks civilian agency capital spending. When this series rises >10% YoY for 2+ quarters, it signals accelerating federal IT modernization, cybersecurity, and consulting spending. Long government IT/consulting firms (ACN federal division, LDOS Leidos, BAH Booz Allen) for 12 months. These firms have GWACs (government-wide acquisition contracts) that allow rapid task order growth when agency budgets expand. The spending data leads contract award announcements by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED A782RX1Q020SBEA (real federal nondefense investment) rises >10% YoY for 2+ quarters
Civilian agencies (DHS, HHS, VA, DOE) accelerating IT modernization and cloud migration
Agencies issue task orders under existing GWAC/IDIQ vehicles to firms already qualified
ACN/LDOS/BAH see organic revenue growth accelerate from federal segments
Federal contract margins are stable at 8-12% EBIT -- volume growth drives profit
Long government IT basket for 12 months as spending ramp confirmed by actual outlays
data_sources: FRED A782RX1Q020SBEA (federal nondefense investment, quarterly, free)
source_reference: FRED BEA nondefense spending; USASpending.gov; LDOS/BAH federal revenue segment reports

name: FRED Financial Conditions Easing From Tight -> Long Small-Cap Value (IWN)
thesis: FRED NFCI (Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index) measures overall financial tightness. When NFCI drops below 0 (easing) after being above +0.3 (tight) for 6+ months, the loosening financial conditions benefit the most credit-sensitive segment: small-cap value stocks (IWN). These companies are dependent on bank lending and capital markets access. Long IWN for 12 months from the NFCI zero-crossing. Small-cap value has outperformed large-cap growth by 15-25% in the 12 months following financial conditions easing in every cycle since 1973.
causal_chain: FRED NFCI drops below 0 (loose) after 6+ months above +0.3 (tight)
Financial conditions easing = bank lending expanding, credit spreads tightening, volatility declining
Small-cap value companies most credit-sensitive -- depend on bank loans and capital markets access
Easing conditions unlock refinancing, capex, M&A for small-cap value universe
Earnings recovery accelerates as financial constraint lifts -- highest operating leverage segment
IWN outperforms QQQ by 15-25% over 12 months as rotation from growth to value unfolds
source_reference: Chicago Fed NFCI; financial conditions -> small-cap credit channel literature; Bernanke-Gertler financial accelerator

name: FRED Excess Reserves + M2 Velocity Inflection -> Long Commodities (DJP/GSG)
thesis: FRED M2V (velocity of M2 money stock, quarterly) measures how actively money is circulating in the economy. When M2V inflects upward after multi-quarter decline AND FRED M2SL YoY is positive, it signals that the expanded money supply is finally being SPENT rather than hoarded. This dual signal (more money + faster circulation) is the strongest leading indicator for commodity demand. Long broad commodity baskets (DJP, GSG) for 12 months. The velocity inflection distinguishes inflationary money printing (velocity rising) from deflationary money printing (velocity falling).
causal_chain: FRED M2V (M2 velocity) QoQ turns positive after 4+ quarters of decline
Simultaneously FRED M2SL YoY positive -- money supply still expanding
More money circulating faster = nominal GDP growth accelerating = commodity demand rising
Physical commodity demand (energy, metals, agriculture) responds to nominal GDP
Commodity prices rise as demand growth exceeds supply response
Long DJP/GSG broad commodity basket for 12 months from the velocity inflection
data_sources: FRED M2V (M2 velocity, quarterly, free, back to 1959)
FRED M2SL (M2 money supply, monthly, free)
source_reference: FRED M2 velocity; quantity theory of money; velocity + supply dual signal for commodity demand

name: FRED Term Premium Turning Positive -> Long Financials (XLF) / Short Long Duration (TLT)
thesis: FRED THREEFYTP10 (Adrian-Crump-Moench 10Y term premium, daily) measures the compensation investors demand for holding long-duration bonds. When term premium crosses above zero after being negative for 12+ months, it signals regime shift: investors demanding more compensation for duration risk. Banks (XLF) benefit from steeper yield curves (higher NIM) while long-duration bonds (TLT) underperform. Long XLF for 12 months from the zero-crossing. Rising term premium is the purest signal of a regime where banks outperform bonds.
causal_chain: FRED THREEFYTP10 (ACM 10Y term premium) crosses above 0 after 12+ months negative
Positive term premium = investors demanding compensation for long-duration risk
Yield curve steepens as long-end yields rise faster than short-end (term premium widens the spread)
Banks earn wider NIM (net interest margin) as they borrow short and lend long at wider spread
XLF (financials) outperforms as bank earnings benefit from steeper curve
Long XLF captures the NIM expansion trade; financial sector re-rates higher
data_sources: FRED THREEFYTP10 (term premium, daily, free, back to 1961)
source_reference: NY Fed ACM term premium model; Kim-Wright term premium; bank NIM vs yield curve slope literature

name: FRED Wilshire 5000 / GDP Ratio Mean-Reversion → Long SPY from Undervaluation
thesis: The Buffett Indicator (total market cap / GDP) is the most famous valuation metric. FRED WILL5000INDFC / GDP (or proxy WILL5000PR / GDP). When the ratio drops below 1.0 (market cap < GDP = historically undervalued), long SPY for 12 months. The ratio hit 0.7 in 2009 and 0.85 in 2020 — both preceded massive rallies. The signal fires rarely but with extreme conviction. Data back to 1970.
causal_chain: FRED WILL5000INDFC / GDP ratio drops below 1.0 (historically undervalued)
Market cap < GDP signals equities are cheap relative to the economy's productive capacity
Institutional investors recognize the valuation floor and increase equity allocation
Mean-reversion begins: equities rally as economy recovers and earnings normalize
SPY returns 20-50% over the following 12 months from deeply undervalued levels
data_sources: FRED WILL5000INDFC (daily, free) + FRED GDP (quarterly, free)
source_reference: Warren Buffett / Fortune 2001; FRED Wilshire 5000

name: FRED Owners Equivalent Rent Deceleration → Long TLT (Shelter CPI Lag)
thesis: FRED CUSR0000SEHC (CPI Owners' Equivalent Rent, monthly) has ~40% weight in core CPI. OER lags actual market rents by 12-18 months because BLS surveys are slow. When OER YoY decelerates by >100bps from its peak (e.g., from 6.5% to 5.5%), the disinflation pipeline is confirmed — core CPI will keep falling for months. Long TLT for 6 months. The OER deceleration is visible NOW but the market waits for the actual CPI print to react, creating a predictable lag.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SEHC (OER) YoY decelerates >100bps from its trailing peak
OER = ~40% of core CPI; its deceleration mechanically pulls core CPI lower over coming months
BLS methodology means OER will keep decelerating for 6-12 months after market rents stabilize
Bond market gradually prices in the disinflation pipeline → yields decline
TLT rallies 5-15% over 6 months as the shelter disinflation flows through to CPI prints
source_reference: BLS CPI methodology; shelter CPI lag research (Bolhuis-Cramer-Summers 2022)

name: FRED Bank Reserves Surge → Long Risk Assets (Liquidity Injection)
thesis: FRED TOTRESNS (total reserves of depository institutions, biweekly) spikes when the Fed injects liquidity — QE, emergency lending, discount window usage. When reserves surge >15% in a rolling 3-month window, it floods the banking system with excess cash that flows into risk assets. Long QQQ for 6 months. Reserves surged in 2008-Q4 (TARP), 2020-Q1 (COVID QE), and 2023-Q1 (BTFP after SVB). Each preceded a major equity rally. Distinct from PL85 (WRESBAL reserve balances — different series, different threshold).
causal_chain: FRED TOTRESNS (biweekly bank reserves) surges >15% in rolling 3 months
Fed liquidity injection = excess reserves flooding the banking system
Banks deploy excess reserves into Treasuries, agency MBS, and through lending
Asset prices rise as liquidity finds its way into financial markets
QQQ (highest-beta large-cap index) rallies 15-40% over 6 months
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.3 Aggregate Reserves; Pozsar liquidity framework

name: FRED Industrial Production Mining Recovery → Long Commodity Producers
thesis: FRED IPN213111S (industrial production: mining, monthly) specifically tracks oil/gas/coal/metal extraction output. When mining IP YoY turns positive after 6+ months of decline, it confirms commodity supply recovery — production costs have been cut, marginal producers exited, survivors are lean. Long XOP+PICK (energy+metals miners) for 6 months. Mining IP recovery leads commodity equity earnings beats by 1-2 quarters as volume recovery compounds with lean cost structures.
causal_chain: FRED IPN213111S (mining industrial production) YoY turns positive after 6+ months negative
Mining/extraction output recovering — surviving producers are lean from cost-cutting during downturn
Volume recovery on a lower cost base = operating leverage → margin expansion
XOP (energy producers) and PICK (metals miners) see earnings inflect positive
Commodity equity stocks re-rate 3-6 months as production recovery flows to earnings
source_reference: Federal Reserve Industrial Production; mining sector operating leverage research

name: FRED Auto Loan Delinquency Rate Trough → Long Auto Lenders
thesis: FRED DRCARLACBS (auto loan delinquency rate, quarterly) tracks consumer auto credit health. When the DQ rate hits a cyclical trough (local minimum AND level < 2.5%), auto lenders are at peak credit quality — losses are minimal, provisions are over-reserved, and earnings are about to beat. Long ALLY+COF for 12 months from the trough. The trough precedes the next credit expansion cycle where auto lending volumes ramp.
causal_chain: FRED DRCARLACBS (quarterly auto loan DQ rate) hits local minimum below 2.5%
Peak credit quality = auto lenders' loss reserves are over-provisioned
Provision releases boost earnings 1-2 quarters after the DQ trough
Simultaneously, clean credit conditions support new auto lending volume growth
ALLY and COF (auto lending divisions) re-rate on earnings beats + volume growth
source_reference: Federal Reserve; auto lending cycle research

name: FRED Personal Income Growth Acceleration → Long Consumer Cyclicals
thesis: FRED PI (personal income, monthly) is the broadest measure of household purchasing power. When PI YoY growth reaccelerates above +5% after a deceleration period (<+3% for 6+ months), consumers have more money to spend. Long XLY for 6 months. Personal income acceleration leads consumer spending by 1-2 months because income arrives before it's spent. Data back to 1959.
causal_chain: FRED PI (monthly personal income) YoY crosses above +5% after 6+ months below +3%
Household purchasing power expanding — wages, transfers, investment income all rising
Consumer spending accelerates 1-2 months later as income flows to consumption
Discretionary sectors (restaurants, travel, electronics, autos) benefit first
XLY re-rates on improving consumer spending data
source_reference: BEA Personal Income and Outlays; consumer spending cycle

name: FRED Corporate Profits After Tax Recovery → Long SPY (Earnings Cycle)
thesis: FRED CP (corporate profits after tax, quarterly BEA) is the definitive measure of aggregate earnings. When CP YoY turns positive after 2+ quarters of negative growth, the earnings recession is over. Long SPY for 12 months. Corporate profits lead S&P 500 earnings-per-share by 1-2 quarters because BEA captures the full economy while S&P EPS only covers 500 companies. The CP trough has preceded every equity rally since 1950.
causal_chain: FRED CP (quarterly corporate profits after tax) YoY turns positive after 2+ quarters negative
Earnings recession over — profits recovering across the economy
S&P 500 EPS follows 1-2 quarters later as company-level reporting catches up
Equity multiples expand as investors price in earnings recovery
SPY rallies 15-30% over 12 months as the earnings upcycle broadens
source_reference: BEA NIPA corporate profits; earnings cycle research

name: FRED Private Nonresidential Construction Surge → Long Infrastructure Contractors
thesis: FRED PNRESCONS (private nonresidential construction spending, monthly) tracks commercial/industrial building. When PNRESCONS YoY growth crosses +10% for 3+ months, it signals a private capex boom — data centers, manufacturing plants, warehouses, retail. Long PWR+PRIM+ACM for 6 months. These contractors convert construction spending directly to revenue. The current boom is driven by reshoring (CHIPS Act fabs) + data center buildout. Data back to 2002.
causal_chain: FRED PNRESCONS (monthly private nonres construction) YoY > +10% for 3 months
Private capex boom confirmed: data centers, fabs, warehouses, commercial buildings
Construction contractors (PWR electrical, PRIM specialty, ACM design-build) see backlog grow
Revenue acceleration as projects move from groundbreaking to active construction phase
Contractor stocks re-rate 3-6 months on improving backlog + margin expansion
source_reference: Census Bureau construction spending; CHIPS Act reshoring; data center capex wave

name: FRED Real Disposable Income Trough → Long Staples (XLP) as Safe Haven
thesis: FRED DSPIC96 (real disposable personal income, monthly) measures actual purchasing power after taxes and inflation. When DSPIC96 YoY turns negative (consumers getting poorer in real terms), staples outperform because people trade down from discretionary to essentials. Long XLP for 12 months from the first negative YoY month. The real income squeeze leads consumer behavior change by 1-2 months — people stop dining out and buying electronics before the data shows it.
causal_chain: FRED DSPIC96 (real disposable income) YoY turns negative for first time in the cycle
Consumers are getting poorer in real terms — inflation eroding purchasing power
Behavioral shift: trade down from restaurants/electronics to groceries/household essentials
Staples companies (PG, KO, CL, WMT) see stable or growing volumes while discretionary suffers
XLP outperforms XLY/SPY over 6-12 months during the real income squeeze
source_reference: BEA Personal Income; consumer trade-down behavior research

name: FRED Advance Retail Sales MoM Surprise Streak → Long XRT
thesis: FRED RSAFS (advance retail sales, monthly) is released mid-month and is the first hard data on consumer spending. When advance retail sales beats the prior month's level for 3 consecutive months (positive MoM streak), it signals sustained consumer momentum. Long XRT (retail ETF) for 60 days. The 3-month streak filters out one-off bounces and confirms trend. XRT captures broad retail exposure (AMZN, HD, TJX, ROST) rather than picking individual names.
causal_chain: FRED RSAFS (advance retail sales) posts positive MoM for 3 consecutive months
Sustained positive streak = consumer spending momentum confirmed, not a one-off bounce
Retail companies see same-store sales accelerate as spending broadens
Analyst revenue estimates get revised upward across the retail sector
XRT re-rates 5-15% over 60 days as earnings expectations improve
source_reference: Census Bureau advance retail sales; retail earnings cycle

name: FRED Mortgage Application Index Surge → Long Homebuilders 3mo Lag
thesis: FRED MORTGAGE30US tracks mortgage rates, but the MBA Mortgage Application Index (FRED MORTAPPW, weekly) tracks actual purchase applications — a direct demand signal. When purchase applications surge >20% from trailing 13-week low, housing demand is inflecting. Long LEN+DHI+NVR for 6 months with a 1-month lag (applications → closings → builder revenue takes 60-90 days). Distinct from PL041 (existing home supply) and PL070 (new home supply spike) — this is a DEMAND signal, not a supply signal.
causal_chain: FRED mortgage application index surges >20% from 13-week trailing low
Purchase applications = buyers actively seeking homes — direct demand proxy
Applications lead closings by 45-60 days → builder contract signings accelerate
Homebuilders (LEN, DHI, NVR) see net new orders inflect 1-2 quarters later
Stocks re-rate 3-6 months as order books and backlog grow
data_sources: FRED MORTAPPW or MBA purchase index (weekly, free if available)
source_reference: MBA Weekly Applications Survey; Census new home sales; builder earnings cycle

name: BDTI Tanker Rate Spike → Long Crude Oil Tanker Equities
thesis: The Baltic Dirty Tanker Index (BDTI) reflects spot charter rates for crude oil tankers. When BDTI spikes >40% in 2 weeks (geopolitical disruption, ton-mile increase from rerouting), tanker equities (FRO, STNG, INSW) lag by 3-7 days as earnings estimates adjust upward. FRED series DISCONTINUED but yfinance has tanker stocks; Clarksons data proxied via FRO earnings correlation.
causal_chain: Geopolitical event or refinery maintenance shift increases crude ton-mile demand
BDTI spikes >40% in 2-week window as spot charter rates surge
Tanker equities (FRO, STNG, INSW) initially lag — market treats spike as transitory
Within 5-10 days, sell-side upgrades earnings estimates for tanker operators
Tanker stocks rally 8-15% over next 2-4 weeks as time-charter equivalent reprices
data_sources: Baltic Exchange BDTI (proxied via tanker ETF flows)
source_reference: Baltic Exchange; Clarksons Shipping Intelligence; Fearnleys tanker market reports

name: FRED SLOOS Credit Card Willingness → Long Consumer Discretionary
thesis: The Fed's SLOOS survey includes net percentage of banks willing to make consumer installment loans (FRED: DRTSCLCC). When willingness shifts from tightening to easing (crosses zero from negative), consumer discretionary (XLY) outperforms SPY by 3-5% over the following quarter as credit availability flows into spending. This is a leading indicator of consumer credit expansion.
causal_chain: Fed SLOOS survey shows banks shifting from tightening to easing on consumer installment loans
FRED DRTSCLCC crosses from negative to positive territory
Consumer credit availability expands with 1-2 month lag (FRED TOTALSL accelerates)
Retail sales and consumer confidence improve as credit flows into spending
XLY outperforms SPY by 3-5% over the following quarter
data_sources: FRED DRTSCLCC (Senior Loan Officer Survey, consumer installment willingness)
source_reference: Federal Reserve SLOOS; Haver Analytics consumer credit research

name: USDA Weekly Crop Progress Corn Silking Delay → Long Corn Futures
thesis: USDA publishes weekly Crop Progress reports (free). Corn silking is a critical growth stage vulnerable to heat stress. When the % of corn crop that has silked lags the 5-year average by >10 percentage points in mid-July, it signals delayed pollination and yield risk. Corn futures (ZC=F) rally 5-10% over 2-4 weeks as yield estimates are revised downward.
causal_chain: USDA Weekly Crop Progress shows corn silking lagging 5-year avg by >10pp in mid-July
Delayed silking means pollination coincides with peak August heat, increasing kernel abortion risk
USDA lowers yield estimate in next WASDE report (typically August)
Corn futures (ZC=F) rally 5-10% as ending stocks projection drops
Downstream soybean meal and feed costs also reprice upward
data_sources: USDA Weekly Crop Progress (free, weekly during growing season)
source_reference: USDA NASS Crop Progress; Pro Farmer Midwest Crop Tour methodology

name: FRED JTSJOL Job Openings Rate Decline → Long Staffing Firms Recovery
thesis: When JOLTS job openings (FRED JTSJOL) decline >15% from cycle peak, the labor market is rebalancing. Counterintuitively, staffing firms (RHI, HAYS, MAN) rally 10-20% from trough because the decline in openings signals the end of the 'Great Resignation' era — workers stop quitting, temp-to-perm conversion slows, and companies return to staffing agencies for flexible labor.
causal_chain: FRED JTSJOL declines >15% from cycle peak — labor market cooling
Quits rate (FRED JTSQUR) declines in sympathy — workers stop voluntarily leaving
Companies shift from permanent hiring back to temp/contract via staffing agencies
Staffing firm revenue per employee stabilizes then inflects upward
RHI, MAN rally 10-20% from trough as bill rates firm and volume recovers
source_reference: BLS JOLTS; Staffing Industry Analysts (SIA) Pulse Survey

name: FRED DGORDER Durable Goods New Orders Acceleration → Long Semiconductor Equipment
thesis: When FRED DGORDER (new orders for durable goods) shows 3 consecutive months of MoM acceleration, it signals a capex upcycle beginning. Semiconductor equipment makers (AMAT, LRCX, KLAC) lead the capex cycle and rally 10-15% over the next 2 months as fab buildout orders materialize. The durable goods report leads semi equipment revenue by 2-3 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED DGORDER shows 3 consecutive months of MoM acceleration in new orders
Manufacturing capex cycle turns positive — companies replace aging equipment
Semiconductor fabs accelerate tool orders as utilization approaches 85%+
AMAT, LRCX, KLAC book-to-bill ratios improve, guidance revised upward
Semi equipment stocks rally 10-15% over 2 months as cycle turn confirms
source_reference: Census Bureau M3 survey; SEMI World Fab Forecast
