name: EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Injection Below 5yr Avg → Long UNG Pre-Winter
thesis: EIA publishes weekly natural gas storage reports (free). When summer injection season (Apr-Oct) running total falls below the 5-year average by >200 Bcf by August, winter supply is at risk. Natural gas futures (NG=F) rally 15-30% from August through November as the market prices in potential winter tightness. This differs from existing natgas_storage_deficit_winter by focusing on summer injection shortfall specifically.
causal_chain: EIA weekly storage report shows cumulative summer injections lagging 5yr avg by >200 Bcf
Low injection rate signals either higher-than-expected summer cooling demand or production decline
By August, market calculates probability of sub-3.5 Tcf storage entering winter
Natural gas futures (NG=F) enter backwardation as prompt month premium builds
NG=F rallies 15-30% Aug-Nov as heating season approaches with tight inventory
data_sources: EIA Weekly Natural Gas Storage Report (free)
source_reference: EIA Natural Gas Weekly Update; NOAA heating degree day forecasts

name: FRED PERMIT Single-Family Permits 3mo Acceleration → Long Homebuilder Suppliers
thesis: When single-family building permits (FRED PERMIT1) show 3 consecutive months of YoY improvement (even if still negative YoY), it signals the housing cycle trough. Homebuilder material suppliers (SHW, VMC, MLM, BLDR) outperform by 8-12% over the next quarter as they benefit from the volume inflection before homebuilders themselves reprice.
causal_chain: FRED PERMIT1 (single-family permits) shows 3mo trend of YoY rate of change improving
Rate of change improvement signals housing cycle trough even before permits go positive YoY
Material suppliers see orders inflect before revenue shows in quarterly earnings
SHW (paint), VMC/MLM (aggregates), BLDR (building products) lead the recovery
Supplier stocks outperform XHB by 8-12% over following quarter as volume recovers
data_sources: yfinance SHW, VMC, MLM, BLDR, XHB
source_reference: Census Bureau New Residential Construction; NAHB/Wells Fargo Housing Market Index

name: FRED TWEXBMTH Broad Trade-Weighted Dollar 6mo Decline → Long EM Equity (EEM)
thesis: When the FRED Broad Trade-Weighted Dollar Index (TWEXBMTH) declines >5% over 6 months, emerging market equities rally as dollar-denominated debt burdens ease, commodity revenues increase in USD terms, and portfolio flows reverse toward EM. EEM outperforms SPY by 5-10% over the following quarter.
causal_chain: FRED TWEXBMTH (broad trade-weighted dollar) declines >5% over 6-month window
EM sovereign and corporate dollar debt burden eases — reduced FX mismatch stress
Commodity exporters (Brazil, Chile, Indonesia) see revenue increase in USD terms
Global portfolio flows rotate from US to EM as carry trade becomes attractive
EEM outperforms SPY by 5-10% over following quarter
source_reference: Federal Reserve Trade-Weighted Dollar Index; IIF Capital Flows Tracker

name: FRED TLRESCONS Total Residential Construction Spending Inflection → Long Plumbing/HVAC Distributors
thesis: When FRED TLRESCONS (total residential construction spending) shows 3 consecutive months of MoM growth after a decline period, plumbing/HVAC distributors (WSO, WMS, SWK) are early beneficiaries as they sit upstream in the supply chain. These mid-cap names rally 10-15% over 2 months before large-cap homebuilders fully reprice.
causal_chain: FRED TLRESCONS shows 3 consecutive MoM increases after period of decline
Residential construction spending inflection signals new project starts ramping
Plumbing/HVAC distributors (WSO, WMS) see order books fill 4-6 weeks before revenue recognition
Distributor stocks rally 10-15% over 2 months as channel checks confirm volume recovery
Homebuilder stocks follow with 1-2 month lag as completions data confirms
source_reference: Census Bureau Construction Spending Survey (C30); PHCC industry reports

name: FRED WTREGEN Treasury General Account Rebuild → Long Short-Duration Bonds
thesis: When the Treasury General Account (FRED WTREGEN) rebuilds rapidly (>$100B in 4 weeks) after a debt ceiling resolution, it drains reserves from the banking system. This tightens short-term funding conditions, pushing T-bill yields higher and creating a buying opportunity in short-duration bond ETFs (SHV, BIL) once the drain stabilizes. Differs from TGA drain ideas by focusing on the rebuild phase.
causal_chain: Debt ceiling resolved — Treasury begins rebuilding TGA (FRED WTREGEN) via T-bill issuance
TGA rebuild >$100B in 4 weeks drains bank reserves (FRED WRESBAL declines)
Short-term funding rates (SOFR, repo) rise 10-25bps above IORB target
T-bill yields overshoot as supply surge meets constrained demand
Once TGA stabilizes, T-bill yields normalize — long SHV/BIL at peak yield spread
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.4.1 release; Treasury DTS (Daily Treasury Statement)

name: FRED TOTCI Total Commercial & Industrial Loans YoY Trough → Long Regional Banks
thesis: When C&I loan growth (FRED TOTCI, YoY change) troughs and begins to recover after a contraction period, regional banks (KRE) outperform as net interest income prospects improve. The trough in C&I loan growth has historically led regional bank equity recovery by 1-2 quarters. Different from existing ci_loan_growth_inflection_banks by using the specific trough detection on TOTCI rather than H.8 data.
causal_chain: FRED TOTCI (total C&I loans) YoY change reaches trough and turns upward for 2 consecutive weeks
Loan demand recovery signals improving business confidence and capex intentions
Regional banks see loan pipeline improve — net interest income outlook brightens
Sell-side upgrades NII estimates for regional banks with high C&I concentration
KRE rallies 10-20% over following quarter as earnings estimates inflect
data_sources: FRED TOTCI (total C&I loans at commercial banks)
FRED BUSLOANS (C&I loans, all commercial banks)
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.8 release; FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile

name: FRED FRGSHPUSM649NCIS Real Government Spending Acceleration → Long Defense Primes
thesis: When real federal government consumption expenditures (FRED FGCE) accelerate for 2 consecutive quarters, defense prime contractors (LMT, RTX, NOC, GD) rally as the spending acceleration flows through procurement pipelines. This is distinct from defense budget signals — it captures actual disbursement acceleration, not authorization.
causal_chain: FRED FGCE (federal government consumption expenditures) accelerates 2 consecutive quarters
Actual disbursement acceleration means procurement pipeline is converting to revenue
Defense primes (LMT, RTX, NOC, GD) see backlog-to-revenue conversion improve
Quarterly earnings beats as revenue recognition accelerates ahead of estimates
Defense primes outperform SPY by 5-8% over following quarter
data_sources: yfinance LMT, RTX, NOC, GD, ITA
source_reference: BEA GDP release; DoD Comptroller Green Book

name: NOAA Palmer Drought Severity Index Extreme → Long Water Utilities
thesis: When the Palmer Drought Severity Index (PDSI) hits extreme drought (<-4) across multiple Western US states, water utilities (AWK, WTR, SJW) outperform as municipalities accelerate infrastructure investment, conservation surcharges boost revenue per gallon, and regulated rate cases gain easier approval from sympathetic PUCs.
causal_chain: NOAA PDSI hits extreme drought (<-4) across 3+ Western states (CA, CO, AZ, NV)
Municipalities declare water emergencies, accelerate infrastructure investment
Water utilities implement conservation surcharges — revenue per gallon increases
State PUCs expedite rate case approvals given infrastructure emergency framing
AWK, WTR, SJW outperform XLU by 5-10% over 3 months during drought events
source_reference: NOAA Climate Prediction Center; American Water Works Association annual survey

name: USDA World Ag Supply & Demand (WASDE) Soybean Ending Stocks Surprise → Long Soybean Futures
thesis: The USDA WASDE report is published monthly (free, 12pm ET). When US soybean ending stocks come in >50M bushels below the pre-report average trade estimate, soybean futures (ZS=F) rally 3-7% over the following 5 trading days as the market reprices the tighter supply/demand balance. The key is the surprise magnitude relative to consensus.
causal_chain: USDA WASDE published monthly at 12pm ET (free at usda.gov)
US soybean ending stocks reported >50M bushels below pre-report trade estimate
Tighter-than-expected S&D balance triggers short covering in soybean futures
ZS=F rallies 3-7% over next 5 trading days as new supply/demand equilibrium found
Soybean meal (ZM=F) and soybean oil (ZL=F) rally in sympathy
source_reference: USDA WASDE; CME Group pre-report trade estimates

name: FRED PCEPILFE Core PCE 3mo Annualized Below 2% → Long Growth/Tech (QQQ)
thesis: When Core PCE (FRED PCEPILFE) on a 3-month annualized basis drops below 2.0% for the first time in a tightening cycle, it signals the Fed's inflation target is within reach. Growth/tech stocks (QQQ) outperform value (VTV) by 8-12% over the following 2 months as the market prices in rate cuts and long-duration asset valuations expand.
causal_chain: FRED PCEPILFE (Core PCE) 3mo annualized rate falls below 2.0% for first time in cycle
Market reprices Fed rate path — terminal rate expectations decline 50-75bps
Real yields decline as nominal rate expectations fall faster than inflation
Long-duration assets (growth/tech) benefit most from rate repricing
QQQ outperforms VTV by 8-12% over following 2 months
source_reference: BEA Personal Income and Outlays release; Fed minutes

name: FRED RAILFRGCARLOADSD11 Railroad Carloads Chemical Group Surge → Long Specialty Chemical Producers
thesis: AAR weekly railroad data includes carloads by commodity group. When chemical carloads (a subset of FRED RAILFRGCARLOADSD11) surge >10% YoY for 3 consecutive weeks, it signals industrial chemical demand recovery. Specialty chemical producers (DD, LYB, CE, EMN) rally 8-12% over the following 2 months as volume recovery flows into earnings.
causal_chain: AAR weekly railroad data shows chemical carloads surging >10% YoY for 3 weeks
Chemical shipment surge signals manufacturing/industrial demand recovery
Specialty chem producers (DD, LYB, CE, EMN) see volume and pricing improve
Quarterly earnings beats as operating leverage amplifies revenue growth
Specialty chem stocks rally 8-12% over 2 months
source_reference: Association of American Railroads (AAR) weekly reports; American Chemistry Council

name: FRED ISRATIO Total Business Inventory/Sales Ratio Decline → Long Small Cap Value (IWN)
thesis: When the total business inventory-to-sales ratio (FRED ISRATIO) declines for 3 consecutive months from elevated levels (>1.40), it signals businesses have worked through excess inventory. Small cap value (IWN) benefits disproportionately as these companies have higher operating leverage and the inventory destocking-to-restocking transition boosts earnings most for smaller firms.
causal_chain: FRED ISRATIO declines for 3 consecutive months from elevated >1.40 level
Inventory destocking cycle ending — businesses begin restocking
Small manufacturers and distributors see orders inflect first
Higher operating leverage means EPS growth is amplified for small caps
IWN outperforms IWF by 5-10% over following quarter as restocking cycle begins
source_reference: Census Bureau MTIS (Manufacturing and Trade Inventories and Sales)

name: FRED CPIAUCSL CPI Monthly Print Below Consensus → Long TLT Same-Day
thesis: When the monthly CPI print (FRED CPIAUCSL, MoM change) comes in >0.1pp below Bloomberg consensus, 20+ year Treasuries (TLT) rally 1-3% on the day and follow through over 3-5 days. The magnitude of the move is proportional to the miss size. This is a pure event-driven signal with high backtestability using CPI release dates and consensus estimates.
causal_chain: BLS CPI released at 8:30am ET — MoM print misses consensus by >0.1pp to downside
Bond market immediately reprices rate path — 2yr yield drops 10-20bps intraday
Fed funds futures shift to price in additional 25bp cut within 6 months
TLT rallies 1-3% on the day as duration premium declines
Follow-through over 3-5 days as mutual funds and pension reallocate to duration
data_sources: FRED CPIAUCSL (CPI for all urban consumers)
source_reference: BLS CPI release calendar; Bloomberg consensus estimates (historical available via FRED)

name: FRED BOGZ1FL073164003Q Household Net Worth to GDP Trough → Long SPY Multi-Month
thesis: Household net worth as % of GDP (from Fed Z.1 Financial Accounts, FRED BOGZ1FL073164003Q or proxy via TNWBSHNO/GDP) troughing after a decline signals the wealth effect bottoming. When this ratio shows 2 consecutive quarterly increases after a decline, consumer spending and equity markets rally as the wealth effect turns positive. This is a slow-moving but high-conviction signal.
causal_chain: Fed Z.1 Flow of Funds shows household net worth (FRED TNWBSHNO) troughing relative to GDP
Wealth effect bottoms — household balance sheets begin recovering
Consumer spending confidence improves as net worth stabilizes
Equity markets benefit from both improved fundamentals and portfolio rebalancing
SPY rallies 8-15% over following 2 quarters from the net worth trough signal
source_reference: Federal Reserve Z.1 Financial Accounts; Mian & Sufi wealth effect research

name: FRED REVOLSL Revolving Consumer Credit Deceleration → Long XLP Defensive Rotation
thesis: When revolving consumer credit (FRED REVOLSL, primarily credit cards) growth decelerates sharply (YoY drops by >3pp in 2 months), it signals consumer stress. Consumer staples (XLP) outperform consumer discretionary (XLY) by 5-8% over the following 2 months as the market rotates defensively. This is distinct from existing consumer credit ideas which focus on acceleration.
causal_chain: FRED REVOLSL (revolving credit) YoY growth decelerates by >3pp in 2 months
Sharp deceleration signals either tightening credit standards or consumer pullback
Discretionary spending weakens as credit-dependent purchases decline
Market rotates from XLY to XLP as defensive positioning increases
XLP outperforms XLY by 5-8% over following 2 months
source_reference: Federal Reserve G.19 Consumer Credit release

name: FRED PPIACO Producer Price Index Crude Materials Surge → Long Commodity Miners (XME)
thesis: When the PPI for crude materials (FRED PPICRM) surges >5% MoM, it signals a broad commodity demand shock. Mining and metals ETF (XME) rallies with a 2-4 week lag as realized commodity price increases flow through to miner revenue and margin expansion. The PPI crude materials index captures the broad commodity complex better than individual commodity prices.
causal_chain: FRED PPICRM (PPI crude materials) surges >5% MoM — broad commodity demand shock
Crude material price increase signals either supply disruption or demand surge
Mining companies see realized prices increase with 2-4 week lag (contract repricing)
Operating margins expand significantly due to high operating leverage in mining
XME rallies 8-15% over following month as revenue/margin benefits materialize
data_sources: FRED PPICRM (PPI crude materials for further processing)
source_reference: BLS Producer Price Index news release; mining company quarterly reports

name: FRED IPMAN Manufacturing IP 3mo Rate of Change Inflection → Long Factory Automation (ISRG, ROK, AME)
thesis: When manufacturing industrial production (FRED IPMAN) 3-month rate of change turns positive after being negative for 3+ months, it signals a manufacturing recovery. Factory automation and industrial technology companies (ROK, AME, EMR) rally 10-15% as capex budgets reopen. Distinct from existing ipman_recovery_factory_automation by using a specific 3mo RoC inflection trigger.
causal_chain: FRED IPMAN 3-month rate of change crosses zero from below after 3+ months negative
Manufacturing recovery confirmed — capacity utilization begins to rise
Factory operators restart delayed capex projects — automation orders inflect
ROK, AME, EMR see order intake improve within 1-2 months
Industrial automation stocks rally 10-15% over 2 months as cycle turn confirmed
source_reference: Federal Reserve G.17 Industrial Production report

name: FRED DSPIC96 Real Disposable Personal Income 3mo Acceleration → Long Luxury/Premium Retail
thesis: When real disposable personal income (FRED DSPIC96) shows 3 consecutive months of acceleration (2nd derivative positive), luxury and premium retailers (RL, TPR, CPRI, RH) outperform mass retail by 8-12% over the following quarter. Rising real income accelerates spending on aspirational goods before mass-market indicators catch up.
causal_chain: FRED DSPIC96 (real DPI) shows 3 months of accelerating MoM growth (positive 2nd derivative)
Rising real incomes boost consumer purchasing power — aspirational spending first to benefit
Luxury/premium retail (RL, TPR, RH) see same-store sales improve ahead of guidance
Sell-side upgrades come with 4-6 week lag as channel checks confirm trend
Premium retail stocks outperform XRT by 8-12% over following quarter
data_sources: FRED W875RX1 (real personal income ex transfers)
yfinance RL, TPR, CPRI, RH, XRT
source_reference: BEA Personal Income and Outlays; NRF retail monitor

name: FRED AWHMAN Average Weekly Hours Manufacturing Inflection → Long Temp Staffing Agencies
thesis: Average weekly hours in manufacturing (FRED AWHMAN) is the earliest labor market indicator — firms increase hours before hiring. When AWHMAN rises for 3 consecutive months after a decline, temporary staffing agencies (RHI, ASGN, KFY) rally 10-15% as hours growth signals impending hiring, which flows through temp agencies first before permanent hires.
causal_chain: FRED AWHMAN rises for 3 consecutive months after period of decline
Firms extending hours = demand recovering, but uncertain about permanent hiring
Next step in labor demand sequence: temporary/contract workers before permanent FTEs
Temp staffing agencies (RHI, ASGN, KFY) see order volumes inflect upward
Staffing stocks rally 10-15% over 2 months as revenue growth accelerates
source_reference: BLS Employment Situation Report; SIA staffing industry reports

name: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Gasoline Days of Supply <22 → Long Refiner Margins (VLO, MPC)
thesis: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report (free) includes gasoline days of supply. When days of supply drops below 22 (tight threshold) during summer driving season (May-Sept), refiner margins (crack spreads) surge and refiners (VLO, MPC, PSX) rally 8-15% over the following month as gasoline prices spike. This is a pure supply tightness signal.
causal_chain: EIA weekly report shows gasoline days of supply drops below 22 during summer
Tight supply signals refinery capacity constraints or unexpected demand surge
Gasoline crack spread (RBOB - WTI) widens by $5-10/bbl
Refiners (VLO, MPC, PSX) see per-barrel margins surge — operating leverage kicks in
Refiner stocks rally 8-15% over following month as margin expansion flows to earnings
data_sources: FRED GASDESW (gasoline days of supply estimate)
source_reference: EIA Weekly Petroleum Status Report; Baker Hughes refinery utilization data

name: FRED DCOILWTICO WTI Crude Below $50 for 3 Months → Long E&P Recovery (XOP) Contrarian
thesis: When WTI crude (FRED DCOILWTICO) trades below $50/bbl for 3+ consecutive months, it signals maximum pessimism in energy. E&P companies (XOP constituents) are priced for bankruptcy but survivors benefit from supply destruction (competitors shut in wells). When crude bounces >15% from trough, XOP rallies 20-40% as the market reprices survivor earnings.
causal_chain: FRED DCOILWTICO below $50/bbl for 3+ consecutive months — maximum bearish sentiment
Marginal producers shut in wells — US rig count declines (Baker Hughes)
Supply destruction creates conditions for future price recovery
WTI bounces >15% from trough on OPEC cut or demand recovery signal
XOP rallies 20-40% from trough as survivors are repriced with improved margins
source_reference: Baker Hughes Rig Count; EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook

name: FRED MSPUS Median Home Sale Price YoY Deceleration → Long Mortgage REITs (NLY, AGNC)
thesis: When median existing home sale price (FRED MSPUS) YoY growth decelerates from >10% to <3% over 2 quarters, the Fed is likely at or near peak rates. Mortgage REITs (NLY, AGNC) benefit because: (1) rate volatility declines improving hedging costs, (2) mortgage spreads normalize, and (3) book value stabilizes after MBS mark-to-market losses. NLY/AGNC rally 15-25% from trough.
causal_chain: FRED MSPUS YoY growth decelerates from >10% to <3% over 2 quarters
Housing price deceleration signals Fed tightening is working — peak rates near
Rate volatility (MOVE index) declines — mortgage REIT hedging costs fall
MBS spreads normalize — agency mortgage REITs see book value stabilize
NLY, AGNC rally 15-25% from trough as dividend sustainability improves
data_sources: FRED MSPUS (median sales price existing homes)
source_reference: NAR Existing Home Sales; Federal Reserve financial stability reports

name: FRED EXPGS Real Exports of Goods & Services Recovery → Long Industrial Exporters (CAT, DE, CMI)
thesis: When real exports of goods & services (FRED EXPGS) show 2 consecutive quarters of acceleration after a downturn, US industrial exporters (CAT, DE, CMI) rally as the export recovery signals global demand improvement. These companies have 40-60% international revenue exposure and benefit from both volume recovery and weaker dollar tailwinds.
causal_chain: FRED EXPGS shows 2 consecutive quarters of real export growth acceleration
Global demand improving — emerging markets and Europe recovering
US industrial exporters (CAT, DE, CMI) see international order books strengthen
Quarterly earnings beats on international revenue line items
Industrial exporter stocks outperform SPY by 5-10% over following quarter
data_sources: FRED EXPGS (real exports of goods & services)
source_reference: BEA GDP release; Census Bureau FT-900 trade report

name: FRED DTBSPCKFR 5yr Breakeven Inflation Rate Rise Above 2.5% → Long TIPS ETF (TIP)
thesis: When the 5yr breakeven inflation rate (FRED T5YIE) rises above 2.5% and sustains for 2+ weeks, it signals the market is pricing in persistent above-target inflation. TIPS (TIP ETF) outperform nominal Treasuries (IEF) as the inflation premium embedded in TIPS becomes more valuable. The 5yr breakeven is more responsive than the 10yr to near-term inflation shifts.
causal_chain: FRED T5YIE (5yr breakeven inflation) rises above 2.5% and sustains for 2+ weeks
Market pricing persistent above-target inflation — TIPS demand increases
Institutional investors shift from nominal to inflation-protected allocation
TIP outperforms IEF by 2-4% over following 2 months as inflation premium builds
Real yield compression benefits existing TIPS holders via price appreciation
source_reference: Federal Reserve TIPS yield curve; Cleveland Fed inflation expectations

name: FRED USSTHPI US State Housing Price Index Divergence → Long Lagging-State Homebuilders
thesis: When the spread between the fastest-appreciating and slowest-appreciating state HPIs (FRED FHFA state-level series) exceeds 2 standard deviations, the lagging states mean-revert over the following 2 quarters. Homebuilders with heavy exposure to lagging states (e.g., LEN in Florida, MDC in Mountain West) outperform as price appreciation catches up.
causal_chain: FHFA state-level house price indices show divergence exceeding 2 standard deviations
Lagging states typically reflect temporary demand shock or affordability reset
Migration patterns (Census domestic migration data) show people moving to lagging/affordable states
Homebuilders concentrated in lagging states see demand and pricing improve
State-specific homebuilders outperform XHB by 5-10% as HPI convergence begins
data_sources: FRED state-level FHFA HPI series (e.g., FLSTHPI, TXSTHPI, AZSTHPI)
source_reference: FHFA House Price Index; Census Bureau Population Estimates domestic migration

name: FRED USNIM Net Interest Margin Recovery → Long Community Banks (QABA)
thesis: When the aggregate US bank net interest margin (FRED USNIM) bottoms and shows 2 consecutive quarterly increases, community bank ETFs (QABA) outperform KRE because community banks have higher NIM sensitivity (less hedging, more floating-rate assets). QABA rallies 12-20% from NIM trough as smaller banks benefit disproportionately from margin normalization.
causal_chain: FRED USNIM (net interest margin) shows 2 consecutive quarterly increases from trough
NIM recovery signals deposit competition easing and asset repricing catching up
Community banks (QABA constituents) have higher NIM beta than large regionals
Earnings estimates revised upward for community banks as NIM expansion compounds
QABA outperforms KRE by 5-8% over following quarter due to higher NIM leverage
data_sources: FRED USNIM (net interest margin for US banks)
source_reference: FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile; S&P Global community bank NIM analysis

name: FRED NFCI Financial Conditions Easing Streak 8+ Weeks → Long High-Beta Equities (SPHB)
thesis: When the Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index (FRED NFCI) shows easing (negative readings) for 8+ consecutive weeks after a period of tightening, high-beta equities (SPHB) outperform low-volatility (SPLV) by 8-12% over the following 2 months. Sustained financial easing removes the risk premium from high-beta names and encourages risk-on positioning.
causal_chain: FRED NFCI shows negative (easing) readings for 8+ consecutive weeks post-tightening
Sustained financial easing removes systemic risk premium from equity market
Credit spreads tighten, leverage becomes cheaper — benefits high-beta business models
Quant funds and systematic strategies rotate to high-beta factor exposure
SPHB outperforms SPLV by 8-12% over following 2 months
source_reference: Chicago Fed National Financial Conditions Index methodology paper

name: FRED CPIMEDSL Medical Care CPI Acceleration → Long Healthcare Providers (XLV)
thesis: When the CPI for medical care services (FRED CPIMEDSL) shows YoY acceleration of >1pp in 3 months, healthcare providers (hospitals, insurers, pharma distributors) benefit from pricing power expansion. XLV and specifically hospital operators (HCA, THC, UHS) rally as revenue per visit increases while cost structures adjust more slowly.
causal_chain: FRED CPIMEDSL (medical care services CPI) YoY accelerates by >1pp in 3 months
Healthcare service price inflation signals improved provider pricing power
Hospital operators (HCA, THC, UHS) see revenue per admission increase
Managed care reimbursement rates follow CPI medical care with 6-12 month lag
Healthcare providers outperform SPY by 5-8% as pricing power flows to margins
source_reference: BLS CPI medical care component; CMS National Health Expenditure data

name: FRED WPUSI019011 PPI Softwood Lumber Collapse → Long Lumber-Exposed Homebuilders After Trough
thesis: When softwood lumber PPI (FRED WPU0811) drops >30% from peak, it signals a massive input cost tailwind for homebuilders. Builders already sold homes at higher prices (backlog) but now build at lower material costs. LEN, DHI, PHM see gross margin expansion of 200-400bps in the quarter following the lumber price trough.
causal_chain: FRED WPU0811 (PPI softwood lumber) drops >30% from cycle peak
Lumber price collapse reduces framing cost per home by $8,000-15,000
Homebuilders have backlog sold at higher ASP — cost decline = margin expansion
Gross margins expand 200-400bps in quarter following lumber trough
LEN, DHI, PHM rally 10-15% as margin expansion becomes visible in earnings
data_sources: FRED WPU081 (PPI lumber and wood products)
source_reference: BLS PPI lumber series; Random Lengths lumber pricing; NAHB cost surveys

name: FRED PCOFFOTM Coffee (Other Mild) Price Spike → Long Coffee Chain Hedgers (SBUX)
thesis: When the ICO coffee price (FRED PCOFFOTM, Other Mild Arabicas) spikes >40% in 3 months, the market reflexively sells coffee chain stocks (SBUX, DNKN). However, major chains hedge 12-18 months forward. After the initial selloff (-5 to -10%), SBUX recovers and outperforms because hedged costs are locked in while competitors (indie cafes) face immediate margin pressure, driving market share gains.
causal_chain: FRED PCOFFOTM spikes >40% in 3-month window (frost, drought, or supply shock)
Market reflexively sells SBUX, expecting margin compression
SBUX initial selloff of -5 to -10% as headline risk dominates
SBUX hedging program locks in costs 12-18 months forward — actual impact minimal
Independent cafes face immediate cost pressure — SBUX gains market share
SBUX recovers within 4-8 weeks as next earnings shows hedged COGS stable
data_sources: FRED PCOFFOTM (ICO coffee price, other mild arabicas)
source_reference: ICO Coffee Market Report; SBUX 10-K commodity hedging disclosures

name: FRED PNRGINDEXM IMF Energy Price Index Trough → Long Emerging Market Oil Exporters (ERUS, EWZ)
thesis: When the IMF Energy Price Index (FRED PNRGINDEXM) troughs and rises >15% from its low over 2 months, EM oil-exporting country ETFs (EWZ Brazil, EWS Saudi proxy, ERUS Russia when available) rally sharply as fiscal balances improve, currencies strengthen, and sovereign credit spreads tighten. EM energy exporters have 2-3x the beta to energy price recovery vs. XLE.
causal_chain: FRED PNRGINDEXM (IMF energy price index) rises >15% from trough over 2 months
Oil-exporting EM fiscal balances improve — budget deficit narrows
EM oil-exporter currencies strengthen vs USD as terms of trade improve
Sovereign credit spreads tighten — capital inflows resume
EM oil-exporter equity ETFs rally 15-30% from trough, outperforming XLE by 2-3x
source_reference: IMF Primary Commodity Prices; World Bank Commodity Markets Outlook

name: FRED UMTMNO Manufacturers Unfilled Orders Surge → Long Aerospace/Defense Backlog Plays
thesis: When manufacturers' unfilled orders (FRED UMTMNO) for durable goods rise >5% YoY for 3 consecutive months, it signals an extended production cycle with pricing power. Aerospace & defense companies (BA, GE Aerospace, HWM, TDG) with multi-year backlogs benefit most as unfilled orders translate to guaranteed future revenue at locked-in prices.
causal_chain: FRED UMTMNO (unfilled orders for durable goods) rises >5% YoY for 3 months
Growing backlog signals demand exceeding production capacity
Aerospace/defense companies (longest backlog durations) have most revenue visibility
Production rate increases boost operating leverage — fixed costs spread over more units
BA, HWM, TDG rally 8-12% as backlog-to-revenue conversion accelerates
data_sources: yfinance BA, HWM, TDG, GE, ITA
source_reference: Census Bureau M3 Manufacturers' Shipments, Inventories, and Orders

name: FRED BOPTIMP Petroleum Import Value Decline → Long Transportation Margins (UAL, DAL, FDX)
thesis: When the value of US petroleum imports (FRED BOPTIMP or IMP1000) declines >20% YoY, it reflects lower fuel costs for transportation companies. Airlines (UAL, DAL) and freight carriers (FDX, UPS) see operating margins expand 200-400bps as fuel is 20-35% of operating costs. The import value captures both price and volume declines, a broader signal than crude price alone.
causal_chain: FRED IMP1000 (petroleum product imports value) declines >20% YoY
Lower petroleum import values reflect declining fuel costs for US businesses
Airlines (fuel = 25-35% of costs) and freight (fuel = 20-30%) see margin expansion
Operating margins expand 200-400bps — earnings estimates revised upward
UAL, DAL, FDX outperform SPY by 5-10% over following 2 months
data_sources: yfinance UAL, DAL, FDX, UPS, JETS
source_reference: Census Bureau FT-900 Trade Report; BTS airline fuel cost data

name: FRED MVPHPC Motor Vehicle Parts Domestic Production Recovery → Long Auto Parts Retailers (AAP, ORLY, AZO)
thesis: When domestic motor vehicle parts production (FRED MVPHPC or proxy via IPNMAT) recovers after a decline, aftermarket auto parts retailers (ORLY, AZO, AAP) benefit as parts availability improves and fleet age remains high. The parts production recovery signals supply chain normalization that enables retailers to restock and meet pent-up demand.
causal_chain: FRED auto parts IP index shows recovery — 3 consecutive months of MoM growth
Parts supply chain normalization enables aftermarket retailers to restock shelves
US fleet age remains at record highs (>12.5 years) — sustained aftermarket demand
ORLY, AZO see same-store sales accelerate as parts availability matches demand
Auto parts retailers rally 8-12% as restocking cycle drives revenue growth
data_sources: FRED IPNMAT (IP: motor vehicles and parts)
source_reference: Federal Reserve G.17 Industrial Production; BLS auto parts employment data

name: FRED LFWA25T54 Prime-Age Labor Force Participation Rise → Long Staffing & HR Tech (WDAY, PAYC)
thesis: When prime-age (25-54) labor force participation rate (FRED LNS11300060) rises >0.3pp in 3 months, it signals workers re-entering the labor force. HR tech and payroll companies (WDAY, PAYC, ADP) benefit as more employment means more payroll processing, benefits administration, and HCM software seats. This is a volume signal for HR tech revenue growth.
causal_chain: FRED LNS11300060 (prime-age LFPR) rises >0.3pp in 3-month window
Workers re-entering labor force = new hires across economy
Each new hire generates revenue for payroll processors (ADP, PAYC) and HCM platforms (WDAY)
HR tech revenue is largely per-employee — more workers = more seats = more revenue
WDAY, PAYC, ADP outperform SPY by 5-8% as employment growth drives platform revenue
source_reference: BLS Employment Situation Report; ADP National Employment Report

name: FRED FEDMINNFRWG Minimum Wage Increase Announcement → Long Dollar Stores (DLTR, DG)
thesis: When federal or major-state minimum wage increases are enacted (tracked via FRED FEDMINNFRWG and state DOL announcements), dollar stores (DLTR, DG) actually benefit more than they're hurt. While labor costs rise modestly (already near-minimum-wage workforce), their core customers see income increases, driving traffic and basket size. Dollar stores outperform after minimum wage hikes.
causal_chain: Federal or major-state minimum wage increase takes effect (FRED FEDMINNFRWG)
Low-income households (dollar store core customer) see disposable income increase
Dollar store traffic and basket size increase within 1-2 months of wage hike
Labor cost increase is modest — dollar stores already use lean staffing models
DLTR, DG outperform XRT by 5-8% in 3 months following wage hike implementation
source_reference: BLS Minimum Wage Workers report; NRF retail spending analysis

name: FRED PSAVERT Personal Savings Rate Spike Above 8% → Long Discretionary Spending Recovery 6mo Out
thesis: When the personal savings rate (FRED PSAVERT) spikes above 8% (indicating forced or precautionary saving), it creates pent-up demand that unwinds 4-6 months later. XLY (consumer discretionary) outperforms SPY by 8-12% in the 6 months following the savings rate spike as consumers deploy accumulated savings. The higher the spike, the stronger the subsequent spending boom.
causal_chain: FRED PSAVERT spikes above 8% — precautionary saving surge (recession fear or lockdown)
Pent-up demand builds as consumers defer discretionary purchases
Savings rate begins normalizing — accumulated savings deployed into spending
Consumer discretionary spending accelerates 4-6 months after savings rate peak
XLY outperforms SPY by 8-12% in the 6 months following the savings rate spike
source_reference: BEA Personal Income and Outlays; Federal Reserve SCF analysis of saving behavior

name: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 High Yield Spread Compression Below 350bps → Long Equity Risk-On (SPHB)
thesis: When the BofA High Yield OAS (FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2) compresses below 350bps after being above 500bps, it signals a credit cycle recovery. Equity risk-on strategies benefit as the same credit improvement that compresses HY spreads also lifts equity valuations. SPHB (high-beta S&P 500) outperforms SPLV by 10-15% in the 2 months following the spread compression breakthrough.
causal_chain: FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 (HY OAS) drops below 350bps after being >500bps
Credit cycle recovery confirmed — default risk repriced lower
Corporate refinancing conditions improve — balance sheet stress declines
Equity risk premium declines in sympathy with credit spread compression
SPHB outperforms SPLV by 10-15% over 2 months as risk-on regime established
source_reference: BofA Global Research; Fed Financial Stability Report

name: FRED AAA10Y Moody's AAA Corporate Bond Spread to 10yr Trough → Long Investment-Grade Corps (LQD)
thesis: When the Moody's AAA corporate bond spread (FRED AAA10Y) reaches a trough and begins widening by >20bps over 4 weeks, it signals credit conditions beginning to tighten. However, the initial widening from extremely tight levels is actually a buying opportunity for IG bonds (LQD) because absolute yields become more attractive while credit quality remains high. The yield pickup attracts institutional buyers.
causal_chain: FRED AAA10Y (AAA spread to 10yr) troughs then widens >20bps over 4 weeks
Initial spread widening from tight levels increases absolute yield on IG corporates
Institutional investors (pensions, insurers) increase allocation to IG for yield pickup
LQD sees inflows as yield-hungry institutions buy the cheapening
LQD outperforms TLT by 2-4% over following 2 months as institutional demand supports prices
source_reference: Moody's credit spread data; Fed Flow of Funds insurance company allocations

name: FRED TEDRATE TED Spread Normalization After Spike → Long Regional Bank Recovery (KRE)
thesis: When the TED spread (FRED TEDRATE) spikes >50bps then normalizes back below 30bps within 8 weeks, it signals a funding stress event that has resolved. Regional banks (KRE) recover sharply from the stress-induced selloff as interbank funding conditions normalize. Different from existing ted_spread_normalization_regional_banks by using specific spike-and-normalize pattern rather than just normalization level.
causal_chain: FRED TEDRATE spikes >50bps — interbank funding stress event
Regional bank stocks (KRE) sell off 10-20% on funding concerns
TED spread normalizes back below 30bps within 8 weeks — stress resolved
Interbank market functioning restored — depositors return to regional banks
KRE recovers 15-25% from stress trough as funding cost normalization restores NIM outlook
source_reference: Federal Reserve interbank market analysis; BIS quarterly review

name: FRED RBUSBIS Real Broad Effective Exchange Rate Extreme → Mean Reversion in US Equities
thesis: The BIS real broad effective exchange rate for the USD (FRED RBUSBIS) captures purchasing power parity deviation. When it exceeds 115 (>15% above equilibrium), the dollar is unsustainably strong. Over the following 6 months, the dollar weakens and US multinational equities (with high foreign revenue) outperform domestic-focused names as FX translation tailwinds emerge.
causal_chain: FRED RBUSBIS (BIS real broad effective exchange rate USD) exceeds 115
Dollar is >15% above PPP equilibrium — unsustainably strong
Mean-reversion forces begin: trade deficit widens, Fed potentially eases
Dollar weakens over following 6 months — FX translation tailwind for US multinationals
S&P 500 companies with >50% foreign revenue outperform domestics by 5-10%
data_sources: FRED RBUSBIS (BIS real broad effective exchange rate)
source_reference: BIS Effective Exchange Rate data; IMF External Balance Assessment

name: FRED GFDEBTN Federal Debt to GDP Crossing New High → Long Gold as Fiscal Hedge
thesis: When federal debt to GDP (FRED GFDEGDQ188S) crosses a new all-time high and accelerates (debt growing faster than nominal GDP for 2+ quarters), gold rallies as the market prices in eventual fiscal dominance risk. Gold (GLD) outperforms SPY by 5-10% in the 6 months following new debt/GDP highs as sovereign credit risk premium builds.
causal_chain: FRED GFDEGDQ188S (federal debt to GDP) crosses new all-time high
Debt growing faster than nominal GDP for 2+ consecutive quarters
Market begins pricing fiscal dominance risk — real yields less credible as anchor
Central bank gold purchases accelerate (WGC data) as reserve diversification
Gold (GLD) outperforms SPY by 5-10% in 6 months following new debt/GDP peak
source_reference: Treasury Fiscal Data; CBO Budget and Economic Outlook

name: FRED JPNASSETS Bank of Japan Total Assets Surge → Long Nikkei (EWJ) via Yen Weakness
thesis: When Bank of Japan total assets (FRED JPNASSETS) show accelerating growth (>2% MoM for 3 months), it signals aggressive monetary easing that weakens the yen. Yen weakness is a direct tailwind for Japanese exporters (Toyota, Sony). EWJ (unhedged) benefits from both equity rally and FX tailwind for USD-based investors.
causal_chain: FRED JPNASSETS (BOJ total assets) growth accelerates >2% MoM for 3 months
Aggressive BOJ balance sheet expansion signals sustained monetary easing
USD/JPY rises as interest rate differential widens — yen weakens
Japanese exporters see margins improve as domestic costs are yen-denominated
EWJ rallies 8-15% as equity appreciation + yen weakening compound for USD investors
source_reference: Bank of Japan monetary policy statements; BOJ balance sheet data

name: USDA Weekly Export Inspections Wheat Above 5yr Avg → Long Wheat Futures Short-Term
thesis: USDA publishes weekly Export Inspections (free, Monday). When wheat export inspections exceed the 5-year seasonal average by >30% for 2 consecutive weeks, it signals unexpected foreign demand (often from MENA countries after poor harvests). Wheat futures (ZW=F) rally 3-8% over the following 2 weeks as the market reprices export demand.
causal_chain: USDA weekly export inspections for wheat exceed 5yr seasonal avg by >30% for 2 weeks
Elevated inspections signal unexpected foreign demand — likely MENA or Asian buyers
Foreign demand surge tightens US exportable surplus projections
USDA revises export forecast upward in next WASDE report
ZW=F rallies 3-8% over 2 weeks as export-driven tightness repriced
source_reference: USDA FAS Export Inspections; GASC tender records

name: NOAA US Drought Monitor D3+ Coverage Expansion → Long Ag Equipment (DE, AGCO, CNHI)
thesis: When the US Drought Monitor shows D3 (Extreme) or D4 (Exceptional) drought coverage expanding across >15% of contiguous US cropland, agricultural equipment companies (DE, AGCO) counterintuitively benefit in the medium term. Drought triggers crop insurance payouts that fund equipment replacement, USDA disaster assistance increases farm liquidity, and post-drought years see accelerated planting/tilling driving equipment demand.
causal_chain: NOAA US Drought Monitor shows D3/D4 coverage expands to >15% of US cropland
USDA activates emergency programs — disaster payments boost farm liquidity
Crop insurance payouts exceed premiums paid — farmers receive cash infusions
Post-drought year sees aggressive planting/soil remediation — equipment demand surges
DE, AGCO rally 8-15% as forward order books strengthen on anticipated post-drought demand
source_reference: NOAA/USDA US Drought Monitor; USDA RMA crop insurance data

name: FRED PCEPILFE Core PCE Supercore (Services Ex-Housing) Deceleration → Long REITs (VNQ)
thesis: When 'supercore' PCE (core services ex-housing, derived from FRED PCEPILFE minus shelter and goods components) decelerates for 3 consecutive months, it signals the Fed's most-watched inflation measure is easing. REITs (VNQ) benefit disproportionately because they are the most rate-sensitive equity sector. Supercore deceleration = Fed rate cut expectations = REIT re-rating.
causal_chain: Core PCE services ex-housing ('supercore') decelerates 3 consecutive months
Fed's preferred inflation measure easing — rate cut expectations build
Long-duration equities benefit most — REITs are effectively leveraged long-duration plays
Cap rate decompression expectations improve REIT NAV estimates
VNQ outperforms SPY by 5-10% over following 2 months as rate expectations ease
data_sources: FRED CUSR0000SASLE (services less energy CPI proxy)
source_reference: BEA PCE release; Fed meeting minutes referencing supercore

name: FRED WPUSI0191RG PPI Copper & Copper Products Recovery → Long Electrical Infrastructure (EATON, NVT)
thesis: When the PPI for copper and copper products (FRED WPUSI0191RG or proxy WPU1022) recovers >10% from trough, it signals a broad electrical infrastructure demand recovery. Companies making electrical distribution equipment (ETN/Eaton, NVT/nVent, HUBB) rally as copper demand recovery confirms grid modernization and data center buildout spending is accelerating.
causal_chain: FRED copper/copper products PPI recovers >10% from recent trough
Copper demand recovery confirms electrical infrastructure spending acceleration
Grid modernization (IRA/IIJA funding), data center buildout, EV charging all drive copper demand
Electrical equipment makers (ETN, NVT, HUBB) see order books strengthen
Electrical infrastructure stocks rally 10-15% as demand acceleration confirmed
data_sources: FRED WPU1022 (PPI copper and copper products)
source_reference: BLS PPI metals series; EEI utility capex survey

name: FRED ALTSALES Light Vehicle Sales Surge >17M SAAR → Long Auto Dealer Groups (AN, PAG, LAD)
thesis: When total light vehicle sales (FRED ALTSALES) surge above 17 million SAAR after a period below 15M, auto dealer groups (AN, PAG, LAD) rally sharply. Dealers benefit from both higher volume and elevated per-unit margins during recovery phases (pent-up demand allows pricing power). Dealer groups outperform OEMs because they capture both new and used vehicle margin expansion.
causal_chain: FRED ALTSALES (light vehicle sales SAAR) crosses above 17M after being <15M
Pent-up demand surge — consumers who deferred purchases during downturn return
Auto dealer groups see both volume surge and per-unit margin expansion
Used vehicle prices remain elevated supporting trade-in values and used margins
AN, PAG, LAD rally 15-25% over 2 months as revenue and margin both expand
source_reference: BEA Vehicle Sales; NADA Data annual reports

name: FRED INDPRO Industrial Production YoY Crossing Zero From Below → Long Materials Sector (XLB)
thesis: When total industrial production (FRED INDPRO) YoY growth crosses zero from negative territory (i.e., production stops contracting), the materials sector (XLB) outperforms SPY by 5-10% over the following 2 months. Materials have the highest operating leverage to production recovery — volume increases hit margins before pricing even improves.
causal_chain: FRED INDPRO YoY growth crosses zero from negative — production contraction ending
Manufacturing demand recovery signals improving end-market conditions
Materials companies (chemicals, metals, packaging) see volume increase
High operating leverage means margin expansion precedes revenue growth
XLB outperforms SPY by 5-10% over following 2 months as cycle turns
source_reference: Federal Reserve G.17 Industrial Production release

name: FRED XTEXVA01CNM667S China Exports Volume Surge → Long Dry Bulk Shippers (SBLK, GNK)
thesis: When China's export volume index (proxied by FRED XTEXVA01CNM667S or similar OECD trade series) surges >10% MoM, it signals a Chinese manufacturing recovery. Dry bulk shippers (SBLK, GNK, GOGL) benefit as increased Chinese manufacturing drives demand for raw material imports (iron ore, coal, grain), boosting freight rates with a 2-4 week lag.
causal_chain: China export volume index surges >10% MoM — manufacturing recovery underway
Higher Chinese output = increased demand for imported raw materials (iron ore, coal)
Dry bulk freight demand rises — BDI increases with 2-4 week lag
Dry bulk shipper daily earnings improve significantly (high operating leverage)
SBLK, GNK rally 10-20% over following month as spot rate improvement flows to earnings
data_sources: yfinance SBLK, GNK, GOGL, BDRY (dry bulk ETF)
source_reference: OECD international trade data; Clarksons Shipping Intelligence

name: FRED PCUOMFG PPI Output Manufacturing Acceleration → Long Packaging Companies (PKG, SEE, IP)
thesis: When the PPI for total manufacturing output (FRED PCUOMFG or proxy PPIMAN) accelerates for 3 months, packaging companies (PKG, SEE, IP) rally as rising output means more goods need to be packaged. Packaging is a 'toll booth' on manufacturing activity — volume-sensitive with 60-70% fixed costs providing high operating leverage on incremental output.
causal_chain: FRED PPI manufacturing output accelerates for 3 consecutive months
Higher manufacturing output = more goods produced = more packaging needed
Containerboard (PKG, IP) and flexible packaging (SEE) see volume improvement
High fixed-cost structure means incremental volume drops to bottom line at 60-70% margin
Packaging stocks rally 8-12% as volume recovery drives earnings leverage
source_reference: BLS PPI; Fibre Box Association (FBA) shipments data

name: FRED CUUR0000SAT1 CPI Transportation Services Surge → Long Auto Insurance Companies (ALL, PGR, CB)
thesis: When CPI transportation services (FRED CUSR0000SAS4, which includes motor vehicle insurance) surges >8% YoY, it signals auto insurers are successfully pushing through rate increases. P&C insurers with heavy auto lines (ALL, PGR, TRV) see combined ratios improve dramatically as earned premium grows faster than claims. Insurance stocks rally 8-15% as underwriting profitability improves.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SAS4 (CPI transportation services) surges >8% YoY
Auto insurance premium rate increases flowing through to CPI measurement
Earned premium growth exceeds loss cost growth for well-managed carriers
Combined ratios improve 3-5 points as rate adequacy established
ALL, PGR rally 8-15% as underwriting profitability visible in quarterly results
source_reference: BLS CPI transportation component; AM Best P&C underwriting data

name: FRED B020RE1Q156NBEA Nonresidential Software Investment Acceleration → Long Cloud/SaaS (WCLD, SKYY)
thesis: When real private nonresidential fixed investment in software (FRED Y033RE1Q156NBEA or B020RE1Q156NBEA) accelerates for 2 consecutive quarters, it signals enterprise IT spending cycle recovery. Cloud/SaaS ETFs (WCLD, SKYY) rally 10-15% as enterprise software budgets are the first line item restored after a capex downturn.
causal_chain: FRED real nonresidential software investment accelerates 2 consecutive quarters
Enterprise IT budget cycle turning positive — software spending first to recover
Cloud migration and SaaS subscription growth re-accelerate
CIOs increase annual recurring revenue commitments to cloud vendors
WCLD, SKYY rally 10-15% over following quarter as billings growth inflects
source_reference: BEA GDP release; Gartner IT Spending Forecast

name: FRED DTCTHFNM Consumer Auto Loan Originations Recovery → Long Used Car Dealers (KMX, CVNA)
thesis: When auto loan originations (proxied by FRED DTCTHFNM finance company owned auto loans, or total motor vehicle loans outstanding) show 3 consecutive months of growth after decline, used car dealers (KMX, CVNA) rally sharply. Auto loan availability is the binding constraint on used car sales — when credit flows resume, transaction volumes surge before pricing improves.
causal_chain: FRED motor vehicle loans outstanding shows 3 consecutive months of growth after decline
Auto loan credit availability improving — lenders easing standards
Used car transaction volume surges as credit-constrained buyers can now finance
KMX, CVNA see units sold inflect upward before GPU (gross profit per unit) improves
Used car dealer stocks rally 15-25% from trough as volume recovery drives revenue
data_sources: FRED DTCTHFNM (finance company motor vehicle loans)
source_reference: Federal Reserve G.19 Consumer Credit; Manheim Used Vehicle Index

name: FRED PCUA Personal Consumption on Computing/Communication Equipment Surge → Long Semiconductor (SMH)
thesis: When personal consumption expenditures on information processing equipment (FRED DODGRG3M086SBEA or computing/communication equipment PCE) surge >10% YoY, it signals a consumer tech upgrade cycle. Semiconductor companies (via SMH) benefit as consumer device demand drives chip orders. The PCE data leads semiconductor revenue by 1-2 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED PCE on computing/communication equipment surges >10% YoY
Consumer tech upgrade cycle underway — PC, smartphone, tablet replacement demand
Consumer electronics OEMs increase chip orders to meet demand
Semiconductor companies see wafer starts and utilization rates increase
SMH rallies 8-15% over following 2 months as consumer demand flows to chip earnings
source_reference: BEA Personal Income and Outlays; SIA semiconductor sales data

name: FRED CUSR0000SAM CPI Medical Care Commodities Trough → Long Generic Pharma (TEVA, MYL proxy)
thesis: When CPI for medical care commodities (FRED CUSR0000SAM, which includes prescription drugs) troughs and begins rising after a deflationary period, generic pharma companies (TEVA, generic drug-exposed names) benefit. The trough signals generic drug pricing stabilization after competitive pressure, meaning margins stop compressing and earnings estimates stabilize.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SAM (CPI medical care commodities) troughs after deflationary period
Generic drug pricing stabilization signals end of competitive pricing pressure
Generic pharma companies see revenue per prescription stabilize
Earnings estimate revisions stop declining — bottoming process begins
TEVA and generic-exposed pharma rally 10-20% from trough as margin compression ends
source_reference: BLS CPI medical components; IQVIA prescription drug pricing trends

name: FRED HLTHSCPSA Healthcare Construction Spending Acceleration → Long Medical Device Companies (SYK, ZBH, MDT)
thesis: When healthcare construction spending (FRED HLTHSCPSA) accelerates for 3 consecutive months, it signals hospital systems are expanding capacity. New hospital wings and outpatient centers require medical equipment — surgical robots, imaging systems, hospital beds. Medical device companies (SYK, ZBH, MDT) rally with a 2-3 quarter lag as construction completion triggers equipment procurement.
causal_chain: FRED HLTHSCPSA (healthcare construction spending) accelerates 3 consecutive months
Hospital systems expanding — new wings, outpatient centers, ambulatory surgery centers
Construction completion in 12-18 months triggers medical equipment procurement cycle
SYK (surgical robots), ZBH (orthopedic implants), MDT (cardiac devices) see order flow
Medical device stocks rally 8-12% as forward order visibility improves
source_reference: Census Bureau C30 Construction Spending; AHA Hospital Construction Survey

name: FRED CUSR0000SETA01 CPI New Vehicles YoY Deflation → Long Auto OEMs (F, GM, TM) Margin Recovery
thesis: Counterintuitively, when CPI new vehicles (FRED CUSR0000SETA01) goes YoY negative (vehicle price deflation), auto OEMs rally within 2 months. The price deflation signals inventory normalization — lots are full, incentives return to normal, and the market stops fearing demand destruction. Volume recovery from normalized pricing more than offsets lower per-unit margins.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SETA01 (CPI new vehicles) turns YoY negative — vehicle price deflation
Price deflation signals dealer inventory normalization after post-COVID shortage premium
Normalized pricing removes demand destruction from unaffordability
Vehicle sales volume recovers as average transaction prices become more accessible
F, GM rally 8-12% as volume recovery offsets per-unit margin normalization
source_reference: BLS CPI motor vehicle components; Ward's Auto SAAR data

name: FRED LOADFACTOR Airline Load Factor Peak → Long Airport/Ground Services (AENA analog: FLR, AECOM)
thesis: When BTS airline load factor (available free from BTS T-100) exceeds 87% for 3 consecutive months, it signals airports are capacity-constrained. Airport engineering and construction companies (FLR, AECOM, J) benefit as airport authorities accelerate terminal expansion capital programs. Load factor >87% is the inflection point where FAA runway delay data spikes, triggering Congressional and local pressure for airport investment.
causal_chain: BTS airline load factor exceeds 87% for 3 consecutive months
FAA OPSNET data shows increasing runway delays at major hubs
Airport authorities accelerate terminal expansion and runway projects
Engineering/construction firms (FLR, AECOM, J) win airport infrastructure contracts
Infrastructure engineering stocks rally 8-12% as airport capex pipeline builds
source_reference: BTS Air Carrier Statistics T-100; FAA OPSNET runway delay database

name: FRED CASS Cass Freight Index Shipments YoY Inflection → Long LTL Truckers (ODFL, SAIA, XPO)
thesis: When the Cass Freight Index shipments component (available free from Cass Information Systems) shows YoY rate of change improving for 3 consecutive months (even if still negative), LTL carriers (ODFL, SAIA, XPO) outperform truckload by 2:1. LTL has higher barriers to entry (terminal networks), meaning capacity stays disciplined even in downturns, so volume recovery drops straight to the bottom line.
causal_chain: Cass Freight Index shipments YoY rate of change improves 3 consecutive months
Freight volume trough confirmed — manufacturing destocking cycle ending
LTL carriers (ODFL, SAIA, XPO) have disciplined capacity — terminal networks intact
Volume recovery flows directly to margins due to high fixed-cost terminal networks
LTL stocks rally 12-20% and outperform truckload (KNX, WERN) by 2:1 from freight trough
source_reference: Cass Information Systems Cass Freight Index; ATA Truck Tonnage

name: FRED TTLHHM156N Total Household Mortgage Debt Stabilization → Long Mortgage Originators (RKT, UWMC)
thesis: When total household mortgage debt (FRED MDOAH or TTLHHM156N from NY Fed) stabilizes after a period of slowing growth (QoQ change near zero but positive), it signals the mortgage market trough. Non-bank mortgage originators (RKT, UWMC) rally 20-30% from trough as origination volumes bottom and refi activity begins to recover when rates ease even slightly.
causal_chain: FRED household mortgage debt QoQ growth stabilizes near zero after deceleration
Mortgage origination market troughing — no further volume decline
Slight rate decrease (25-50bps) triggers wave of refinancing activity
Non-bank originators (RKT, UWMC) see application volume surge from trough
Mortgage originator stocks rally 20-30% from trough as volume recovery begins
source_reference: NY Fed Household Debt and Credit Report; MBA Mortgage Applications Index

name: EIA Monthly Electric Power Renewable Generation Share Crossing New High → Long Clean Energy (ICLN, TAN)
thesis: When EIA Monthly Electric Power data shows renewable generation (wind + solar) share of total US electricity crosses a new quarterly high, it signals acceleration in the energy transition. Clean energy ETFs (ICLN, TAN) rally as the data confirms deployment is accelerating and policy momentum is positive. The generation share data is a lagging confirmer that validates forward deployment expectations.
causal_chain: EIA Electric Power Monthly shows renewable generation share crosses new quarterly high
Data confirms energy transition acceleration — policy and economics aligned
Investors increase allocation to clean energy as growth trajectory confirmed
Solar and wind developers (FSLR, ENPH, NEE) see valuation multiple expansion
ICLN, TAN rally 8-15% as generation data confirms deployment acceleration
source_reference: EIA Electric Power Monthly; IRENA Renewable Capacity Statistics

name: FRED ELECT000000 PPI Electricity Surge → Long Regulated Utilities (NEE, DUK, SO) Revenue Growth
thesis: When PPI for electricity (FRED WPU0542 or CUSR0000SEHF01 electricity CPI) surges >10% YoY, regulated utilities with fuel cost pass-through mechanisms see revenue growth accelerate without margin compression. Utilities (NEE, DUK, SO) with formula rate plans benefit because higher electricity prices flow directly to revenue while authorized returns are unchanged.
causal_chain: FRED electricity PPI/CPI surges >10% YoY — electricity prices rising across the board
Regulated utilities with fuel adjustment clauses pass through higher costs to ratepayers
Revenue growth accelerates while authorized ROE remains unchanged by rate cases
Earnings per share benefit from revenue growth on flat or slowly growing rate base
Utility stocks rally 5-8% as revenue growth narrative attracts yield-seeking investors
source_reference: BLS PPI electricity series; EEI Industry Statistical Yearbook

name: FRED TOTALSA EV Adoption Proxy (via Tesla Sales Share) → Long Lithium Miners (ALB, SQM, LAC)
thesis: When total vehicle sales (FRED TOTALSA) show EV penetration crossing new high (proxied by Tesla quarterly deliveries as % of US light vehicle sales), lithium miners (ALB, SQM, LAC) benefit from forward demand expectations. Each 1pp of EV penetration increase represents ~150,000 additional vehicles annually requiring ~1,500 tonnes of lithium carbonate equivalent. Lithium miners rally as demand projections are revised upward.
causal_chain: Tesla quarterly deliveries as % of FRED TOTALSA crosses new quarterly high
EV penetration acceleration confirmed — broader EV market growing even faster than Tesla alone
Each 1pp EV penetration = ~1,500 tonnes additional lithium carbonate demand annually
Lithium supply is constrained — new mines take 5-7 years to develop
Lithium miners (ALB, SQM, LAC) rally 10-20% as forward demand expectations increase
data_sources: yfinance TSLA, ALB, SQM, LAC, LIT
source_reference: IEA Global EV Outlook; USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries (lithium)

name: FRED CUSR0000SAF11 CPI Food at Home Deceleration → Long Grocery Chains (KR, ACI, GO)
thesis: When CPI food at home (FRED CUSR0000SAF11) decelerates from >8% YoY to <4% YoY over 6 months, grocery chains (KR, ACI, GO/Grocery Outlet) benefit. The deceleration means input cost pressure is easing but shelf prices adjust slowly — creating a margin windfall. Grocery chains see gross margin expansion of 50-100bps during the deceleration phase.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SAF11 (CPI food at home) decelerates from >8% to <4% YoY over 6 months
Wholesale food input costs (PPI food) decelerate faster than shelf price adjustments
Grocery chains maintain elevated shelf prices while COGS decline — margin expansion
Gross margins expand 50-100bps during deceleration phase (prices sticky, costs falling)
KR, ACI outperform XLP by 5-8% during food inflation deceleration phase
source_reference: BLS CPI food components; USDA ERS Food Price Outlook

name: FRED PCUASERV PPI Airline Services Collapse → Long Airlines Contrarian (JETS) Post-Trough
thesis: When the PPI for airline services (proxy: FRED CPI air transportation CUSR0000SETG01) drops >15% YoY, it signals airlines are in a price war and demand trough. Counterintuitively, this is the signal to go long — capacity cuts follow within 2-3 months, and the stocks have already priced in the worst. JETS rallies 15-25% from the PPI trough as capacity discipline restores pricing power.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SETG01 (CPI airline fares) drops >15% YoY — fare war and demand trough
Airlines respond with capacity cuts — schedule reductions announced within 2-3 months
Weaker carriers reduce routes — surviving airlines see load factors improve
Fuel costs often declining at same time (correlated with demand weakness)
JETS ETF rallies 15-25% from trough as capacity discipline restores pricing
source_reference: BLS CPI air transportation; BTS airline statistics

name: FRED RSXFS Retail Sales ex-Food Services Control Group Surge → Long Payment Processors (V, MA, SQ)
thesis: When the retail sales control group (FRED RSXFS, ex-food services, auto dealers, building materials, gas stations) surges >0.5% MoM for 2 consecutive months, payment processors (V, MA, SQ) rally as transaction volumes increase. Payment processors are 'toll booths' on consumer spending — they take a percentage of each transaction regardless of what's being sold.
causal_chain: FRED RSXFS (retail sales control group) surges >0.5% MoM for 2 consecutive months
Consumer spending acceleration drives higher card transaction volumes
Payment processors take fixed % of each transaction — revenue scales linearly with spending
V, MA see cross-border volumes also improve as domestic spending strength correlates with travel
Payment processor stocks rally 5-10% as transaction volume growth accelerates
source_reference: Census Bureau Advance Retail Sales; Visa/Mastercard quarterly earnings

name: FRED ECOMPCTSA E-Commerce Share of Retail Sales New Quarterly High → Long Logistics REITs (PLD, REXR)
thesis: When e-commerce as % of total retail sales (FRED ECOMPCTSA) crosses a new quarterly high, logistics/warehouse REITs (PLD/Prologis, REXR) benefit as incremental e-commerce volume requires 3x the warehouse space of traditional retail. Each 1pp increase in e-commerce share represents ~300M sqft of additional warehouse demand. PLD and REXR outperform VNQ during e-commerce acceleration phases.
causal_chain: FRED ECOMPCTSA (e-commerce % of retail) crosses new quarterly high
E-commerce acceleration confirmed — structural shift continuing
Each 1pp e-commerce share increase = ~300M sqft additional warehouse demand (CBRE estimate)
Logistics REITs (PLD, REXR) see occupancy and rent growth accelerate
PLD, REXR outperform VNQ by 5-8% in quarters with new e-commerce share highs
data_sources: FRED ECOMPCTSA (e-commerce as % of retail sales)
source_reference: Census Bureau Quarterly Retail E-Commerce Sales; CBRE warehouse demand research

name: FRED BOGZ1FA106130005Q Nonfinancial Corporate Profits Recovery → Long SPY Earnings Expansion
thesis: When nonfinancial corporate profits (FRED CP or BOGZ1FA106130005Q) show 2 consecutive quarters of YoY growth after a decline, SPY rallies 8-12% over the following quarter as earnings expansion drives valuation support. Corporate profits from NIPA data lead S&P 500 EPS by 1-2 quarters because BEA captures the full universe of businesses, not just public companies.
causal_chain: FRED CP (corporate profits with IVA and CCAdj) shows 2 quarters of YoY growth after decline
BEA NIPA profits lead S&P 500 EPS by 1-2 quarters — captures full economy
S&P 500 earnings estimates revised upward as economy-wide profitability recovers
Forward P/E appears more attractive as denominator (E) increases
SPY rallies 8-12% over following quarter as earnings expansion confirms
data_sources: FRED CP (corporate profits with IVA and CCAdj)
source_reference: BEA NIPA corporate profits; Federal Reserve Z.1 Financial Accounts

name: FRED LOANINV Bank Loan-to-Deposit Ratio Trough → Long KRE Bank Lending Cycle
thesis: When the bank loan-to-deposit ratio (computable from FRED TOTLL/DPSACBW027SBOG or similar H.8 data) troughs and begins rising, it signals banks are transitioning from hoarding deposits to deploying them into loans. This is the earliest signal of a new credit expansion cycle. Regional banks (KRE) rally 15-25% from loan-to-deposit trough as NII growth expectations improve.
causal_chain: Bank loan-to-deposit ratio (FRED TOTLL / DPSACBW027SBOG) troughs and begins rising
Banks shifting from deposit hoarding to loan deployment — credit cycle turning
Loan growth drives net interest income expansion — banks' primary revenue driver
NII estimates revised upward for regional banks (higher loan/deposit sensitivity)
KRE rallies 15-25% from loan-to-deposit trough as credit expansion cycle begins
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.8 release; FDIC Quarterly Banking Profile

name: FRED PZINC IMF Zinc Price Recovery → Long Galvanized Steel Users (Automakers, Infrastructure)
thesis: When IMF zinc price (FRED PZINCUSDM) recovers >20% from trough, it signals global industrial demand recovery (zinc is 60% used for galvanizing steel). This is a cleaner industrial cycle indicator than copper because zinc has less speculative/financial positioning. The zinc recovery signal leads XLI (industrials) by 4-6 weeks with 75%+ hit rate historically.
causal_chain: FRED PZINCUSDM (IMF zinc price) recovers >20% from trough
Zinc recovery signals global galvanized steel demand improving
Galvanized steel demand = auto production + infrastructure construction recovering
Industrial sector earnings estimates revised upward as end-market demand improves
XLI rallies 5-8% over following 4-6 weeks, led by machinery and capital goods
source_reference: IMF Primary Commodity Prices; ILZSG zinc supply/demand balance

name: FRED PLOGORE IMF Iron Ore Price 3-Month Surge → Long Australian Dollar (FXA) and BHP
thesis: When IMF iron ore price (FRED PIORECRUSDM) surges >25% in 3 months, the Australian dollar (FXA) and Australian miners (BHP, RIO) rally with a 1-2 week lag. Australia's terms of trade are dominated by iron ore exports — a 25% price increase improves Australia's current account by ~0.5% of GDP, strengthening AUD. BHP has 60%+ EBITDA from iron ore.
causal_chain: FRED PIORECRUSDM (iron ore price) surges >25% in 3-month window
Australia's terms of trade improve dramatically — iron ore is largest export
AUD strengthens vs USD as current account improves and hot money flows to Aussie assets
BHP (60%+ EBITDA from iron ore) sees earnings estimates revised up 20-30%
FXA rallies 3-5%, BHP rallies 10-15% over following 2-4 weeks
source_reference: IMF Primary Commodity Prices; RBA Terms of Trade Index

name: FRED PNICKUSDM Nickel Price Collapse → Long Stainless Steel End-Users (SWK, SNA, GWW)
thesis: When IMF nickel price (FRED PNICKUSDM) collapses >25% in 3 months, stainless steel costs decline dramatically (nickel is 8-10% of stainless steel cost). Industrial distributors and tool makers that use stainless steel (SWK, SNA, GWW) see input costs decline while selling prices remain stable — creating a margin windfall visible in the next quarter's earnings.
causal_chain: FRED PNICKUSDM (nickel price) collapses >25% in 3 months
Stainless steel prices decline with 4-8 week lag as mills pass through lower nickel costs
Industrial tool and equipment makers (SWK, SNA) see raw material costs decline
Selling prices remain stable (contract-based, slow to adjust) — margin windfall
SWK, SNA, GWW see gross margin expansion of 100-200bps in next quarterly earnings
source_reference: IMF Primary Commodity Prices; ISSF stainless steel production data

name: FRED WALCL Fed Balance Sheet Weekly Decline Pace Slowing → Long Duration (TLT) QT Tapering Signal
thesis: When the pace of Fed balance sheet decline (FRED WALCL, weekly changes) slows — 4-week rolling decline rate reduces by >50% — it signals QT tapering is imminent or underway. Long-duration Treasuries (TLT) rally 3-5% as the market prices in reduced Treasury supply pressure and eventual QT end. The balance sheet pace change leads the official QT taper announcement by 4-8 weeks.
causal_chain: FRED WALCL (Fed total assets) 4-week rolling decline rate reduces by >50%
Slowing QT pace signals Fed approaching reserve scarcity threshold
Market anticipates official QT taper announcement — reduced Treasury supply ahead
Long-duration yields decline 15-25bps as term premium compresses
TLT rallies 3-5% over following month as QT tapering confirmed
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.4.1 release; FOMC meeting minutes

name: FRED WORAL Fed Total Repos Outstanding Surge → Short-Term Funding Stress Signal → Long Gold
thesis: When the Fed's repo operations (FRED RRPONTSYD for reverse repo, or total repo facility usage) show a sudden surge in usage (>$50B increase in a week), it signals short-term funding stress in the banking system. Gold (GLD) rallies 2-5% over the following 2 weeks as the funding stress signal triggers safe-haven demand, even if the stress is 'contained'.
causal_chain: Fed repo facility usage surges >$50B in a single week — funding stress signal
Interbank market shows signs of strain — SOFR spikes above IORB target
Financial media coverage amplifies concern about banking system liquidity
Safe-haven demand increases — gold and Treasuries benefit
GLD rallies 2-5% over following 2 weeks as funding stress triggers risk reduction
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.4.1 repo operations; NY Fed repo data

name: FRED M1SL M1 Money Supply 6mo Rate of Change Positive After Contraction → Long Bitcoin (BTC-USD)
thesis: When M1 money supply (FRED M1SL) 6-month rate of change turns positive after a contraction period, Bitcoin and risk assets rally as liquidity conditions improve. M1 growth leads Bitcoin price by 2-3 months because expanding narrow money supply signals increased transactional liquidity that eventually flows into speculative assets.
causal_chain: FRED M1SL 6-month rate of change turns positive after period of contraction
Narrow money supply expansion signals improved liquidity conditions
Expanding M1 means more transactional balances available for deployment
Speculative assets (crypto, growth stocks) benefit from liquidity expansion with 2-3 month lag
BTC-USD rallies 20-40% over following quarter as liquidity cycle turns positive
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.6 Money Stock Measures; Howell Global Liquidity Index methodology

name: NOAA HDD Spike in Dec-Feb → Long Propane Distributors (SPH, NGL, GLP)
thesis: When winter HDD (Heating Degree Days, FRED CLVLNDCHDD or regional HDD data) spike >20% above normal for 2+ consecutive weeks during Dec-Feb, propane distributors benefit disproportionately. Propane is the primary heating fuel for 5.5M US homes (mostly rural). Distributors (SPH, NGL, GLP) see volume and per-gallon margin surge during cold spikes as retail propane prices spike faster than wholesale.
causal_chain: NOAA HDD data shows 2+ consecutive weeks >20% above normal during Dec-Feb
Residential propane demand surges — 5.5M US homes use propane for heating
Wholesale propane prices rise but retail prices rise faster — distributor margin expands
Propane distributors (SPH, NGL, GLP) see both volume and per-gallon margin increase
Distributor stocks rally 8-15% during cold spells as earnings expectations improve
source_reference: EIA Winter Fuels Outlook; NOAA Climate Prediction Center

name: USDA NASS Cotton Crop Condition 'Poor/Very Poor' >30% → Long Cotton Futures (CT=F)
thesis: USDA NASS weekly crop condition reports rate cotton as Excellent/Good/Fair/Poor/Very Poor. When the combined 'Poor' and 'Very Poor' percentage exceeds 30% of the Texas cotton crop during July-August (peak growing season), US cotton production is at severe risk. Cotton futures (CT=F) rally 10-20% over the following month as abandonment rates increase and USDA cuts production estimates.
causal_chain: USDA weekly crop condition shows Texas cotton 'Poor/Very Poor' >30% during Jul-Aug
Texas produces 40%+ of US cotton — crop stress signals major production shortfall
Abandonment rates increase as fields fail to reach economic yield threshold
USDA WASDE revises US cotton production estimate downward by >10%
Cotton futures (CT=F) rally 10-20% over following month as supply tightness confirmed
source_reference: USDA NASS; NCC National Cotton Council; ICAC World Cotton Statistics

name: FRED CUSR0000SETB CPI Used Cars & Trucks 3-Month Annualized Spike → Long CVNA/KMX Short-Term
thesis: When CPI used cars & trucks (FRED CUSR0000SETA02) shows a 3-month annualized rate exceeding 15%, used car dealers (KMX, CVNA) are in a seller's market. Rising used car prices mean higher gross profit per unit on existing inventory (bought at lower prices). The CPI confirmation of price acceleration leads same-store-sales beats. KMX and CVNA rally 5-10% on earnings beats driven by GPU expansion.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SETA02 (CPI used cars) 3-month annualized rate exceeds 15%
Used car price appreciation means existing inventory appreciates while sitting on lot
Gross profit per unit (GPU) expands as selling prices rise faster than acquisition costs
KMX, CVNA report earnings beats driven by GPU expansion
Used car dealer stocks rally 5-10% around earnings on margin surprise
data_sources: FRED CUSR0000SETA02 (CPI used cars and trucks)
source_reference: BLS CPI used vehicles; Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index

name: FRED FDEFX Federal Defense Expenditures QoQ Surge → Long Mid-Cap Defense (LDOS, BWXT, KTOS)
thesis: When federal defense expenditures (FRED FDEFX) show QoQ growth >5% (real, annualized), mid-cap defense contractors outperform primes. The spending surge signals procurement acceleration that favors specialty/niche contractors (LDOS for IT, BWXT for nuclear, KTOS for drones) because large primes are already backlogged while mid-caps have capacity to absorb incremental orders.
causal_chain: FRED FDEFX (federal defense expenditures) shows QoQ growth >5% (real, annualized)
Defense spending acceleration signals procurement pipeline converting to contracts
Large primes (LMT, RTX) already backlogged — limited capacity for incremental orders
Mid-cap defense (LDOS, BWXT, KTOS) have capacity and specialization to absorb surge
Mid-cap defense outperforms ITA by 5-10% as orders flow to available capacity
data_sources: FRED FDEFX (federal defense consumption expenditures and gross investment)
source_reference: BEA GDP defense component; DoD Comptroller budget execution data

name: FRED TOTRESNS Total Bank Reserves Decline to Scarcity Threshold → Long Repo Desk Profits (GS, MS, JPM)
thesis: When total bank reserves (FRED TOTRESNS or WRESBAL) decline to the 'ample but approaching scarce' threshold (~$3T as of 2024-2025), repo desk spreads widen as banks compete for reserves. Dealer banks (GS, MS, JPM) with large repo operations see trading revenue increase as bid-ask spreads in the repo market widen. This is a niche revenue driver for FICC trading desks.
causal_chain: FRED WRESBAL (reserve balances) decline toward scarcity threshold (~$3T)
Interbank reserve competition increases — SOFR volatility rises
Repo market bid-ask spreads widen as reserve scarcity creates friction
Dealer banks (GS, MS, JPM) earn higher spreads on matched-book repo activity
FICC trading revenue beats at money center banks in following quarter
source_reference: Federal Reserve H.4.1; Pozsar Reserve Scarcity Framework; Fed Staff Papers on Reserve Demand

name: FRED GFDEGDQ188S Debt/GDP Quarterly Jump >3pp → Long Defense (ITA) as Fiscal Expansion Signal
thesis: When federal debt to GDP (FRED GFDEGDQ188S) jumps >3 percentage points in a single quarter, it signals a major fiscal expansion event (stimulus, emergency spending). Defense spending is historically protected during fiscal expansions while other discretionary is cut in subsequent austerity. Defense ETF (ITA) outperforms SPY by 5-8% in the 6 months following debt/GDP jumps.
causal_chain: FRED GFDEGDQ188S jumps >3pp in single quarter — major fiscal expansion event
Fiscal expansion means government is spending aggressively — often emergency/defense
Defense budget historically protected during fiscal expansion then subsequent austerity
Defense contractors (ITA constituents) benefit from spending surge and sustained budgets
ITA outperforms SPY by 5-8% in 6 months following large debt/GDP quarterly jumps
source_reference: BEA GDP release; CBO Budget and Economic Outlook

name: FRED PERMIT5MNED Multi-Family Permits (5+ Units) Collapse → Long Multi-Family REITs 18mo Out (EQR, AVB)
thesis: When multi-family building permits for 5+ units (FRED PERMIT5MNED or PERMIT5) collapse >30% YoY, it signals a future supply cliff 18-24 months later (construction-to-completion lag). Multi-family REITs (EQR, AVB, MAA) become attractive 12-18 months after the permit collapse because new apartment supply dries up, driving rent growth acceleration and occupancy improvement.
