causal_chain: FRED PERMIT5 (5+ unit building permits) collapse >30% YoY
Multi-family construction pipeline dries up with 18-24 month delivery lag
12-18 months later: new apartment completions cliff — supply growth decelerates sharply
Existing apartment owners (EQR, AVB, MAA) see occupancy and rent growth improve
Multi-family REITs rally 15-20% as supply cliff becomes visible in completion data
source_reference: Census Bureau New Residential Construction; RealPage apartment supply pipeline data

name: FRED CPFF Commercial Paper Financial Spread to Fed Funds Spike → Long Money Market (BIL) Safe Haven
thesis: When the financial commercial paper rate minus fed funds rate (computable from FRED DCPF3M - DFEDTARU) spikes >50bps, it signals acute financial sector funding stress. Ultra-short duration Treasury ETFs (BIL, SHV) outperform as flight-to-quality drives T-bill yields down relative to everything else. The commercial paper spread spike is the earliest warning signal of financial stress.
causal_chain: FRED DCPF3M (financial commercial paper 3mo) minus DFEDTARU spikes >50bps
Financial commercial paper spread spike = acute funding stress for financials
Flight to quality — money flows from commercial paper and bank deposits to T-bills
T-bill yields decline relative to fed funds as demand surges
BIL, SHV outperform as Treasury quality premium increases during stress episode
source_reference: Federal Reserve CP rate data; Fed Staff Papers on commercial paper market stress

name: FRED WILL5000INDFC Wilshire 5000 Market Cap Relative to GDP (Buffett Indicator) Mean Reversion From Trough
thesis: When the Wilshire 5000 to GDP ratio (FRED WILL5000INDFC / GDP) drops >1 standard deviation below its 10-year average, US equities are undervalued relative to the real economy. The ratio mean-reverts over 6-12 months with 80%+ probability. Long SPY when the ratio drops below the threshold and hold for 6 months for 10-15% outperformance vs. cash.
causal_chain: FRED WILL5000INDFC / GDP drops >1 standard deviation below 10yr average
US equity market capitalization undervalued relative to GDP (real economy proxy)
Valuation mean-reversion forces: buybacks accelerate at low prices, institutional rebalancing
Fundamental recovery (GDP growth) eventually catches up with depressed valuations
SPY delivers 10-15% return over following 6 months as ratio mean-reverts
data_sources: FRED WILL5000INDFC (Wilshire 5000 Full Cap Price Index)
source_reference: Wilshire Associates; Buffett Indicator methodology; various Hussman funds research

name: FRED SLOAS Student Loan Repayment Restart → Long Consumer Staples (XLP) Relative to Discretionary
thesis: When student loan repayments restart after a forbearance period (trackable via FRED SLOAS or policy announcements), ~$5-10B/month in disposable income is redirected from discretionary spending to debt service. Consumer staples (XLP) outperform discretionary (XLY) by 5-8% in the 3 months following repayment restart as younger consumers cut discretionary spending first.
causal_chain: Student loan repayments restart after forbearance (FRED SLOAS reflects outstanding balance changes)
~$5-10B/month in household disposable income redirected to debt service
Younger consumers (25-35) disproportionately affected — cut discretionary first
Restaurants, entertainment, apparel see same-store-sales decelerate
XLP outperforms XLY by 5-8% in 3 months following repayment restart
source_reference: NY Fed Household Debt and Credit Report; Federal Student Aid portfolio data

name: FRED CUSR0000SEHA CPI Rent of Primary Residence Deceleration → Long Homebuilders (ITB) Rate Sensitivity
thesis: When CPI rent of primary residence (FRED CUSR0000SEHA) deceleration accelerates (YoY dropping by >1pp in 3 months), it signals shelter inflation is finally feeding into CPI. This is the missing piece for Fed rate cuts. Homebuilders (ITB) rally 10-15% as the market prices in rate cuts that will lower mortgage rates and boost housing demand.
causal_chain: FRED CUSR0000SEHA (CPI rent of primary residence) YoY drops by >1pp in 3 months
Shelter inflation deceleration feeding into CPI — Fed's concern easing
Fed funds futures reprice to expect additional 50-75bps of cuts
Mortgage rate expectations decline — housing affordability improving
Homebuilders (ITB) rally 10-15% as demand outlook brightens from rate expectations
data_sources: FRED CUSR0000SEHA (CPI rent of primary residence)
source_reference: BLS CPI shelter components; Zillow Observed Rent Index (ZORI) as leading indicator

name: FRED BUSAPPWNSAUS Business Applications (High-Propensity) Surge → Long Small Cap (IWM) New Business Cycle
thesis: When high-propensity business applications (FRED BABATOTALSAUS or Census Bureau Business Formation Statistics) surge >15% YoY for 3 months, it signals a wave of new business formation. Small cap stocks (IWM) outperform because new businesses create demand for the types of products/services small caps provide (local services, SMB software, equipment rental). The business formation wave leads small cap earnings by 2-3 quarters.
causal_chain: FRED high-propensity business applications surge >15% YoY for 3 consecutive months
New business formation wave underway — entrepreneurial activity accelerating
New businesses need equipment, software, insurance, accounting — small cap revenue drivers
Small cap earnings growth accelerates as new business demand flows through
IWM outperforms SPY by 5-10% over following quarter as small cap earnings inflect
source_reference: Census Bureau Business Formation Statistics; Kauffman Foundation entrepreneurship research

name: FRED WPUSI0811 PPI Pulp, Paper & Products Recovery → Long Paper/Packaging (IP, PKG, WRK)
thesis: When PPI for pulp, paper, and allied products (FRED WPU0911 or proxy) recovers >5% from trough over 3 months, paper and packaging companies (IP, WRK, PKG) rally as pricing power returns. Paper is a cyclical commodity — price recovery signals demand > supply (often from corrugated packaging for e-commerce). High fixed costs mean price recovery produces outsized margin expansion.
causal_chain: FRED PPI for pulp/paper/packaging recovers >5% from trough over 3 months
Paper pricing recovery signals corrugated packaging demand exceeding supply
E-commerce growth and industrial packaging demand driving recovery
IP, WRK, PKG see revenue per ton increase while costs are largely fixed
Paper/packaging stocks rally 10-15% as pricing recovery drives margin expansion
data_sources: FRED WPU0911 (PPI pulp paper and allied products)
source_reference: BLS PPI pulp and paper series; AF&PA (American Forest & Paper Association) statistics

name: USGS Water Use Report → Long Water Treatment Equipment (XYL, WTS, FBIN)
thesis: When USGS releases updated water use data showing per-capita water withdrawals declining while total demand is rising (due to population growth + data centers + industrial), water treatment and infrastructure companies (XYL, WTS, FBIN) benefit. The divergence signals that water efficiency investment must accelerate to prevent shortages, driving orders for treatment equipment and smart water infrastructure.
causal_chain: USGS water use data shows per-capita withdrawals declining but total demand rising
Data center cooling, industrial, and population growth driving demand against conservation
Municipalities accelerate water infrastructure investment to prevent shortages
Water treatment equipment orders (XYL, WTS, FBIN) increase as infrastructure spending rises
Water infrastructure stocks rally 8-12% as capex pipeline builds
source_reference: USGS Water Use in the United States; EPA Drinking Water Infrastructure Needs Survey

name: FRED NAPMS ISM Prices Paid Collapse Below 40 → Long Industrial Buyers (MMM, HON, GE)
thesis: When ISM Prices Paid (FRED NAPMPRI) drops below 40, it signals massive input cost deflation for manufacturers. Industrial conglomerates (MMM, HON, GE) with diversified cost structures see gross margin expansion of 200-300bps in the following quarter. The ISM Prices Paid <40 reading has occurred only 5-6 times in 30 years, and industrial stocks rally 10-15% each time.
causal_chain: FRED NAPMPRI (ISM Prices Paid) drops below 40 — severe input cost deflation
Raw material, component, and energy costs declining sharply for manufacturers
Industrial conglomerates (MMM, HON, GE) have sticky pricing — revenues stable while COGS drops
Gross margin expansion of 200-300bps visible in following quarter earnings
Industrial stocks rally 10-15% as margin expansion drives earnings beats
source_reference: ISM Report on Business; IHS Markit manufacturing cost indices

name: FRED DEXKOUS Korean Won Strengthening → Long Korean Tech/Semi (EWY, Samsung Proxy)
thesis: When the Korean Won strengthens vs USD (FRED DEXKOUS declining) by >5% over 3 months, it signals global risk-on and semiconductor cycle recovery (Korea is export-driven, 20% of exports are semiconductors). EWY (iShares MSCI Korea) rallies 10-15% as won strength reflects both EM inflows and semiconductor demand recovery.
causal_chain: FRED DEXKOUS (USD/KRW) declines >5% over 3 months — won strengthening
Won strength signals global risk-on sentiment and Korean export demand improving
Korean semiconductor exports (20% of total exports) recovering — memory cycle turning
Foreign portfolio inflows to Korean equities as EM allocation increases
EWY rallies 10-15% as semiconductor cycle recovery + EM inflows compound
source_reference: Bank of Korea; SIA semiconductor monthly sales data

name: FRED DEXINUS Indian Rupee Stability → Long India Equity (INDA) as FII Confidence Signal
thesis: When the Indian Rupee (FRED DEXINUS, USD/INR) trades in a <2% range for 8+ weeks, it signals RBI intervention providing FX stability. This stability encourages Foreign Institutional Investor (FII) inflows into Indian equities because FX risk is temporarily suppressed. INDA rallies 5-10% during periods of rupee stability as FII allocation to India increases.
causal_chain: FRED DEXINUS (USD/INR) trades in <2% range for 8+ consecutive weeks
RBI FX intervention providing currency stability — reducing FII FX risk perception
Foreign Institutional Investors increase India equity allocation given low FX volatility
FII net buying drives Indian large-cap equity higher (Nifty 50 rally)
INDA rallies 5-10% during extended periods of rupee stability
source_reference: RBI monetary policy data; SEBI FPI/FII flow data (free monthly)

name: FRED DEXBZUS Brazilian Real Strengthening + Soybean Price Rising → Long EWZ Brazil Double Tailwind
thesis: When the Brazilian Real strengthens vs USD (FRED DEXBZUS declining) by >5% AND soybean prices (FRED PSOYBUSDM) are rising simultaneously, Brazil gets a double tailwind: commodity export revenue increases AND portfolio inflows accelerate. EWZ rallies 15-25% when both conditions align because Brazil's equity market is heavily weighted toward commodity exporters and banks that lend to them.
causal_chain: FRED DEXBZUS (USD/BRL) declines >5% in 3 months — Real strengthening
Simultaneously FRED soybean price rising >10% — commodity tailwind for Brazil
Double tailwind: stronger currency + higher commodity prices = fiscal improvement
Foreign portfolio inflows surge as Brazil becomes attractive on both fundamentals and FX
EWZ rallies 15-25% as commodity exporters, banks, and Real appreciation compound
source_reference: BCB monetary policy; USDA FAS Brazil soybean production data

name: NOAA Billion-Dollar Weather Disasters Count Surge → Long Reinsurers (RNR, ACGL, RE)
thesis: When NOAA's count of billion-dollar weather disasters (free annual tracker, but real-time via individual event tracking) exceeds historical average by >50% through the first half of the year, reinsurance stocks (RNR, ACGL, RE) rally. Counterintuitively, elevated cat losses trigger premium hardening in the Jan-1 renewal cycle — reinsurers raise prices 15-30%, driving earnings growth the following year.
causal_chain: NOAA billion-dollar weather disaster count exceeds first-half historical average by >50%
Elevated insured losses deplete reinsurer capital — but surviving reinsurers gain pricing power
Jan-1 treaty renewal cycle sees reinsurance premiums increase 15-30%
Reinsurers (RNR, ACGL, RE) that survive the cat year see massive premium growth next year
Reinsurance stocks rally 10-20% in Q4 as market anticipates Jan-1 premium increases
source_reference: NOAA NCEI Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters; Aon Annual Global Catastrophe Report

name: FRED DEXCAUS Canadian Dollar Strengthening + WTI Rising → Long Canadian Energy (XEG.TO proxy, CNQ, SU)
thesis: When the Canadian dollar strengthens (FRED DEXCAUS declining) by >3% while WTI rises >15% simultaneously, Canadian oil sands producers (CNQ, SU, IMO) get a triple tailwind: higher crude prices, narrower WCS-WTI differential, and CAD strength attracting foreign buyers. Canadian E&Ps outperform US XOP by 10-15% when both conditions align.
causal_chain: FRED DEXCAUS (USD/CAD) declines >3% AND WTI rises >15% simultaneously over 3 months
Canadian dollar strength reflects improving terms of trade from oil exports
WCS-WTI differential typically narrows during strong crude markets (pipeline not bottleneck)
CNQ, SU see triple tailwind: higher benchmark + narrower diff + FX-driven foreign buying

thesis: EIA publishes weekly refinery utilization rates (free). name: Container Ship Scrapping Rate Surge → Orderbook Thinning → Liner Shipping Rate Support
thesis: Clarksons and BIMCO publish container ship scrapping data (partially free via industry reports). name: Panama Canal Gatun Lake Level → Transit Restrictions → US East Coast LNG Rerouting Premium
thesis: Panama Canal Authority publishes daily Gatun Lake water levels and transit slot availability (free). When Gatun Lake drops below 80ft (historical drought threshold), ACP restricts daily transits from 36 to 18-22 and imposes draft limitations. LNG tankers from US Gulf to Asia must reroute via Suez (adding 15-20 days and $2-3M/voyage). Long US LNG exporters with East Coast terminals (CLNE concept, but specifically LNG Sabine Pass, GLNG) as rerouting premium widens US-Asia LNG spread.

ACP restricts daily transits to 18-22 and imposes maximum draft of 44ft (from 50ft)

Asian LNG (JKM) premium over US Henry Hub widens by $1-2/MMBtu to cover rerouting costs
US LNG exporters capture higher netback margins — LNG, GLNG stocks rally 8-15%

name: NERC Seasonal Reliability Assessment → Grid Emergency Risk → Battery Storage/Peaker IPP Long
thesis: NERC publishes seasonal reliability assessments (free, biannual) flagging regions at elevated risk of generation shortfall during peak summer/winter. When NERC identifies 'High Risk' areas (ERCOT, MISO, SPP) with reserve margins below 15%, grid stress events trigger emergency pricing ($5,000-9,000/MWh scarcity pricing). Long battery storage operators and gas peaker IPPs (VST Vistra, NRG, FSLR for solar+storage) positioned in NERC high-risk regions.
causal_chain: NERC Seasonal Reliability Assessment flags regions with reserve margin <15% as 'High Risk' (free at nerc.com)
Identified regions (ERCOT, MISO, SPP) face elevated probability of operating reserve emergencies during peak demand
Grid stress events trigger scarcity pricing — real-time prices spike to $5,000-9,000/MWh for hours/days
Battery storage operators dispatch stored energy at scarcity prices — capturing $2,000-5,000/MWh spread
Gas peaker IPPs (VST, NRG) with uncommitted capacity in high-risk regions earn windfall margins
VST, NRG rally 10-20% following grid stress events as market prices in elevated power revenue
data_sources: NERC Seasonal Reliability Assessment (free biannual at nerc.com)
source_reference: NERC seasonal assessments; ERCOT CDR reports; Brattle Group grid reliability studies

name: USDA APHIS Livestock Disease Outbreak → Veterinary Pharmaceutical Demand Surge → IDXX/ZTS Long
thesis: USDA APHIS publishes real-time disease situation reports for avian influenza, African swine fever, foot-and-mouth disease, and other OIE-notifiable diseases (free). When APHIS confirms a new high-consequence livestock disease outbreak (HPAI in commercial poultry, bovine TB cluster), veterinary diagnostic testing surges 50-100% and vaccination/treatment protocols activate. Long IDXX (veterinary diagnostics) and ZTS (animal health therapeutics) as testing volumes and prophylactic treatment demand spike.
causal_chain: USDA APHIS confirms high-consequence livestock disease outbreak (free at aphis.usda.gov/animal-health/status)
State veterinarians implement quarantine zones and mandatory surveillance testing
Veterinary diagnostic testing volumes surge 50-100% — IDXX instruments see elevated consumable pull-through
Prophylactic vaccination and treatment protocols activate in surrounding regions — ZTS benefits
Media coverage drives additional voluntary testing by producers outside quarantine zones
IDXX, ZTS rally 5-12% over 2-4 weeks as livestock health spending spikes
data_sources: USDA APHIS disease situation reports (free, real-time at aphis.usda.gov)
WOAH (OIE) disease notification system (free at wahis.woah.org)

name: Port of LA/LB Container Dwell Time Spike → Chassis Shortage → Intermodal Equipment Leasing Long
thesis: Port of Los Angeles publishes daily container dwell time statistics (free). When average import container dwell time exceeds 8 days (vs normal 3-4 days), it signals chassis and warehouse congestion. Intermodal equipment leasing companies (TGH Textainer merged into TL, but think Beacon Intermodal or Triton) see spot chassis lease rates spike 30-50%. Long R (Ryder) and GATX (railcar/container lessor) as equipment scarcity drives pricing power.
causal_chain: Port of LA Signal daily data shows import container dwell time exceeding 8 days (free at signal.portoflosangeles.org)
Extended dwell time means containers are sitting on chassis at warehouses — chassis not returning to port
Chassis pool availability drops below 30% — truckers cannot pick up new containers without chassis
Spot chassis lease rates spike 30-50% above contract rates
Equipment leasing companies (R, GATX) see utilization rates approach 100% with pricing power
Equipment lessors rally 5-10% as congestion-driven demand persists for 4-8 weeks
data_sources: Port of LA Signal — container dwell time (free daily at signal.portoflosangeles.org)
Port of Long Beach monthly container statistics (free)
source_reference: Port of LA Signal; IANA intermodal statistics; Journal of Commerce port congestion reports
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: Port of LA Signal dwell time data launched ~2021; insufficient history for meaningful backtest. TRTN went private in 2023 merger.

name: USDA Dairy Export Sales Surge to SE Asia → US Whey/NDM Price Recovery → Dairy Processor Long
thesis: USDA FAS publishes weekly dairy export sales (free). When US nonfat dry milk (NDM) and whey protein concentrate export sales to SE Asia (Philippines, Indonesia, Vietnam) surge >40% above 4-week average, it signals renewed Asian demand for US dairy ingredients at current prices. NDM and whey spot prices bottom and recover 10-20%. Long dairy processors (DAR, LNDC) and the dairy commodity complex.
causal_chain: USDA FAS weekly dairy export sales show NDM/whey exports to SE Asia surging >40% above 4wk avg (free)
SE Asian buyers (Philippines, Indonesia) increasing purchases signals price acceptance and restocking
US NDM and whey spot prices at CME stabilize and begin 10-20% recovery from cyclical trough
Dairy processors with ingredient exposure (DAR Darling Ingredients, LNDC) see margin improvement
Higher dairy ingredient prices flow through to quarterly earnings with 1-2 quarter lag
Dairy-levered stocks rally 8-15% as commodity price recovery becomes visible
data_sources: USDA FAS weekly dairy export sales (free at fas.usda.gov)
CME dairy commodity prices — NDM, whey (free delayed at cmegroup.com)
source_reference: USDA FAS dairy exports; USDEC dairy trade data; CME dairy markets
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: USDA FAS dairy-specific weekly export data requires commodity-level parsing; LNDC is small-cap with limited liquidity; DAR is not a pure dairy play

name: USGS NdPr Oxide Price Spike → EV Motor Cost Inflation → Ferrite Motor Alternative Suppliers Long
thesis: USGS tracks rare earth prices and China export volumes (free). When NdPr (neodymium-praseodymium) oxide prices spike >50% in 3 months due to China export restrictions, EV OEMs face motor cost inflation. This accelerates adoption of rare-earth-free motor designs (switched reluctance, ferrite magnet). Companies developing RE-free motors (Tesla internal, BorgWarner BWA, Nidec) gain competitive advantage. Long BWA as the leading supplier of next-gen ferrite/RE-free EV drive units.
causal_chain: USGS mineral data shows NdPr oxide prices spiking >50% in 3 months on China export controls (free at usgs.gov)
EV permanent magnet motor costs increase $200-500/vehicle — margin compression for OEMs
OEMs accelerate qualification of rare-earth-free motor designs (SRM, ferrite, wound-rotor)
BorgWarner (BWA) with ferrite magnet motor platform wins OEM contracts for RE-free drive units
BWA ePropulsion backlog grows as OEMs diversify away from NdFeB dependency
BWA re-rates 15-25% as rare-earth-free motor wins demonstrate technology competitive advantage
data_sources: USGS Mineral Commodity Summaries — rare earths (free)
China GACC rare earth export data (free monthly)
source_reference: USGS rare earth data; Adamas Intelligence rare earth market reports; BWA investor presentations
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: NdPr oxide pricing not freely machine-readable; rare earth price spikes are infrequent and driven by ad hoc China policy decisions not systematically predictable

name: DOE Clean Hydrogen Hub Award → Regional Industrial Cluster Activation → Electrolyzer/Fuel Cell Long
thesis: DOE publishes Regional Clean Hydrogen Hub (H2Hub) award announcements and milestone disbursements (free). Each hub receives $500M-1.2B in federal funding, triggering $3-5B in private co-investment over 5-7 years. When DOE announces milestone achievement (Phase 1 FEED completion, FID), it de-risks the project timeline. Long electrolyzer manufacturers (PLUG, BE, NEL) and industrial gas companies with hydrogen offtake (APD, LIN) as hub activation creates guaranteed demand.

Federal cost-share disbursement triggers private co-investment commitments from industrial partners
Hub participants issue RFPs for electrolyzer procurement — $100-500M equipment orders
Electrolyzer manufacturers (PLUG, BE) win supply contracts with federal cost-sharing backstop
Industrial gas companies (APD, LIN) secure long-term hydrogen offtake agreements at hub sites
Clean hydrogen stocks rally 15-30% as hub milestones demonstrate commercial traction

source_reference: DOE OCED; National Clean Hydrogen Strategy; H2Hub award announcements
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: DOE H2Hub awards are all post-2023; zero historical events for backtest. Electrolyzer stocks (PLUG, BE) have short trading histories and extreme volatility.

name: Census Business Formation Statistics (BFS) High-Propensity Applications Surge → Small-Cap Revenue Acceleration
thesis: Census Bureau publishes weekly Business Formation Statistics (BFS) tracking new EIN applications (free). The 'high-propensity' subset (applications likely to become employer businesses with payroll) is the most signal-rich. When high-propensity applications exceed the 80th percentile of the 5-year range for 4+ consecutive weeks, it signals accelerating entrepreneurial activity. New businesses generate demand for small-business infrastructure: payment processing (TOST, SQ), payroll (PAYX), insurance (HRTG), and commercial banking (community banks). Long IWM or specific small-business enablers.
causal_chain: Census BFS shows high-propensity business applications at 80th percentile for 4+ weeks (free weekly at census.gov/econ/bfs)
High-propensity applications correlate 0.7+ with actual employer business formation 6-12 months later
New employer businesses need payment processing, payroll, insurance, and commercial banking services
Small-business enablers (TOST, SQ, PAYX, HRTG) see new customer acquisition rates accelerate
Community banks (CVBF, UMPQ, PACW) see commercial account growth and SBA lending demand
Small-cap enabler stocks rally 10-20% as quarterly metrics confirm new business formation wave
data_sources: Census Bureau Business Formation Statistics — high propensity (free weekly at census.gov/econ/bfs)
BLS BED (Business Employment Dynamics) for validation (free quarterly)

source_reference: Census BFS; Kauffman Foundation entrepreneurship reports; NBER business formation research

name: USACE Inland Waterway Tonnage Surge → Barge Operator Revenue Beat → KIRBY Long
thesis: USACE Waterborne Commerce Statistics Center publishes monthly inland waterway tonnage data (free). When total inland waterway tonnage grows >5% YoY (driven by grain, petrochemical, and coal movements on the Mississippi/Ohio/Illinois systems), barge operators see utilization rates exceed 90%. Kirby Corp (KEX), the dominant inland tank barge operator, benefits from both volume growth and spot rate increases. Long KEX when USACE tonnage data inflects higher.
causal_chain: USACE Waterborne Commerce Statistics show inland waterway tonnage growing >5% YoY (free at iwr.usace.army.mil)
Grain export season + petrochemical shipping demand drives barge utilization above 90%
Spot barge rates rise 15-25% above contract rates as available capacity tightens
Kirby Corp (KEX) as the dominant inland tank barge operator captures both volume and pricing uplift
KEX inland marine segment margins expand 300-500bps on operating leverage
KEX rallies 10-15% over 1-2 months as quarterly earnings beat consensus

USDA grain transportation report — barge rates (free weekly)

name: USASpending DOD Contract Obligations Acceleration → Defense Subcontractor Revenue Pipeline
thesis: USASpending.gov publishes real-time federal contract obligation data (free). When DOD contract obligations in a specific NAICS code (e.g., 336411 Aircraft Manufacturing, 334511 Radar) accelerate >20% YoY in a quarter, it signals new program ramp-ups. The prime contractors are well-followed, but Tier 2/3 subcontractors often fly under the radar. Long specialized defense subcontractors (KTOS, MRCY, SWBI, CW) when USASpending shows their prime customers' contract obligations surging.
causal_chain: USASpending.gov shows DOD contract obligations in specific NAICS codes accelerating >20% YoY (free, real-time)
New program awards and existing program ramp-ups drive prime contractor production increases
Prime contractors flow down 40-60% of contract value to Tier 2/3 subcontractors
Specialized defense subcontractors (KTOS drones, MRCY defense electronics, AXON sensors) see order inflows
Subcontractor backlog-to-revenue ratios improve — providing 12-24 months of revenue visibility
Defense subcontractors rally 15-30% as quarterly bookings beats confirm pipeline strength

FPDS (Federal Procurement Data System) for detailed contract data (free)

name: ITC Solar Panel AD/CVD Determination → Domestic Module Price Floor → FSLR/MAXN Long
thesis: USITC publishes antidumping/countervailing duty (AD/CVD) preliminary and final determinations for crystalline silicon photovoltaic cells/modules (free). When USITC issues an affirmative injury determination with combined AD+CVD duties >30%, it creates a price floor for domestic solar module manufacturers. First Solar (FSLR, US cadmium telluride) and Maxeon (MAXN, US IBC panels) benefit from tariff protection that makes their US-manufactured panels price-competitive with Chinese imports.
causal_chain: USITC issues affirmative AD/CVD determination with combined duties >30% on crystalline silicon panels (free at usitc.gov)
Chinese and SE Asian solar module imports face effective tariff of 30-50% — landed costs rise $0.05-0.10/W
US-manufactured panels become price-competitive at current ASPs — no need for further price cuts
FSLR US fab utilization rises to 95%+ as developers switch procurement to tariff-exempt US modules
FSLR gross margins expand 300-500bps on volume growth with stable ASPs
FSLR rallies 15-25% as tariff protection secures domestic manufacturing margin
data_sources: USITC AD/CVD case determinations (free at usitc.gov)
Commerce Department preliminary/final AD/CVD margins (free at trade.gov/enforcement)
source_reference: USITC; Commerce Department AD/CVD; Solar Energy Industries Association trade policy reports

name: MBA Mortgage Forbearance Exit Rate Spike → Loan Modification Wave → Mortgage Servicer Revenue
thesis: MBA publishes monthly forbearance and call volume survey (free). When the forbearance exit rate spikes (borrowers leaving forbearance into modification, repayment plans, or reinstatement), mortgage servicers earn modification fees ($1,000-3,000 per loan) and retain servicing income on performing modified loans. Long mortgage servicers (PFSI, COOP, OCN) when MBA data shows forbearance exits accelerating — it signals revenue from loan workout fees plus improved credit quality on modified portfolio.
causal_chain: MBA monthly forbearance survey shows forbearance exit rate accelerating — borrowers leaving forbearance (free at mba.org)
Exiting borrowers receive loan modifications, repayment plans, or partial claims from servicers
Servicers earn modification processing fees of $1,000-3,000 per loan from GSE/Ginnie Mae
Modified loans return to performing status — reduces delinquency rates and advances outstanding
Improved portfolio performance reduces servicing expense (no more advances on delinquent loans)
PFSI, COOP rally 10-20% as forbearance unwind drives both fee income and portfolio improvement

source_reference: MBA forbearance data; FHFA; Urban Institute housing finance analysis

name: NOAA ONI El Nino Transition → West Africa Cocoa Drought → Cocoa Futures Long / HSY Short
thesis: NOAA CPC Oceanic Nino Index (ONI) tracks ENSO state (free). Strong El Nino events (ONI >+1.5) correlate with below-normal rainfall in West Africa (Ivory Coast, Ghana = 60% of global cocoa supply) during the Oct-Mar main crop season. Cocoa futures rally 30-80% during strong El Nino years as Harmattan winds desiccate pods and black pod disease spreads. Long CC=F cocoa futures or short HSY/MDLZ (chocolate manufacturers facing input cost inflation) when ONI crosses +1.5.
causal_chain: NOAA CPC ONI crosses +1.5 signaling strong El Nino (free monthly at cpc.ncep.noaa.gov)
El Nino teleconnection pattern brings below-normal rainfall to West Africa cocoa belt during main crop
Harmattan dry winds extend into growing season — cocoa pod development impaired
Black pod disease (Phytophthora) spreads in stressed trees — crop losses of 10-25%
Cocoa futures (CC=F) rally 30-80% over 3-6 months as supply deficit materializes
Chocolate manufacturers (HSY, MDLZ) face cocoa cost inflation — gross margins compress 200-400bps
data_sources: NOAA CPC Oceanic Nino Index (free monthly)
ICCO monthly cocoa market reports (partially free)
source_reference: NOAA CPC ONI; ICCO; IRI ENSO-cocoa correlation studies

name: BLS ECI Benefits Cost Deceleration → Corporate Margin Expansion → SPY Earnings Uplift
thesis: BLS Employment Cost Index (ECI) separates wage and benefits cost trends (free quarterly). When the benefits component of ECI decelerates >100bps YoY while wages hold steady, it signals corporate labor cost relief without consumer income destruction. Healthcare benefit costs (largest benefits component) declining means employer health insurance cost inflation is easing. Long SPY/QQQ as declining benefits cost growth expands S&P 500 operating margins.
causal_chain: BLS ECI quarterly report shows benefits cost growth decelerating >100bps YoY (free at bls.gov)
Healthcare benefits cost inflation easing — employer health insurance premiums growing slower
Total compensation cost growth moderates without wage cuts — workers' spending power maintained
S&P 500 companies see 50-100bps operating margin expansion from lower benefits cost inflation
Earnings estimates revised upward 2-3% as labor cost relief flows through income statements
SPY rallies 3-5% over 1-2 months as margin expansion narrative strengthens
data_sources: BLS Employment Cost Index — benefits component (free quarterly, also on FRED ECIBEN)
BLS Employer Costs for Employee Compensation (free quarterly)
source_reference: BLS ECI; Kaiser Family Foundation employer health benefits survey; Goldman Sachs labor cost model

name: EIA Natural Gas Industrial Consumption Dip → Ammonia Plant Turnaround → Nitrogen Fertilizer Spot Price Spike
thesis: EIA publishes monthly natural gas consumption by sector (free). A sharp drop in industrial gas consumption during spring (March-May) signals simultaneous ammonia plant turnarounds. US ammonia production capacity drops 20-30% for 4-6 weeks during synchronized turnaround season. With US ammonia production offline, nitrogen fertilizer spot prices (urea, UAN) spike 15-30% as spring application season creates demand. Long CF Industries (CF) and LSB Industries (LXU) when EIA industrial gas data signals turnaround-driven supply squeeze.
causal_chain: EIA monthly natural gas consumption data shows industrial sector dip in March-May (free at eia.gov)
Industrial gas consumption drop correlates with ammonia plant turnaround season
US ammonia production capacity drops 20-30% for 4-6 weeks during turnarounds
Spring planting season creates peak nitrogen fertilizer demand simultaneously with supply reduction
Urea and UAN spot prices spike 15-30% as supply-demand gap tightens
CF, LXU benefit from pre-turnaround inventory sales at elevated spot prices; margins expand
data_sources: EIA monthly natural gas consumption by sector (free at eia.gov)
USDA AMS fertilizer prices (free weekly at ams.usda.gov)
source_reference: EIA natural gas data; USDA fertilizer price reports; CF Industries turnaround schedule disclosures

name: FAA ATADS Airport Operations Record → Airport Concession Revenue Acceleration → Airport Operator/F&B Long
thesis: FAA Air Traffic Activity Data System (ATADS) publishes monthly airport operations counts (free). When total operations at the top 30 airports exceed prior-year records by >5% for 3+ consecutive months, airport concession revenue (F&B, retail, parking) surges with high margins. causal_chain: FAA ATADS data shows top-30 airport operations exceeding prior-year records by >5% for 3+ months (free at faa.gov/air_traffic/publications)
Record passenger throughput drives proportional increase in concession revenue (F&B, retail, parking)
Airport concession revenue per enplanement holds steady or rises — total revenue accelerates with volume
Concession operators see 30-40% EBITDA flow-through on incremental revenue above fixed rent obligations
Airport-adjacent hotels see 85%+ occupancy with ADR pricing power from constrained supply
Airport-levered stocks rally 5-10% as quarterly results reflect record traffic contribution

name: USDA Organic Integrity Database → Certified Acreage Stagnation → Organic Premium Widening → HAIN/SFM Long
thesis: USDA Organic Integrity Database tracks all certified organic operations (free). When certified organic crop acreage growth stalls (<2% YoY) while organic food demand grows 8-10% YoY (per Organic Trade Association), organic price premiums widen. Organic-focused brands (HAIN, Sprouts SFM) benefit from widening organic-conventional spread as their supply chain locks in long-term organic contracts. Long HAIN and SFM when organic acreage stagnation signals structural supply constraint.

Three-year organic transition requirement creates structural lag between demand growth and supply response
Organic food demand growing 8-10% YoY while supply grows <2% — organic price premium widens

Organic Trade Association industry statistics (partially free)

name: Treasury TIC Data → Japan/China Net UST Selling → Long-End Rate Pressure → Long TBT
thesis: Treasury International Capital (TIC) data shows foreign official holdings of US Treasuries (free, monthly with 2-month lag). When Japan and China combined reduce UST holdings by >$50B in a single month (or >$100B cumulative over 3 months), it signals structural foreign demand withdrawal. Long-end Treasury yields rise 20-40bps as the marginal buyer disappears. Long TBT (2x short 20yr) or short TLT as foreign official selling pressures the long end.
causal_chain: Treasury TIC data shows Japan + China reducing UST holdings by >$50B in one month (free at treasury.gov/tic)
Foreign official sector withdrawing from US Treasuries — reduced demand for long-duration USTs
Term premium rises as marginal buyer (foreign CBs) steps back — 20-30yr yields rise 20-40bps
Long-end yield rise pressures duration-sensitive assets (utilities, REITs, growth stocks)
TLT declines 3-5% as 20yr+ yields back up; TBT (inverse) rallies proportionally
Rate-sensitive sectors underperform for 1-3 months until new equilibrium found
data_sources: Treasury TIC major foreign holders of UST (free monthly at treasury.gov/tic)
Fed H.4.1 for foreign official custody holdings (free weekly)
source_reference: Treasury TIC; Setser/Council on Foreign Relations UST flow analysis; BIS reserves data
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=4 but SHORT-SIDE: TBT is an inverse Treasury ETF, making this effectively a short-bond position. Pipeline is LONG-SIDE ONLY.

name: NOAA SNOTEL April 1 SWE Record → Western Reservoir Refill → Hydropower Output Surge → Utility Cost Reduction
thesis: NOAA/NRCS SNOTEL network publishes daily snow water equivalent (SWE) at 800+ mountain sites across the West (free). April 1 SWE is the canonical metric for summer water supply. When April 1 SWE exceeds 130% of median across the Sierra Nevada and Cascades, it signals exceptional reservoir refill and hydropower generation capacity. Utilities with significant hydro portfolios (BEP, AES with Pacific hydro, PNW utilities like POR Portland General) see lower generation costs and higher clean energy margins. Long POR and hydro-heavy utilities when April 1 SWE signals abundant water year.
causal_chain: NRCS SNOTEL April 1 SWE exceeds 130% of median in Sierra Nevada/Cascades (free at wcc.nrcs.usda.gov)
Record snowpack guarantees above-normal reservoir inflows through June-August snowmelt season
Western reservoirs (Shasta, Oroville, Grand Coulee) fill to 100%+ capacity — hydropower heads maximize
Hydropower output exceeds normal by 30-50% — displaces expensive gas-fired generation
Utilities with significant hydro portfolios (POR, AES) see 200-400bps fuel cost reduction
Hydro-heavy utilities report improved margins; stocks re-rate 5-10% on lower generation costs
data_sources: NRCS SNOTEL SWE data (free at wcc.nrcs.usda.gov)
NOAA California Nevada River Forecast Center (free)
source_reference: NRCS SNOTEL; NOAA River Forecast Centers; EIA hydropower data
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: SNOTEL April 1 SWE data is annual; hydropower output to utility cost mapping requires regional analysis not automatable

name: IRS SOI Estate Tax Filing Surge → Wealth Transfer Wave → RIA/Trust Company Acquirer Long
thesis: IRS Statistics of Income (SOI) publishes estate tax filing data annually (free). The 2025 estate tax exemption sunset (reverting from $13.6M to ~$7M per person under TCJA expiration) was expected to trigger a massive wave of estate planning and wealth transfers. IRS SOI data showing >30% increase in gift tax returns filed signals an ongoing wealth transfer wave. Long RIA aggregators (LPLA, RJF) and trust companies (STT, BK, NTRS) as wealth transfer events generate advisory fees, trust administration revenue, and new AUM.

Estate tax exemption sunset fear drives accelerated wealth transfers before threshold reduction
Wealth transfers generate advisory fees for RIAs ($5,000-50,000 per estate plan restructuring)
Trust companies (NTRS, BK, STT) see new trust establishment and AUM growth from irrevocable trusts
RIA aggregators (LPLA, RJF) benefit as advisors capture recurring fees on transferred assets
Wealth management stocks rally 10-15% as fee income acceleration becomes visible in quarterly results
data_sources: IRS SOI estate and gift tax statistics (free at irs.gov/statistics)
Census wealth and asset ownership data (free)

name: Cruise Ship Orderbook Delivery Cluster → Berth Capacity Growth → Caribbean Port Revenue Long
thesis: Cruise Industry News publishes orderbook data showing newbuild delivery schedules (partially free). When 10+ new cruise ships (200K+ gross tonnage total) deliver within a 12-month window, it adds 25,000-40,000 berths of capacity. This drives Caribbean/Alaska port call revenue and shore excursion volume for port operators. Long RCL and CCL (operators benefiting from fleet growth) and cruise port-adjacent real estate when orderbook deliveries cluster.
causal_chain: Cruise industry orderbook shows 10+ new ships delivering within 12 months (partially free at cruiseindustrynews.com)
New ships add 25,000-40,000 berths — deployed on Caribbean/Alaska/Mediterranean itineraries
Port call volumes at Caribbean destinations increase proportionally — port revenue per call stays stable
Shore excursion and onboard spending increase with younger/wealthier demographic on new ships
RCL, CCL see capacity-driven revenue growth of 15-25% without diluting yield (pricing holds)
Cruise operators rally 10-20% as fleet expansion drives revenue scale without margin compression

reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: Cruise ship orderbook data (Clarksons) is paywalled; Caribbean port revenue data not freely available

name: EPA PFAS MCL Final Rule → Municipal Water Treatment Capex Mandate → Water Treatment Equipment Long
thesis: EPA publishes final drinking water Maximum Contaminant Levels (MCLs) for PFAS compounds (free at epa.gov). The 2024 final PFAS MCL of 4 ppt for PFOA/PFOS requires ~10,000 US water systems to install granular activated carbon (GAC) or ion exchange treatment. This represents $30-50B in mandatory compliance capex over 5 years. Long water treatment equipment manufacturers (XYL Xylem, WTRG Essential Utilities, WMS Advanced Drainage) positioned to capture PFAS remediation spending.
causal_chain: EPA PFAS MCL final rule sets 4 ppt limit for PFOA/PFOS (free at epa.gov/pfas)
~10,000 US public water systems exceed new MCL — must install treatment within 3-5 year compliance deadline
Municipal water utilities must invest $30-50B in GAC, ion exchange, or reverse osmosis treatment
Water treatment equipment manufacturers (XYL, WMS, AQUA) see multi-year order pipeline for PFAS treatment
EPA State Revolving Fund and IIJA provide $10B+ in federal assistance — de-risks utility spending
Water treatment stocks rally 15-30% over 12-24 months as compliance spending materializes in orders
data_sources: EPA PFAS regulations and MCL (free at epa.gov/pfas)
EPA UCMR5 (Unregulated Contaminant Monitoring Rule) occurrence data (free)
source_reference: EPA PFAS final rule; AWWA compliance cost estimates; EPA UCMR5 data
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: EPA PFAS MCL final rule is a one-time regulatory event (2024); no historical precedent for backtest. Water treatment equipment stocks (XYL, ECL) are diversified with PFAS revenue being small fraction.

name: DOL WARN Act Filings Geographic Cluster → Regional Consumer Spending Collapse → Regional Retailer Short
thesis: State labor departments publish WARN Act layoff notices 60 days before mass layoffs (free, state-by-state). When WARN filings cluster geographically — multiple large employers announcing layoffs in the same metro within 30 days — it signals an imminent local consumer spending contraction. Regional retailers and restaurants with concentration in affected metros face revenue headwinds. Reef-dependent tourism stocks (hotel REITs with Hawaii/Caribbean exposure) underperform by 5-10%
data_sources: NOAA Coral Reef Watch — bleaching alerts and SST monitoring (free daily at coralreefwatch.noaa.gov)
source_reference: NOAA Coral Reef Watch; ICRI global coral bleaching reports; Hawaii Tourism Authority data
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3 and SHORT-SIDE: NOAA coral bleaching alerts are infrequent; the thesis proposes shorting Hawaiian/Caribbean hotels which violates long-only constraint

name: CISA KEV Catalog Entry Surge → Cybersecurity Spending Urgency → CRWD/PANW/FTNT Long
thesis: CISA publishes the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog (free, updated continuously). Federal agencies must remediate KEV entries within defined deadlines. When CISA adds >20 new KEV entries in a single month (vs avg 8-10/month), it signals a wave of actively exploited vulnerabilities requiring emergency patching and security tool purchases. Federal cyber spending surges; private sector follows. Long cybersecurity leaders (CRWD, PANW, FTNT) when KEV additions spike.
causal_chain: CISA KEV catalog receives >20 new entries in one month — active exploitation confirmed (free at cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog)
BOD 22-01 mandates federal agencies remediate within 2-3 weeks — emergency IT spending authorized
Federal cybersecurity vendors (CRWD, PANW) see accelerated procurement through BPA/GWAC vehicles
Private sector CISOs use KEV surge as justification for emergency security tool purchases
Cybersecurity companies report bookings acceleration and deal cycle compression
CRWD, PANW rally 5-12% as quarterly results reflect urgency-driven demand
data_sources: CISA KEV catalog (free JSON/CSV at cisa.gov)
NVD (National Vulnerability Database) for severity context (free at nvd.nist.gov)
source_reference: CISA KEV; BOD 22-01; Gartner cybersecurity spending forecasts
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: CISA KEV catalog entries are sporadic; cybersecurity spending decisions are not directly driven by KEV entries; CRWD/PANW/FTNT are already well-covered by analysts

name: USDA Weekly Rice Milling Data → US Rice Surplus → Long Asian Rice Importers / Short US Rice Futures
thesis: USDA AMS publishes weekly rice milling and stocks data (free). When US rice millings exceed shipments for 4+ consecutive weeks (building milled rice inventory), it signals a domestic surplus developing. Combined with USDA WASDE showing higher ending stocks, rough rice futures (ZR=F) face downward pressure. Long importers benefiting from cheap rice (processed food companies) and short rough rice futures at seasonal tops.
causal_chain: USDA AMS weekly rice milling data shows millings > shipments for 4+ weeks — inventory building (free at ams.usda.gov)
Milled rice inventory accumulation signals weak domestic and export demand vs supply
USDA WASDE confirms higher US rice ending stocks — bearish supply-demand balance
Rough rice futures (ZR=F) decline 5-15% as surplus weighs on prices
US rice exporters compete aggressively on price — benefits Asian importers and food processors
Processed food companies using rice as input (LNDC, CPB) see ingredient cost reduction
data_sources: USDA AMS weekly rice milling and stocks (free at ams.usda.gov)
USDA WASDE rice balance sheet (free monthly)
source_reference: USDA AMS rice data; USA Rice Federation; USDA WASDE
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3 and SHORT-SIDE: proposal includes shorting US rice futures; USDA weekly rice milling data is niche with limited stock proxies

name: BTS Airline On-Time Performance Deterioration → MRO Provider Demand Surge → TDG/HEI Long
thesis: DOT Bureau of Transportation Statistics (BTS) publishes monthly airline on-time performance with mechanical cancellation/delay breakdown (free). When mechanical delays/cancellations rise >30% YoY across multiple carriers, it signals aging fleet maintenance needs outpacing MRO capacity. Airlines must increase AOG (Aircraft on Ground) parts purchases at premium prices. Long aerospace aftermarket parts companies (TDG TransDigm, HEI HEICO) as AOG premium pricing drives margin expansion.
causal_chain: BTS airline on-time data shows mechanical delays/cancellations rising >30% YoY (free at transtats.bts.gov)
Rising mechanical issues signal fleet maintenance backlog from pandemic-era deferred maintenance
Airlines increase AOG parts purchases at 200-500% premium over planned maintenance pricing
TransDigm (TDG) and HEICO (HEI) sole-source aftermarket parts see pricing power increase
Aftermarket parts revenue mix shifts toward high-margin AOG/expedited orders
TDG, HEI margins expand 200-400bps; stocks rally 8-15% on earnings beats
data_sources: BTS airline on-time performance and cause of delay (free at transtats.bts.gov/ot_delay/OT_DelayCause1.asp)
source_reference: BTS on-time data; FAA; TransDigm/HEICO aftermarket pricing model disclosures

name: EIA Underground Natural Gas Storage Fill Rate Below Avg → Winter Premium → Long UNG/BOIL Before Injection Season End
thesis: EIA publishes weekly natural gas storage reports (free). The injection season (April-October) must refill storage to ~3.7+ Tcf to meet winter heating demand. When storage fill rate (weekly injections) runs >10% below the 5-year average pace through August, the market recognizes that achieving adequate winter preparedness is at risk. Henry Hub futures rally 20-40% between August and November as winter premium builds. Long UNG or BOIL when mid-injection-season storage deficit exceeds 200 Bcf below 5yr avg.
causal_chain: EIA weekly storage report shows cumulative injections running >10% below 5yr avg pace through August (free at eia.gov/naturalgas/storage)
Storage deficit of >200 Bcf below 5yr avg signals risk of inadequate winter preparedness
Market begins pricing winter supply risk — nearby/deferred spread shifts to backwardation
Speculative buying and utility hedging demand push Henry Hub futures up 20-40% over Aug-Nov
LNG export pull and power generation demand compete for available supply
Natural gas equities (AR, EQT, CTRA) rally with commodity price; UNG/BOIL offer leveraged exposure
data_sources: EIA weekly natural gas storage report (free at eia.gov/naturalgas/storage)
EIA natural gas spot and futures prices (free)
yfinance UNG, BOIL, AR, EQT, CTRA
source_reference: EIA natural gas storage; NOAA winter heating degree day forecasts

name: Census ACS County Migration Inflows → High-Growth Metro Real Estate → Sunbelt Builder/Land Developer Long
thesis: Census American Community Survey (ACS) and IRS SOI migration data track county-to-county population flows (free, annual with 1-year lag). When net domestic migration inflows to a metro exceed 3% of population for 2+ consecutive years, housing demand structurally exceeds existing supply. Homebuilders with large lot positions in high-inflow metros (DHI in Dallas/Phoenix, NVR in Southeastern metros) benefit from sustained demand and pricing power. Long DHI/NVR when Census migration data confirms multi-year inflow acceleration to their core markets.
causal_chain: Census ACS/IRS SOI migration data shows net inflows >3% of population for 2+ years in specific metros (free at census.gov/topics/population/migration)
Sustained population inflow creates structural housing demand exceeding existing inventory
Homebuilders with land banks in high-inflow metros see absorption rates accelerate
Strong demand enables pricing power — new home ASPs rise 3-5% above material cost inflation
Community count growth + pricing power = 15-20% revenue growth for positioned builders
DHI, NVR re-rate as sustained inflow metros demonstrate multi-year housing demand runway
data_sources: Census ACS migration flow data (free at census.gov/topics/population/migration)
IRS SOI county migration data (free at irs.gov/statistics/soi-tax-stats-migration-data)
source_reference: Census ACS; IRS SOI migration; Joint Center for Housing Studies
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: Census ACS county migration data is annual with 1-2 year lag; sunbelt builder stocks already well-covered by analysts tracking permits/starts data

name: BLS PPI Semiconductor Manufacturing Trough → Chip Price Recovery Inflection → Semiconductor Distributor Long
thesis: BLS PPI for semiconductor and related device manufacturing (NAICS 3344) is published monthly (free). When semiconductor PPI declines >5% YoY and then shows first month of sequential MoM increase after a 6+ month decline, it signals chip price trough. Semiconductor distributors (ARW Arrow, AVT Avnet) see gross margin improvement as prices stabilize on existing inventory purchased at deflated costs. Long ARW/AVT at the PPI inflection point — distributor margins expand 200-400bps as selling prices recover against low-cost inventory.
causal_chain: BLS PPI semiconductor manufacturing shows first MoM increase after 6+ month YoY decline (free at bls.gov/ppi)
Price trough signals end of inventory destocking cycle — customers resume ordering at stabilized prices
Distributors (ARW, AVT) sell inventory purchased at trough prices at recovering ASPs — gross margin expands
Order backlog rebuilds as channel partners shift from just-in-time to safety stock rebuilding
Distributor revenue growth inflects positive within 1-2 quarters of PPI trough
ARW, AVT rally 15-25% as margin expansion and revenue recovery drive earnings beats
data_sources: BLS PPI semiconductor and related device manufacturing — NAICS 3344 (free at bls.gov/ppi)
SIA monthly global semiconductor sales (partially free at semiconductors.org)
source_reference: BLS PPI; SIA; WSTS semiconductor market forecasts

name: EPA Emergency RVP Waiver → Expanded Gasoline Blending Pool → Ethanol Demand Spike → GPRE Long
thesis: EPA can issue emergency fuel waivers allowing higher Reid Vapor Pressure (RVP) gasoline blends when supply is tight (free announcements at epa.gov). When EPA issues summer RVP waivers, it expands the gasoline blending pool to include E15 (15% ethanol) year-round in affected states. This creates a sudden 50-100M gallon/month incremental ethanol demand. Long ethanol producers (GPRE Green Plains, REX) when EPA issues RVP waivers during summer driving season supply crunches.
causal_chain: EPA issues emergency RVP waiver allowing E15 sales during summer months in additional states (free at epa.gov/fuels)
E15 year-round availability expands ethanol blending pool by 50-100M gallons/month
Incremental ethanol demand tightens supply — ethanol spot prices rise 10-20 cents/gallon
Ethanol producers (GPRE, REX) see immediate margin improvement on higher selling prices
Corn crush spread widens by $0.10-0.20/gallon — operating margins expand significantly
Ethanol producer stocks rally 5-12% in the 2-3 weeks following RVP waiver announcement
data_sources: EPA fuel waivers and RVP regulations (free at epa.gov/fuels)
EIA weekly ethanol production and stocks (free)
source_reference: EPA fuel waivers; RFA ethanol market analysis; EIA ethanol data
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: EPA emergency RVP waivers are ad hoc events (maybe 1-2/decade); too few for backtest. GPRE is small-cap with volatile earnings.

name: Treasury Quarterly Refunding → Coupon Issuance Shift to Bills → Money Market Fund AUM Growth → BEN/TROW Long
thesis: Treasury publishes quarterly refunding announcements detailing coupon vs bill issuance plans (free). When Treasury shifts issuance mix toward T-bills (shorter duration), it expands the supply of high-quality money market instruments. Money market fund AUM grows as yield-seeking investors allocate to bills. Asset managers with large money market platforms (BEN/Franklin, TROW, BLK, STT) earn management fees on growing MMF AUM. Long BEN/STT when Treasury refunding shifts to bills-heavy issuance mix.
causal_chain: Treasury quarterly refunding announces bills-heavy issuance mix (free at treasury.gov/resource-center/data-chart-center)
Expanded T-bill supply keeps money market yields attractive relative to bank deposits
Money market fund AUM grows as retail and institutional cash flows into MMFs
Asset managers with MMF platforms (BEN, STT, BLK) earn 10-15bps management fees on growing AUM
Every $100B in MMF AUM growth generates ~$100-150M in annual fee revenue across industry
MMF-heavy asset managers see fee revenue acceleration — stocks rally 3-8% on AUM growth
data_sources: Treasury quarterly refunding statements (free at treasury.gov)
ICI weekly money market fund AUM (free at ici.org)
source_reference: Treasury refunding; ICI money market statistics; Office of Financial Research MMF data

name: USDA Catfish Processing Roundweight Decline → Domestic Supply Shortage → Import Surge → Seafood Distributor Long
thesis: USDA NASS publishes monthly catfish processing data (free). The US is the largest domestic producer of farmed catfish, but production has been in structural decline. When USDA catfish roundweight processed declines >10% YoY for 3+ consecutive months, domestic supply shortage drives import surge from Vietnam/Indonesia (pangasius). Seafood distributors and importers benefit from higher volumes and pricing power. Long USFD (US Foods) and SYY (Sysco) with significant seafood distribution as catfish supply shift drives margin opportunities.
causal_chain: USDA NASS monthly catfish processing data shows roundweight declining >10% YoY (free at nass.usda.gov)
Mississippi Delta catfish farms exit production due to feed costs and labor — domestic supply contracts
Restaurant chains and food service must substitute imported pangasius or other whitefish
Seafood import volume increases — distributors (USFD, SYY) with import infrastructure capture spread
Higher-margin imported product replaces lower-margin domestic catfish in food service mix
Food service distributors see seafood category margin expansion of 50-100bps
data_sources: USDA NASS catfish processing (free monthly at nass.usda.gov)
NOAA fisheries imports data (free at fisheries.noaa.gov/foss)
source_reference: USDA NASS catfish data; Catfish Institute; NOAA fisheries trade data
reject_reason: bt_feasibility=3: USDA catfish processing data is very niche; no liquid publicly traded seafood distributor proxies

name: USPS Revenue-Per-Piece Data → Package Volume Recovery → Last-Mile Logistics Competitor Signal
thesis: USPS publishes quarterly revenue and volume data in its financial reports (free at about.usps.com). When USPS Shipping & Package revenue-per-piece grows >5% YoY while volume declines, it signals USPS raising parcel rates. This creates pricing umbrella for private last-mile carriers. UPS and FedEx can match USPS rate increases without losing share. Long UPS and FDX when USPS rate increases signal industry-wide parcel pricing power.
causal_chain: USPS quarterly financial report shows Shipping & Packages revenue/piece growing >5% YoY (free at about.usps.com/financials)
USPS rate increase creates pricing umbrella for the parcel shipping industry
UPS and FedEx match or exceed USPS rate increases on competitive parcel products
E-commerce shippers face higher rates across all carriers — cannot arbitrage between carriers
UPS, FDX revenue per piece grows 3-5% without volume loss — margin expansion follows
UPS, FDX rally 5-10% as industry pricing discipline drives earnings improvement
data_sources: USPS quarterly financial reports (free at about.usps.com)
USPS rate change announcements (free at pe.usps.com)
source_reference: USPS financial reports; PRC rate review proceedings; ShipMatrix parcel rate analysis

name: SBA 7(a) Loan Approval Volume Surge → Small Business Expansion Wave → Community Bank NII Growth
thesis: SBA publishes weekly/monthly 7(a) loan approval data (free). The 7(a) program is the primary federal guarantee program for small business lending. When 7(a) approvals surge >20% YoY for 3+ months, it signals small business confidence in expansion. SBA-preferred lenders (community and regional banks) earn origination fees plus ongoing interest income on guaranteed portions. Long community bank ETFs (KRE) or specific SBA-heavy lenders (LKFN, BANR, UBSI) when 7(a) approvals inflect.
causal_chain: SBA 7(a) loan approval volume surges >20% YoY for 3+ consecutive months (free at sba.gov/funding-programs/loans/data)
Small business borrowers securing SBA loans for expansion, equipment, and real estate
SBA-preferred lenders earn 2-3% origination fees plus 4-6% ongoing interest income
Guaranteed portion (75-85%) can be sold in secondary market — generates gain-on-sale income
Community banks (LKFN, BANR, UBSI) with active SBA lending see NII and fee income growth
SBA-active community banks outperform KRE by 3-5% as loan growth drives earnings acceleration
data_sources: SBA 7(a) loan approval data (free at sba.gov/funding-programs/loans/data)
source_reference: SBA lending data; FDIC community banking research; SBA preferred lender program

name: BLM Grazing Permit AUM Fee Increase → Ranching Cost Inflation → Beef Supply Contraction → Cattle Futures Long
thesis: Bureau of Land Management (BLM) publishes annual grazing fee per Animal Unit Month (AUM) and permit modifications (free). When BLM increases AUM fees >15% or reduces permitted AUMs due to drought/ESA requirements, it increases ranching costs on federal land (245M acres of grazing allotments). Marginal ranchers exit — accelerating beef cow herd liquidation. Live cattle futures (LE=F) rally as supply contraction tightens. Long LE=F or beef-levered equities when BLM grazing permit changes signal structural supply reduction.
causal_chain: BLM publishes grazing fee increase >15% or AUM reduction for major grazing allotments (free at blm.gov/programs/natural-resources/rangelands-and-grazing)
Higher grazing costs or reduced permit capacity squeezes marginal ranchers' profitability
Ranchers liquidate cow herds — USDA Cattle inventory report confirms herd decline
Smaller breeding herd = fewer calves = tighter beef supply 12-18 months forward
Live cattle futures (LE=F) rally 10-20% as forward supply scarcity gets priced in
Beef retail prices rise — restaurant chains face food cost inflation (DRI, MCD margin pressure)
data_sources: BLM grazing statistics and fee schedules (free at blm.gov)
USDA NASS Cattle Inventory report (free semiannual Jan/Jul)
source_reference: BLM grazing program data; USDA NASS Cattle Inventory; Public Lands Council

name: OSHA Industry Injury Rate Spike → Workers Comp Premium Hardening → Specialty Insurer Long
thesis: BLS/OSHA publishes the annual Survey of Occupational Injuries and Illnesses (SOII) by industry (free). When total recordable injury rates in a major industry (construction, transportation, warehousing) rise >10% YoY, workers' compensation insurance loss ratios deteriorate. NCCI files for rate increases 6-12 months later. Specialty workers' comp insurers (ERIE, HMN, Employers Holdings EIG) benefit from premium rate hardening on January policy renewals. Long EIG/ERIE when OSHA injury data signals coming rate cycle turn.
causal_chain: BLS SOII shows total recordable injury rate in major industry rising >10% YoY (free at bls.gov/iif)
Higher injury rates drive increased workers' comp claims frequency and severity
NCCI and state rating bureaus file loss cost increases of 5-15% for affected class codes
Workers' comp premium rates increase on January policy renewals — 6-12 month lag from data release
Specialty workers' comp insurers (EIG Employers Holdings, ERIE) earn higher premium per policy
Combined ratios improve as rate increases outpace loss trend — insurer stocks rally 10-15%
data_sources: BLS Survey of Occupational Injuries and Illnesses (free annual at bls.gov/iif)
OSHA establishment-level injury data (partially free at osha.gov/data)
source_reference: BLS SOII; NCCI rate filings; OSHA data initiative

name: FDA Biosimilar Approval Wave → PBM Formulary Switching → Biosimilar Manufacturer Revenue Acceleration
thesis: FDA publishes biosimilar approval decisions (free at fda.gov/drugs/biosimilars). When FDA approves 3+ interchangeable biosimilars for the same reference product within 12 months, PBMs (Express Scripts, CVS Caremark, Optum Rx) switch formulary preferred status to the lowest-cost biosimilar. This drives rapid market share shifts. Long biosimilar-focused manufacturers (TEVA, VTRS Viatris, AMGN biosimilar portfolio, SAN Sandoz) positioned with competitive pricing at formulary launches.

PBMs leverage competitive biosimilar market to negotiate 40-60% discounts vs reference product
PBMs switch formulary preferred status to lowest-cost interchangeable biosimilar
Patients auto-switched at pharmacy — biosimilar market share reaches 50-80% within 12 months
Biosimilar manufacturer with lowest WAC price and best rebate structure captures majority volume
Biosimilar-focused companies (TEVA, VTRS) see revenue acceleration as formulary wins drive volume
data_sources: FDA biosimilar approvals and interchangeability determinations (free at fda.gov)
CMS Part B and Part D drug spending data (free at cms.gov)
source_reference: FDA biosimilar data; IQVIA biosimilar market reports; CMS drug pricing data

name: Census E-Commerce Share Sequential Decline → Physical Retail Recovery → Mall REIT / Department Store Long
thesis: Census Bureau publishes quarterly e-commerce as a percentage of total retail sales (free). E-commerce share has been on a secular uptrend, but shows cyclical reversals. When e-commerce share declines for 2 consecutive quarters (consumers returning to physical stores), it signals foot traffic recovery for brick-and-mortar retail. Long mall REITs (SPG, MAC) and department stores (M, KSS) at the inflection — physical retail traffic recovery drives occupancy rates and same-store sales.
causal_chain: Census quarterly retail e-commerce report shows e-commerce share declining for 2 consecutive quarters (free at census.gov/retail)
Declining e-commerce share signals consumers shifting spending back to physical stores
In-store foot traffic increases — retail tenants report same-store sales acceleration
Mall REITs (SPG, MAC) see tenant sales per square foot recover — supports rent renewal negotiations
Occupancy rates rise and tenant defaults decline — NOI growth accelerates
Mall REITs rally 10-20% as physical retail recovery drives rental income growth
data_sources: Census Bureau quarterly e-commerce report (free at census.gov/retail)
yfinance SPG, MAC, M, KSS, TGT
source_reference: Census e-commerce data; ICSC retail traffic reports; Placer.ai foot traffic
